TC-101.description: Verify system behavior when calculating pick time for an order with zero items. Ensure the base pick time of 200 seconds is still applied.
TC-101.preconditions:
1. The system is configured with the default picking time of 200 seconds.
2. The system allows creation of orders with zero items (if applicable).
TC-101.title: Test pick time calculation with zero quantities

steps.step: Attempt to create an order with zero items.
steps.result: The system either creates the order with zero items or provides an appropriate error message if such orders are not allowed.

steps.step: If the order is created, trigger the Pure Pick Time (PPT) calculation for this order.
steps.result: The system initiates the PPT calculation process.

steps.step: Observe the calculated Pure Pick Time.
steps.result: If zero-item orders are allowed, the Pure Pick Time is still 200 seconds (the base pick time). If not allowed, the system should handle this edge case appropriately (e.g., by returning an error or a default value).

steps.step: Verify that no additional buffer time has been added to the base pick time.
steps.result: The system confirms that no extra buffer time was added, as there were no items to consider for additional buffers.

TC-102.description: Calculate pick time for an order with the minimum possible number of items (likely 1). Confirm that only the base pick time of 200 seconds is applied without any additional buffers.
TC-102.preconditions:
1. The system is configured with the default picking time of 200 seconds.
2. The system allows creation of orders with a minimum of 1 item.
TC-102.title: Check pick time for minimum possible order

steps.step: Create an order with the minimum possible number of items (1 item).
steps.result: The order is successfully created in the system with 1 item.

steps.step: Ensure the item in the order is not a loose item.
steps.result: The order is confirmed to have 1 non-loose item.

steps.step: Trigger the Pure Pick Time (PPT) calculation for this minimal order.
steps.result: The system initiates the PPT calculation process.

steps.step: Observe the calculated Pure Pick Time.
steps.result: The Pure Pick Time is exactly 200 seconds, which is the default picking time.

steps.step: Verify that no additional buffer time has been added to the base pick time.
steps.result: The system confirms that no extra buffer time was added, as there were no extra items beyond max_items_for_picking and no loose items.

TC-99.description: Confirm that the base pick time is correctly set to the default picking time of 200 seconds when no additional buffers are applicable.
TC-99.preconditions:
1. The system is configured with the default picking time of 200 seconds.
2. An order is created with a number of SKUs equal to or less than the max_items_for_picking.
3. The order contains no loose items.
TC-99.title: Verify base pick time calculation

steps.step: Create an order with the number of SKUs equal to max_items_for_picking and no loose items.
steps.result: The order is successfully created in the system.

steps.step: Trigger the Pure Pick Time (PPT) calculation for the created order.
steps.result: The system initiates the PPT calculation process.

steps.step: Observe the calculated Pure Pick Time.
steps.result: The Pure Pick Time is exactly 200 seconds, which is the default picking time.

steps.step: Verify that no additional buffer time has been added to the base pick time.
steps.result: The system confirms that no extra buffer time was added, as the conditions for additional buffers were not met.

TC-100.description: Ensure that when an order has no extra items or loose items, the pure pick time equals exactly 200 seconds (the default picking time).
TC-100.preconditions:
1. The system is configured with the default picking time of 200 seconds.
2. An order is created with a number of SKUs less than max_items_for_picking.
3. The order contains no loose items.
TC-100.title: Validate pick time with only default configuration

steps.step: Create an order with a number of SKUs less than max_items_for_picking and no loose items.
steps.result: The order is successfully created in the system.

steps.step: Trigger the Pure Pick Time (PPT) calculation for the created order.
steps.result: The system initiates the PPT calculation process.

steps.step: Observe the calculated Pure Pick Time.
steps.result: The Pure Pick Time is exactly 200 seconds, which is the default picking time.

steps.step: Verify that the system did not add any extra buffer time for additional items or loose items.
steps.result: The system confirms that no extra buffer time was added, as there were no extra items beyond max_items_for_picking and no loose items.

Steps
  1. Navigate to the Product Detail Page (PDP) or Product Card of an out-of-stock item

  3. Tap the “Notify Me” button
  4. Verify confirmation message is displayed (e.g., “We’ll notify you when it’s back in stock”)
  5. Update the product inventory status to In Stock (via admin or backend)
steps.result:
• User successfully subscribes to stock notifications
  • A push notification is sent when the item becomes In Stock

TC-01.preconditions:
Math:
max(order picking time, order handover time) - 2 >= ACTUAL Out for delivery timestamp - ACTUAL Order place timestamp,
condition met.
TC-01.title: Verify "Early" Status (Out for Delivery)

steps.step:
1. Place a single order.


  2. Wait for Packing status.


  3. Immediately mark “Out for Delivery” {Ensure Actual Time(b) is <= Expected Time(a) - 2 mins}


  4. Check PN, Home Page, and Track Order Page.
steps.result:
PN: Received "Early" status notification.
Home/Track Page: Shows "Early" pill/status.
Track Order Page : Shows 'Early' status

TC-02.preconditions:
Math:
max(order picking time, order handover time) + 3 < ACTUAL Out for delivery timestamp - ACTUAL Order place timestamp,
condition met.
TC-02.title: Verify "Delay" Status (Out for Delivery)

steps.step:
1. Place a single order.


  2. Wait for Packing status.


  3. Wait X+ mins (past the ETA) before marking “Out for Delivery” (Ensure Actual Time > Expected Time + 3 mins).


  4. Check PN, Home Page, and Track Order Page.
steps.result:
PN: Received "Delay" status notification.
Home/Track Page: Shows "Delay" pill/status.
Track Order Page : Shows 'Delay' status

steps.step:
1. Place a single order.


  2. Wait for Packing status.


  3. Mark “Out for Delivery” roughly at the ETA (Ensure time difference is within -2 to +3 mins).


  4. Check PN, Home Page, and Track Order Page.
steps.result:
PN: Received "On-Time" status notification.

Home/Track Page: Shows "On-Time" pill/status.

Track Order Page : Shows 'Early' status

TC-04.preconditions:
Math:
max(order picking time, order handover time) + Ride time (google time form the BE) - 2 > ACTUAL arrived timestamp - ACTUAL order place timestamp,
condition met.
TC-04.title: Verify "Early" Status (Arrived at Location)

steps.step:
1. Proceed from TC-01 (Order is Out for Delivery).


  2. Immediately mark “Arrived” (Ensure Actual Total Time < Expected Total Time - 2 mins).
steps.result:
PN: Received "Early" status notification.
Home/Track Page: Shows "Early" status.
Track Order Page : Shows 'Early' status

TC-05.preconditions:
Math:
max(order picking time, order handover time) + Ride time (google time form the BE) + 3 < ACTUAL arrived timestamp - ACTUAL order place timestamp,
condition met.
TC-05.title: Verify "Delay" Status (Arrived at Location)

steps.step:
1. Proceed from TC-02 or a new order.


  2. Order is Out for Delivery.


  3. Wait significantly (simulate heavy traffic/lost rider) before marking “Arrived.”
steps.result:
PN: Received "Delay" status notification.
Home/Track Page: Shows "Delay" status.
Track Order Page : Shows 'Delay' status

TC-06.title: Verify 'On time' Status (Arrived at Location) with Boundary (Upper Limit)

steps.step:
max(order picking time, order handover time) (Expected) = 10

Out for delivery timestamp - Order place timestamp (Actual) = 13

(Check: Exactly +3)
steps.result:
Status: "On Time", showing on Notification, Home Page pill, Track Order Page

(Since +3 is excluded from Delay, it falls to On Time).

TC-07.title: Verify 'On Time' Status (Arrived at Location) with Boundary (Lower Limit)

steps.step:
max(order picking time, order handover time) (Expected) = 10

Out for delivery timestamp - Order place timestamp (Actual) = 8

(Check: Exactly -2)
steps.result:
Status: "On Time", showing on Notification, Home Page pill, Track Order Page

(Since -2 is inclusive for On Time).

Variables in strings (e.g., "Get at ₹{price}, shop for ₹{amount} more") must populate correctly in the local language without breaking syntax.
Verify that non-Latin characters (like Devanagari) render clearly without pixelation or "boxes" (tofu characters).

CTA buttons (e.g., "ADD", "NOTIFY", "REMOVE") must expand or wrap text gracefully if the local word is significantly longer.
Badges like "No Stock" or "MRP-Off-Tag" must maintain their respective positions regardless of the language script direction.

TC-1149.title: Verify app behavior when app is killed on the OTP page (New Card + Cashback Coupon).

steps.step:
1. Add item(s) to cart and apply a ‘Cashback Coupon’.
  2. Proceed to payment with ‘Saved Card’ and reach the OTP page.
  3. Force-kill the application.
  4. Relaunch the app.
steps.result: The user is redirected to the Homepage. The transaction is failed, and the cashback coupon remains applied to the items in the cart.

TC-1150.title: Verify app behavior when app is killed on the OTP page (Saved Card + Cashback Coupon).

steps.step:
1. Add item(s) to cart and apply a ‘Cashback Coupon’.
  2. Proceed to payment with ‘Saved Card’ and reach the OTP page.
  3. Force-kill the application.
  4. Relaunch the app.
steps.result: The user is redirected to the Homepage. The transaction is failed, and the cashback coupon remains applied to the items in the cart.

TC-1151.title: Verify app behavior when app is killed on the OTP page (New Card).

steps.step:
1. Add item(s) to cart.
  2. Proceed to payment with a ‘New Card’.
  3. Enter details and reach the 3D Secure (OTP) verification page.
  4. Force-kill the application (or close the browser tab).
  5. Relaunch the application.
steps.result: The user is redirected to the Homepage. The transaction is marked as failed, and the items remain in the cart.

TC-1152.title: Verify app behavior when app is killed on the OTP page (Saved Card).

steps.step:
1. Add item(s) to cart.
  2. Proceed to payment with a ‘Saved Card’.
  3. Reach the 3D Secure (OTP) verification page.
  4. Force-kill the application (or close the browser tab).
  5. Relaunch the application.
steps.result: The user is redirected to the Homepage. The transaction is marked as failed, and the items remain in the cart.

TC-1134.title: Verify successful payment with a New Card + Multiple Offers (Discount, MT, WSH).

steps.step:
1. Add item(s) to cart that qualify for all offers.
  2. Apply ‘Discount Coupon’.
  3. Verify ‘MT Offer’ and ‘WSH Offer’ are also applied (auto or manual).
  4. Verify cart total reflects all stacked discounts.
  5. Proceed to payment with a ‘New Card’ and complete the transaction.
steps.result: Payment is successful. Order Confirmation page clearly lists all applied offers and the final amount paid.

TC-1135.title: Verify successful payment with a Saved Card + Cashback Coupon.

steps.step:
1. (Precondition: User has a saved card).
  2. Add item(s) to cart and apply a ‘Cashback Coupon’.
  3. Verify the cashback offer details are displayed.
  4. Proceed to payment and select the ‘Saved Card’.
  5. Enter CVV and complete the OTP verification.
steps.result: Payment is successful for the full amount. Order Confirmation page shows the order details and information about the cashback.

TC-1136.title: Verify successful payment with a New Card + Cashback Coupon.

steps.step:
1. Add item(s) to cart.
  2. Apply a valid ‘Cashback Coupon’ from the cart page.
  3. Verify the cashback offer details are displayed.
  4. Proceed to payment and select ‘Add New Card’.
  5. Enter valid card details and complete the OTP verification.
steps.result: Payment is successful for the full amount. Order Confirmation page shows the order details and information about the cashback to be credited.

TC-1137.title: Verify successful payment with a Saved Card + Discount Coupon.

steps.step:
1. (Precondition: User has a saved card).
  2. Add item(s) to cart and apply a ‘Discount Coupon’.
  3. Verify the cart total is reduced.
  4. Proceed to payment and select the ‘Saved Card’.
  5. Enter CVV and complete the OTP verification.
steps.result: Payment is successful for the discounted amount. Order Confirmation page shows the correct total and applied discount.

TC-1138.title: Verify successful payment with a New Card + Discount Coupon.

steps.step:
1. Add item(s) to cart.
  2. Apply a valid ‘Discount Coupon’ from the cart page.
  3. Verify the cart total is reduced.
  4. Proceed to payment and select ‘Add New Card’.
  5. Enter valid card details and complete the OTP verification.
steps.result: Payment is successful for the discounted amount. Order Confirmation page shows the correct total and applied discount.

TC-1139.title: Verify successful payment with a Saved Card.

steps.step:
1. (Precondition: User has a card saved to their account).
  2. Add item(s) to cart and proceed to payment.
  3. Select the existing ‘Saved Card’.
  4. Enter the valid CVV.
  5. Complete the 3D Secure (OTP) verification successfully.
steps.result: Payment is successful. User is redirected to the Order Confirmation page.

TC-1140.title: Verify successful payment with a New Card.

steps.step:
1. Add item(s) to cart and proceed to payment.
  2. Select ‘Card (Credit/Debit)’ as the payment method.
  3. Select ‘Add New Card’ and enter valid card details.
  4. Complete the 3D Secure (OTP) verification successfully.
steps.result: Payment is successful. User is redirected to the Order Confirmation page.

TC-1141.title: Verify failure bottom sheet when canceling on the OTP page (New Card + Cashback Coupon).

steps.step:
1. Add item(s) to cart and apply a ‘Cashback Coupon’.
  2. Proceed to payment with ‘Saved Card’ and reach the OTP page.
  3. Press the ‘Cancel’ or ‘Back’ button.
steps.result: A payment failure bottom sheet is displayed. The cart still contains the items, and the cashback coupon remains applied.

TC-1142.title: Verify payment failure with a New Card + Discount Coupon.

steps.step:
1. (Precondition: User has a saved card).
  2. Add item(s) to cart and apply a ‘Discount Coupon’.
  3. Proceed to payment with ‘Saved Card’ and induce a failure (e.g., bank decline).
steps.result: Payment fails, and a failure message is shown on the cart page. The discount coupon remains applied to the cart for the user's next attempt.

TC-1143.title: Verify failure bottom sheet when canceling on the OTP page (Saved Card + Cashback Coupon).

steps.step:
1. Add item(s) to cart and apply a ‘Cashback Coupon’.
  2. Proceed to payment with ‘Saved Card’ and reach the OTP page.
  3. Press the ‘Cancel’ or ‘Back’ button.
steps.result: A payment failure bottom sheet is displayed. The cart still contains the items, and the cashback coupon remains applied.

TC-1144.title: Verify payment failure with a Saved Card + Discount Coupon.

steps.step:
1. (Precondition: User has a saved card).
  2. Add item(s) to cart and apply a ‘Discount Coupon’.
  3. Proceed to payment with ‘Saved Card’ and induce a failure (e.g., bank decline).
steps.result: Payment fails, and a failure message is shown on the cart page. The discount coupon remains applied to the cart for the user's next attempt.

TC-1145.title: Verify 'Retry' flow from the failure bottom sheet (New Card).

steps.step:
1. Follow steps for TC_CARD_F_005, but use a ‘New Card’.
  2. On the payment failure bottom sheet, tap the “Retry online” (or similar) button.
steps.result: The user is redirected back to the payment selection screen, and the "Add a new card" page/interface is presented.

TC-1146.title: Verify failure bottom sheet when canceling on the OTP page (Saved Card).

steps.step:
1. Add item(s) to cart.
  2. Proceed to payment with a ‘Saved Card’.
  3. Reach the 3D Secure (OTP) verification page.
  4. Press the ‘Cancel’ or ‘Back’ button on the bank’s page.
  5. Verify the user is redirected back to the app.
steps.result: A payment failure bottom sheet is displayed, confirming the cancellation and providing options (e.g., "Retry," "Change Payment Method").

TC-1147.title: Verify payment failure on the cart page (e.g., bank decline) with a New Card.

steps.step:
1. Add item(s) to cart and proceed to payment.
  2. Select ‘Add New Card’.
  3. Enter details for a test card that triggers a generic bank decline.
  4. Complete the OTP step.
steps.result: Payment fails. The user remains on the Cart Page, and a clear, user-friendly error message is displayed.

TC-1148.title: Verify payment failure on the cart page (e.g., bank decline) with a Saved Card.

steps.step:
1. (Precondition: User has a saved card).
  2. Add item(s) to cart and proceed to payment.
  3. Select the ‘Saved Card’.
  4. Enter CVV and proceed to OTP.
  5. Use a test card/OTP that triggers a generic bank decline.
steps.result: Payment fails. The user remains on the Cart Page, and a clear error message (e.g., "Your bank has declined the transaction") is displayed.

TC_CARD_S_004.title: Verify successful payment with a Saved Card + Discount Coupon.

steps.step:
1. (Precondition: User has a saved card).
  2. Add item(s) to cart and apply a ‘Discount Coupon’.
  3. Verify the cart total is reduced.
  4. Proceed to payment and select the ‘Saved Card’.
  5. Enter CVV and complete the OTP verification.
steps.result: Payment is successful for the discounted amount. Order Confirmation page shows the correct total and applied discount.

TC_CARD_S_005.title: Verify successful payment with a New Card + Cashback Coupon.

steps.step:
1. Add item(s) to cart.
  2. Apply a valid ‘Cashback Coupon’ from the cart page.
  3. Verify the cashback offer details are displayed.
  4. Proceed to payment and select ‘Add New Card’.
  5. Enter valid card details and complete the OTP verification.
steps.result: Payment is successful for the full amount. Order Confirmation page shows the order details and information about the cashback to be credited.

TC_CARD_S_006.title: Verify successful payment with a Saved Card + Cashback Coupon.

steps.step:
1. (Precondition: User has a saved card).
  2. Add item(s) to cart and apply a ‘Cashback Coupon’.
  3. Verify the cashback offer details are displayed.
  4. Proceed to payment and select the ‘Saved Card’.
  5. Enter CVV and complete the OTP verification.
steps.result: Payment is successful for the full amount. Order Confirmation page shows the order details and information about the cashback.

TC_CARD_S_007.title: Verify successful payment with a New Card + Discount Coupon + Multiple Offers (MT, WSH).

steps.step:
1. Add item(s) to cart that qualify for all offers.
  2. Apply ‘Discount Coupon’.
  3. Verify ‘MT Offer’ and ‘WSH Offer’ are also applied (auto or manual).
  4. Verify cart total reflects all stacked discounts.
  5. Proceed to payment with a ‘New Card’ and complete the transaction.
steps.result: Payment is successful. Order Confirmation page clearly lists all applied offers and the final amount paid.

TC_CARD_F_011.title: Verify app behavior when app is killed on the OTP page (New Card + Cashback Coupon).

steps.step:
1. Add item(s) to cart and apply a ‘Cashback Coupon’.
  2. Proceed to payment with ‘Saved Card’ and reach the OTP page.
  3. Force-kill the application.
  4. Relaunch the app.
steps.result: The user is redirected to the Homepage. The transaction is failed, and the cashback coupon remains applied to the items in the cart.

TC_CARD_F_012.title: Verify failure bottom sheet when canceling on the OTP page (New Card + Cashback Coupon).

steps.step:
1. Add item(s) to cart and apply a ‘Cashback Coupon’.
  2. Proceed to payment with ‘Saved Card’ and reach the OTP page.
  3. Press the ‘Cancel’ or ‘Back’ button.
steps.result: A payment failure bottom sheet is displayed. The cart still contains the items, and the cashback coupon remains applied.

TC_CARD_S_001.title: Verify successful payment with a New Card.

steps.step:
1. Add item(s) to cart and proceed to payment.
  2. Select ‘Card (Credit/Debit)’ as the payment method.
  3. Select ‘Add New Card’ and enter valid card details.
  4. Complete the 3D Secure (OTP) verification successfully.
steps.result: Payment is successful. User is redirected to the Order Confirmation page.

TC_CARD_S_002.title: Verify successful payment with a Saved Card.

steps.step:
1. (Precondition: User has a card saved to their account).
  2. Add item(s) to cart and proceed to payment.
  3. Select the existing ‘Saved Card’.
  4. Enter the valid CVV.
  5. Complete the 3D Secure (OTP) verification successfully.
steps.result: Payment is successful. User is redirected to the Order Confirmation page.

TC_CARD_S_003.title: Verify successful payment with a New Card + Discount Coupon.

steps.step:
1. Add item(s) to cart.
  2. Apply a valid ‘Discount Coupon’ from the cart page.
  3. Verify the cart total is reduced.
  4. Proceed to payment and select ‘Add New Card’.
  5. Enter valid card details and complete the OTP verification.
steps.result: Payment is successful for the discounted amount. Order Confirmation page shows the correct total and applied discount.

TC_CARD_F_006.title: Verify 'Retry' flow from the failure bottom sheet (New Card).

steps.step:
1. Follow steps for TC_CARD_F_005, but use a ‘New Card’.
  2. On the payment failure bottom sheet, tap the “Retry online” (or similar) button.
steps.result: The user is redirected back to the payment selection screen, and the "Add a new card" page/interface is presented.

TC_CARD_F_007.title: Verify payment failure with a Saved Card + Discount Coupon.

steps.step:
1. (Precondition: User has a saved card).
  2. Add item(s) to cart and apply a ‘Discount Coupon’.
  3. Proceed to payment with ‘Saved Card’ and induce a failure (e.g., bank decline).
steps.result: Payment fails, and a failure message is shown on the cart page. The discount coupon remains applied to the cart for the user's next attempt.

TC_CARD_F_008.title: Verify app behavior when app is killed on the OTP page (Saved Card + Cashback Coupon).

steps.step:
1. Add item(s) to cart and apply a ‘Cashback Coupon’.
  2. Proceed to payment with ‘Saved Card’ and reach the OTP page.
  3. Force-kill the application.
  4. Relaunch the app.
steps.result: The user is redirected to the Homepage. The transaction is failed, and the cashback coupon remains applied to the items in the cart.

TC_CARD_F_009.title: Verify failure bottom sheet when canceling on the OTP page (Saved Card + Cashback Coupon).

steps.step:
1. Add item(s) to cart and apply a ‘Cashback Coupon’.
  2. Proceed to payment with ‘Saved Card’ and reach the OTP page.
  3. Press the ‘Cancel’ or ‘Back’ button.
steps.result: A payment failure bottom sheet is displayed. The cart still contains the items, and the cashback coupon remains applied.

TC_CARD_F_010.title: Verify payment failure with a New Card + Discount Coupon.

steps.step:
1. (Precondition: User has a saved card).
  2. Add item(s) to cart and apply a ‘Discount Coupon’.
  3. Proceed to payment with ‘Saved Card’ and induce a failure (e.g., bank decline).
steps.result: Payment fails, and a failure message is shown on the cart page. The discount coupon remains applied to the cart for the user's next attempt.

TC_CARD_F_001.title: Verify payment failure on the cart page (e.g., bank decline) with a Saved Card.

steps.step:
1. (Precondition: User has a saved card).
  2. Add item(s) to cart and proceed to payment.
  3. Select the ‘Saved Card’.
  4. Enter CVV and proceed to OTP.
  5. Use a test card/OTP that triggers a generic bank decline.
steps.result: Payment fails. The user remains on the Cart Page, and a clear error message (e.g., "Your bank has declined the transaction") is displayed.

TC_CARD_F_002.title: Verify payment failure on the cart page (e.g., bank decline) with a New Card.

steps.step:
1. Add item(s) to cart and proceed to payment.
  2. Select ‘Add New Card’.
  3. Enter details for a test card that triggers a generic bank decline.
  4. Complete the OTP step.
steps.result: Payment fails. The user remains on the Cart Page, and a clear, user-friendly error message is displayed.

TC_CARD_F_003.title: Verify app behavior when app is killed on the OTP page (Saved Card).

steps.step:
1. Add item(s) to cart.
  2. Proceed to payment with a ‘Saved Card’.
  3. Reach the 3D Secure (OTP) verification page.
  4. Force-kill the application (or close the browser tab).
  5. Relaunch the application.
steps.result: The user is redirected to the Homepage. The transaction is marked as failed, and the items remain in the cart.

TC_CARD_F_004.title: Verify app behavior when app is killed on the OTP page (New Card).

steps.step:
1. Add item(s) to cart.
  2. Proceed to payment with a ‘New Card’.
  3. Enter details and reach the 3D Secure (OTP) verification page.
  4. Force-kill the application (or close the browser tab).
  5. Relaunch the application.
steps.result: The user is redirected to the Homepage. The transaction is marked as failed, and the items remain in the cart.

TC_CARD_F_005.title: Verify failure bottom sheet when canceling on the OTP page (Saved Card).

steps.step:
1. Add item(s) to cart.
  2. Proceed to payment with a ‘Saved Card’.
  3. Reach the 3D Secure (OTP) verification page.
  4. Press the ‘Cancel’ or ‘Back’ button on the bank’s page.
  5. Verify the user is redirected back to the app.
steps.result: A payment failure bottom sheet is displayed, confirming the cancellation and providing options (e.g., "Retry," "Change Payment Method").

TC-932.description: Select the minimum rating (1 star) from the order rating pills. Verify that this minimum value is correctly mapped and displayed in the rider rating card. Ensure all UI elements and animations function correctly with this edge case.
TC-932.preconditions:
1. The user has completed an order.
2. The user is on the order rating screen.
3. The order rating pills with category order_rider are visible.
4. The rider rating card is not yet open.
TC-932.title: Verify minimum rating selection and display

steps.step: Select the minimum rating (1 star) from the order rating pills.
steps.result: The 1-star order rating pill is highlighted.

steps.step: Observe the transition effect after selecting the minimum rating.
steps.result: A smooth transition effect occurs, leading to the opening of the rider rating card.

steps.step: Check the mapped rating on the rider rating card.
steps.result: The rider rating card displays the minimum rating (1 star) mapped from the order rating.

steps.step: Verify the UI elements on the rider rating card.
steps.result: All UI elements (stars, buttons, text) are correctly displayed and aligned for the 1-star rating.

steps.step: Attempt to decrease the rating below 1 star.
steps.result: The system does not allow selection below 1 star, maintaining the minimum rating.

steps.step: Click the 'X' button to save and exit the rating.
steps.result: The rating is saved as 1 star, and the user exits the rating screen.

steps.step: Re-enter the rating screen and check the persisted rating.
steps.result: The previously saved 1-star rating is displayed correctly.

steps.step: Use a screen reader to navigate through the rating options.
steps.result: The screen reader correctly announces the minimum rating and associated UI elements.

TC-933.description: Choose the maximum rating (5 stars) from the order rating pills. Confirm that this maximum value is accurately mapped to the rider rating card and displayed correctly. Verify that all associated UI components handle this upper limit appropriately.
TC-933.preconditions:
1. The user has completed an order.
2. The user is on the order rating screen.
3. The order rating pills with category order_rider are visible.
4. The rider rating card is not yet open.
TC-933.title: Test maximum rating selection and mapping

steps.step: Select the maximum rating (5 stars) from the order rating pills.
steps.result: The 5-star order rating pill is highlighted.

steps.step: Observe the transition animation after selecting the maximum rating.
steps.result: A smooth transition animation occurs, leading to the opening of the rider rating card.

steps.step: Check the mapped rating on the rider rating card.
steps.result: The rider rating card displays the maximum rating (5 stars) mapped from the order rating.

steps.step: Verify the UI elements on the rider rating card.
steps.result: All UI elements (stars, buttons, text) are correctly displayed and aligned for the 5-star rating.

steps.step: Attempt to increase the rating above 5 stars.
steps.result: The system does not allow selection above 5 stars, maintaining the maximum rating.

steps.step: Click the 'Tell us more' button to submit the rating.
steps.result: The rating is submitted as 5 stars, and the user cannot edit it further.

steps.step: Restart the app and check the persisted rating.
steps.result: The previously submitted 5-star rating is displayed correctly and cannot be edited.

steps.step: Change the app language to a different locale.
steps.result: All labels and prompts related to the 5-star rating are correctly localized.

TC-934.description: Attempt to proceed without selecting any order_rider pill. Verify that the rider rating card doesn't open or that the system prompts the user to make a selection. Ensure this validation prevents submission of unintended or null ratings.
TC-934.preconditions: 1. The user has completed an order. 2. The user is on the order rating screen. 3. The order rating pills with category order_rider are visible. 4. No rating has been selected yet.
TC-934.title: Validate system behavior with no rating selected

steps.step: In initial state.
steps.result: The rating screen is in its initial state with no rating selected.

steps.step: Try to proceed or submit without selecting a rating.
steps.result: The system prevents submission and reminds the user to make a selection.

steps.step: Attempt to click the 'X' button without selecting a rating.
steps.result: The user is able to exit the rating screen without saving any rating.

TC-935.description: After selecting a rating on the rider rating card, click the 'Tell us more' button. Verify that the rating is saved and submitted as final. Ensure that the user cannot edit the rating further after this action, maintaining data integrity.
TC-935.preconditions: 1. User has completed an order 2. User has navigated to the rating screen 3. User has selected an order rating pill with category order_rider 4. Rider rating card is open with a mapped rating

steps.result: The selected rating is displayed on the rider rating card

steps.step: Click on the 'Tell us more' textarea.
steps.result: You are being able to write the tell us more

TC-938.description: Assess the system's offline rating functionality. Allow users to complete the entire rating process while offline, including order rating pill selection and rider rating card interactions. Verify that all offline ratings are properly stored locally and synchronized with the server once the network connection is restored.
TC-938.preconditions:
1. The user has completed an order but hasn't rated it yet.
2. The app is installed on a device with offline capability.
3. The device is set to airplane mode or has no network connectivity.
4. The app has sufficient local storage for offline data.
TC-938.title: Offline rating capability and synchronization

steps.step: Ensure the device is offline by enabling airplane mode or disabling all network connections.
steps.result: The device shows no active internet connection.

steps.step: Open the app and navigate to the rating screen for the completed order.
steps.result: The rating screen loads successfully, displaying order rating pills.

steps.step: Select an order rating pill with category order_rider.
steps.result: The pill is selected and the rider rating card opens with the mapped rating and transition animation, even in offline mode.

steps.step: Modify the rider rating if desired (e.g., change from 4 to 5 stars).
steps.result: The rating change is reflected in the UI without issues.

steps.step: Click the 'Tell us more' button to submit the rating.
steps.result: The app saves the rating locally and shows a message indicating the rating will be synced when online.

steps.step: Exit the rating screen and reopen it while still offline.
steps.result: The previously entered rating is displayed correctly, showing it was saved locally.

steps.step: Enable network connectivity on the device.
steps.result: The device regains internet connection.

steps.step: Open the app and wait for synchronization to occur.
steps.result: The app detects the network connection and automatically syncs the offline rating data with the server.

steps.step: Check the rating status on the server or in the app's online mode.
steps.result: The rating submitted offline is now visible and correctly synchronized with the server.

steps.step: Verify the app's analytics logs.
steps.result: Confirm that all offline rating actions and subsequent synchronization events are properly logged.

TC-941.description: Validate that for users without saved addresses, the bottom sheet opens with the keyboard visible for immediate address entry. Verify that users can successfully enter and submit a new address.
TC-941.preconditions: 1. The app is installed and launched. 2. User has completed the signup process. 3. User has no saved addresses in their profile. 4. The app is on the screen where address selection/entry is required.
TC-941.title: Test bottom sheet behavior for users without saved addresses

steps.step: Navigate to the address entry screen
steps.result: The address bottom sheet opens

steps.step: Observe the state of the bottom sheet
steps.result: The bottom sheet is visible with the keyboard automatically shown for address entry

steps.step: Check the content of the bottom sheet
steps.result: An empty address entry form is displayed

steps.step: Enter a new address in the provided fields
steps.result: The address details are visible as they are typed

steps.step: Confirm Location the new address
steps.result: The app processes the entered address

steps.step: Verify the submission of the new address
steps.result: The bottom sheet closes and the new address is applied to the current location

steps.step: Check the applied address
steps.result: The newly entered address is correctly displayed in the relevant part of the app (e.g., Home Page)

TC-943.description: Verify app behavior when location permission is revoked after initial grant. The app should detect the change, update UI accordingly, and provide appropriate options to re-request permission or use alternative flows.
TC-943.preconditions: 1. The app is installed and set up on the device. 2. The user has completed the initial onboarding process. 3. Location permission has been initially granted to the app. 4. The app is in a state where it's actively using or displaying location-based features.
TC-943.title: Location permission revocation after initial grant

steps.step: Open the device's Settings app.
steps.result: The Settings app opens successfully.

steps.step: Navigate to the app's permission settings.
steps.result: The app's permission settings are displayed.

steps.step: Revoke the location permission for the app.
steps.result: The location permission is successfully revoked.

steps.step: Return to the app without closing it (if it's in the background).
steps.result: The app is brought to the foreground.

steps.step: Observe the app's behavior and UI updates.
steps.result: The app should detect the permission change and update its UI accordingly. This may include showing a message about the revoked permission and providing options to re-request permission or use alternative flows.

steps.step: Attempt to use a location-based feature in the app.
steps.result: The app should prompt the user to grant location permission again or offer an alternative method (e.g., manual address entry).

steps.step: Using a location-based feature in the app. leads to the app settings and ask for the grant the location for permission.
steps.result: The app should resume normal location-based functionality.

steps.step: Verify that the app maintains correct state and functionality after permission is re-granted.
steps.result: The app should return to its previous state with location features working correctly.

TC-944.description: Test app's response when user changes location permission from precise to approximate. The app should treat this as location not given and trigger the appropriate fallback flow.
TC-944.preconditions: 1. The app is installed and set up on the device. 2. The user has completed the initial onboarding process. 3. Precise location permission has been initially granted to the app. 4. The app is in a state where it's actively using or displaying location-based features.
TC-944.title: Transition from precise to approximate location

steps.step: Open the device's Settings app.
steps.result: The Settings app opens successfully.

steps.step: Navigate to the app's location permission settings.
steps.result: The app's location permission settings are displayed.

steps.step: Change the location permission from precise to approximate.
steps.result: The location permission is successfully changed to approximate.

steps.step: Return to the app without closing it (if it's in the background).
steps.result: The app is brought to the foreground.

steps.step: Observe the app's behavior and UI updates.
steps.result: The app should detect the permission change and treat it as if location is not given. It should update its UI accordingly, possibly showing a message about the need for precise location.

steps.step: Attempt to use a location-based feature in the app.
steps.result: The app should trigger the appropriate fallback flow, such as prompting for precise location permission or offering manual address entry.

steps.step: Verify that the app maintains correct state and functionality.
steps.result: The app should work with approximate location correctly.

TC-948.description: Verify that the app preserves the state of the address bottom sheet, including keyboard visibility and saved addresses, when backgrounded and foregrounded during user interaction.
TC-948.preconditions:
1. The app is installed on the device.
2. The user has completed the initial setup and granted necessary permissions.
3. The user has reached the point where the address bottom sheet is displayed.
4. The app has a mix of users with and without saved addresses to test both scenarios.
TC-948.title: Background transition during address bottom sheet interaction

steps.step: Launch the app and progress through the initial setup and permission grants to reach the address bottom sheet.
steps.result: The address bottom sheet should be displayed. For users with saved addresses, it should show the list of addresses without the keyboard. For users without saved addresses, it should show the keyboard for address entry.

steps.step: For users with saved addresses, scroll through the list of addresses. For users without saved addresses, start typing an address.
steps.result: The bottom sheet should respond correctly to user interactions, showing smooth scrolling for saved addresses or appropriate keyboard behavior for address entry.

steps.step: Without completing the address selection or entry, background the app (e.g., press the home button or switch to another app).
steps.result: The app should go into the background without crashing. The address bottom sheet should disappear from view.

steps.step: Wait for approximately 30 seconds.
steps.result: The app should remain in the background without any visible activity.

steps.step: Bring the app back to the foreground (e.g., tap on the app icon or use the recent apps menu).
steps.result: The app should resume, displaying the address bottom sheet in the same state as before backgrounding. For users with saved addresses, the list should be visible. For users without saved addresses, the keyboard and any partially entered address should be preserved.

steps.step: Continue interacting with the address bottom sheet (select a saved address or complete address entry).
steps.result:
The app should respond appropriately to the user's actions:
- For saved address selection: The chosen address should be highlighted or set as the current address.
- For manual address entry: The app should accept the entered address and proceed to the next step.

steps.step: Complete any remaining steps in the app's setup or onboarding process.
steps.result: The app should guide the user through any remaining steps without issues, using the selected or entered address as appropriate.

steps.step: Verify that the app reaches its final state correctly (e.g., store assigned, main screen displayed) with the correct address information.
steps.result: The app should reach its final state correctly, with the chosen or entered address properly associated with the user's account or current session.

steps.step: If possible, navigate to a section of the app where the user's address is displayed or used.
steps.result: The address information should be correctly displayed or utilized, matching the selection or entry made during the bottom sheet interaction.

TC-949.description: Calculate buffer time for an order with a significantly higher number of SKUs than max_items_for_picking. Set num_skus to 50, max_items_for_picking to 10, and extra_items_buffer to 40s. Expected buffer time should be (50 - 10) * 40s = 1600s.
TC-949.preconditions:
1. The system is configured with default picking time of 200s.
2. The max_items_for_picking is set to 10.
3. The extra_items_buffer is set to 40s per extra item.
4. An order with 50 SKUs is created in the system.
TC-949.title: Test buffer time calculation for large number of extra items

steps.step: Calculate the number of extra items
steps.result: The system should determine that there are 40 extra items (50 SKUs - 10 max_items_for_picking)

steps.step: Calculate the buffer time for extra items
steps.result: The system should calculate the buffer time as 40 * 40s = 1600s

steps.step: Verify the total Pure Pick Time (PPT)
steps.result: The system should return a total PPT of 1800s (200s default picking time + 1600s buffer time)

TC-950.description: Verify the comprehensive ETA calculation using all components: Pure Pick Time (A), Pick Delay (B), Pure Ride Time (C), Rider Handover Time (D), and Rain Buffer (F). Set A=200s, B=100s, C=900s, D=150s, F=900s. Expected ETA should be C + max(A+B, D) + F = 900s + max(300s, 150s) + 900s = 2100s.
TC-950.preconditions:
1. The ETA calculation system is up and running.
2. All components of the ETA calculation are available and functioning correctly.
3. The system is configured with the following values:
   - Pure Pick Time (A) = 200s
   - Pick Delay (B) = 100s
   - Pure Ride Time (C) = 900s
   - Rider Handover Time (D) = 150s
   - Rain Buffer (F) = 900s
4. There are sufficient pickers and riders available in the system.
5. The weather condition is set to 'raining' to activate the rain buffer.
TC-950.title: Calculate ETA with all components present

steps.step: Initiate an order in the system that requires ETA calculation.
steps.result: The system accepts the order and begins the ETA calculation process.

steps.step: Trigger the ETA calculation for the order.
steps.result: The system starts calculating the ETA using all components: A, B, C, D, and F.

steps.step: Verify that the Pure Pick Time (A) is set to 200s.
steps.result: The system uses 200s for the Pure Pick Time component.

steps.step: Confirm that the Pick Delay (B) is set to 100s.
steps.result: The system uses 100s for the Pick Delay component.

steps.step: Check that the Pure Ride Time (C) is set to 900s.
steps.result: The system uses 900s for the Pure Ride Time component.

steps.step: Ensure that the Rider Handover Time (D) is set to 150s.
steps.result: The system uses 150s for the Rider Handover Time component.

steps.step: Verify that the Rain Buffer (F) is applied and set to 900s.
steps.result: The system applies the 900s Rain Buffer to the calculation.

steps.step: Observe the final ETA calculation performed by the system.
steps.result: The system calculates the ETA using the formula: C + max(A+B, D) + F

steps.step: Verify the calculated ETA value.
steps.result: The system returns an ETA of 2100s (900s + max(300s, 150s) + 900s).

steps.step: Compare the system-calculated ETA with the expected value of 2100s.
steps.result: The system-calculated ETA matches the expected value of 2100s.

TC-951.description: Verify the system's accuracy in detecting when riders cross the store geofence boundary. Test various scenarios including riders moving in and out of the boundary rapidly, and ensure that the system correctly marks riders out when they are detected outside the geofence.
TC-951.preconditions:
1. The system is set up with a defined store geofence boundary.
2. Multiple riders are registered and active in the system.
3. GPS tracking is enabled and functioning for all riders.
4. The system's rider mark out/in logic is operational.
5. A test environment is set up to simulate various GPS coordinates.
TC-951.title: Rider Geofence Boundary Detection Accuracy

steps.step: Configure a rider's GPS to show them inside the store geofence.
steps.result: The system should recognize the rider as being inside the geofence.

steps.step: Simulate the rider moving just outside the geofence boundary.
steps.result: The system should detect that the rider has crossed the boundary and mark them out.

steps.step: Quickly move the simulated GPS location back inside the geofence.
steps.result: The system should detect the rider is back inside and not mark them out if the duration outside was very brief (less than the system's threshold).

steps.step: Simulate the rider moving back and forth across the geofence boundary multiple times in quick succession.
steps.result: The system should accurately track these movements without false mark-outs for very brief boundary crossings.

steps.step: Keep the rider outside the geofence for a duration exceeding the system's threshold for marking out.
steps.result: The system should mark the rider out after the specified duration (as per the rider mark out logic).

steps.step: Simulate GPS signal loss while the rider is near the boundary.
steps.result: The system should handle this gracefully, potentially prompting for manual location confirmation if the feature is available.

steps.step: Test with multiple riders simultaneously crossing boundaries at different locations.
steps.result: The system should accurately track and update the status of all riders without delays or errors.

TC-952.description: Test the system's performance when recalculating ETAs for a large number of orders simultaneously, such as when weather conditions change (activating or deactivating the Rain Buffer). Ensure that all ETA components (PPT, PD, PRT, RHT, RB) are calculated correctly for each order without system slowdown.
TC-952.preconditions:
1. The system is operational with the ETA calculation module functioning correctly.
2. A large number of active orders (at least 100) are in various stages of the delivery process.
3. The weather condition monitoring system is active and can trigger changes.
4. All ETA components (PPT, PD, PRT, RHT, RB) are implemented and functioning.
5. The system's caching mechanism for order data is operational.
6. Some riders and pickers are available in the system.
TC-952.title: Simultaneous ETA Recalculations for Multiple Orders

steps.step: Simulate a sudden change in weather conditions (e.g., from not raining to raining).
steps.result: The system should detect the weather change and prepare to update the Rain Buffer (RB) for all active orders.

steps.step: Trigger simultaneous ETA recalculations for all active orders (at least 100).
steps.result: The system should initiate ETA recalculations for all affected orders concurrently.

steps.step: Monitor the calculation of Pure Pick Time (PPT) for each order.
steps.result: PPT should be calculated correctly for each order based on its specific characteristics (number of items, loose items, etc.).

steps.step: Verify the Pick Delay (PD) calculations during the mass recalculation.
steps.result: PD should be accurately computed for each order, considering the current picker availability and order queue.

steps.step: Check the Pure Ride Time (PRT) calculations for all orders.
steps.result: PRT should be calculated correctly, either using the Google API (if available) or falling back to the default time.

steps.step: Validate the Rider Handover Time (RHT) calculations for all orders.
steps.result: RHT should be accurately calculated for each order based on the current rider availability and order status.

steps.step: Confirm the application of the Rain Buffer (RB) to all orders.
steps.result: The 15-minute rain buffer should be correctly applied to all orders due to the simulated weather change.

steps.step: Verify the final ETA calculations for all orders.
steps.result: The system should compute the final ETA for each order correctly using the formula: C + max(A+B, D) + F.

steps.step: Monitor system performance and response time during the mass ETA recalculation.
steps.result: The system should maintain responsiveness and complete all ETA recalculations without significant delays or errors.

steps.step: Check the caching mechanism for updated order data.
steps.result: The system should efficiently update and manage the cache for all recalculated order ETAs.

steps.step: Attempt to retrieve updated ETAs for multiple orders simultaneously.
steps.result: The system should provide the newly calculated ETAs for all queried orders quickly and accurately.

TC-953.description: Test the system's ability to maintain cache consistency when multiple orders are being updated simultaneously while ETA calculations are in progress. Verify that the ETA calculations use the most up-to-date order information from the cache.
TC-953.preconditions:
1. The order delivery system is operational with cache management active.
2. Multiple orders (at least 10) are present in the system with various statuses.
3. Sufficient riders and pickers are available and marked as 'in'.
4. The ETA calculation system is ready to process multiple orders simultaneously.
5. The system is configured to handle concurrent updates and calculations.
TC-953.title: Concurrent Order Updates and ETA Calculations

steps.step: Initiate simultaneous status updates for multiple orders (at least 5) to different statuses (e.g., ORDER_CONFIRMED, dispatched, out for delivery, delivered).
steps.result: All order status updates are processed concurrently without errors.

steps.step: Trigger ETA calculations for all orders in the system immediately after initiating the status updates.
steps.result: ETA calculations start for all orders without waiting for status update completions.

steps.step: Monitor the cache updates for each order during the status change process.
steps.result: The cache reflects real-time status changes for each order without conflicts or data loss.

steps.step: Verify the ETA calculation results for each order after completion.
steps.result: All ETA calculations use the most recent order status information from the cache.

steps.step: Rapidly change the status of one order multiple times during ongoing ETA calculations.
steps.result: The system handles the rapid status changes without crashing, and the final ETA calculation uses the latest status.

steps.step: Check the consistency of cache data for all orders after the concurrent operations.
steps.result: The cache data is consistent with the actual order statuses in the database for all orders.

steps.step: Attempt to retrieve cached data for an order that was marked as delivered during the process.

steps.step: Verify the system logs for any errors or inconsistencies during the concurrent operations.
steps.result: No critical errors or data inconsistencies are reported in the system logs.

Ensure applying a WH discount doesn’t conflict with or overwrite the milestone offer flow.

The invoice after placing the order reflects the actual savings and final price paid to the user.

steps.result: The UI must provide a transparent breakdown of savings to the user.
The final bill value must be correct: (WH Price + Commission + Discounts).

TC-799.description: Verify that the system handles session timeouts gracefully during the payment process, preventing unauthorized transactions and providing clear user feedback.

steps.result: The system prevents the payment from proceeding

steps.step: Observe the user interface
steps.result: A clear error message is displayed informing the user about the session timeout

steps.step: Attempt to navigate back to the payment page
steps.result: The user is redirected to the login page or asked to re-authenticate

steps.step: Log back in and navigate to the cart
steps.result: The cart items are preserved, and no unauthorized transaction has occurred

steps.step: Check the payment status in the backend
steps.result: No payment transaction is recorded for the timed-out session

TC-789.description: Verify that the horizontal scrolling of the coupon carousel remains smooth and responsive, even when multiple coupons are loaded.

2. Multiple coupons are available and loaded in the system.
3. The user is on the payment page where the coupon carousel is visible.

attachments.name: Shiv - Card Payments & MOP Offers Test scenarios (1).pdf

steps.step: Load the payment page with the coupon carousel.
steps.result: The payment page loads successfully with the coupon carousel visible.

steps.step: Observe the initial state of the coupon carousel.

steps.step: Perform a horizontal swipe gesture on the coupon carousel.
steps.result: The carousel responds immediately to the touch input and begins scrolling smoothly in the direction of the swipe.

steps.step: Release the swipe gesture.
steps.result: The carousel continues to scroll with a natural deceleration, coming to a stop without any abrupt movements.

steps.step: Quickly swipe left and right multiple times.
steps.result: The carousel responds to each swipe promptly, changing direction without lag or stutter.

steps.step: Slowly scroll through the entire carousel from start to end.
steps.result: All coupons are displayed smoothly as the user scrolls, with no jumping or skipping of coupons.

steps.step: Tap on an arrow button (if available) to scroll the carousel.
steps.result: The carousel scrolls smoothly to the next set of coupons without any delay or jerky movement.

steps.step: Scroll to the end of the carousel and attempt to scroll further.
steps.result: The carousel shows a visual indication (e.g., a bounce effect or fade) that the end has been reached, without breaking the smooth scrolling experience.

steps.step: Scroll back to the beginning of the carousel.
steps.result: The scrolling remains smooth and responsive, with the carousel returning to its initial position without any glitches.

steps.step: Repeat steps 3-9 multiple times to ensure consistent performance.
steps.result: The carousel maintains smooth and responsive scrolling behavior throughout repeated interactions, with no degradation in performance.

attachments.name: Shiv - Card Payments & MOP Offers Test scenarios (1).pdf

steps.step: Navigate to the final payment confirmation page.
steps.result: The page loads successfully with payment details and a 'Pay Now' or similar button visible.

steps.step: Click on the 'Pay Now' button to initiate the payment process.
steps.result: The UI immediately shows a loading indicator or animation to signify that the payment is being processed.

steps.result: The loading indicator remains active and animating smoothly, indicating ongoing processing.
