steps.result: The UI smoothly transitions to a payment confirmation or error screen without any abrupt changes or flickering.

steps.step: Initiate another payment process immediately after the previous one.
steps.result: The UI resets and begins the new payment process responsively, without any lag from the previous transaction.

steps.step: Simulate a slow network connection and initiate a payment.
steps.result: The UI remains responsive, showing appropriate loading indicators and possibly a message about the slow connection.

steps.step: Attempt to cancel the payment during processing (if this feature is available).
steps.result: The cancel action is registered immediately, and the UI responds by either stopping the process or showing a cancellation in progress message.

steps.step: Rapidly switch between different sections of the app during payment processing.
steps.result: The app remains responsive to navigation attempts, either allowing the user to leave the payment screen (with appropriate warnings) or gracefully preventing navigation while maintaining UI responsiveness.

TC-793.description: Ensure that the coupon popup appears and disappears smoothly when triggered from the cart, with no delay or jerky animations.
TC-793.preconditions:
1. The payment system is operational and accessible.
2. The user is logged in and has items in the cart.
3. Coupons are available for the items in the cart.
4. The user is on the cart page with an option to apply coupons.
TC-793.title: Dynamic coupon popup responsiveness

attachments.name: Shiv - Card Payments & MOP Offers Test scenarios (1).pdf

steps.step: Load the cart page with items and the 'Apply Coupon' option.
steps.result: The cart page loads successfully with all items and the 'Apply Coupon' button or link clearly visible.

steps.step: Click on the 'Apply Coupon' button or link.
steps.result: The coupon popup appears immediately without any noticeable delay, smoothly animated if animations are used.

steps.step: Observe the initial state of the coupon popup.
steps.result: The popup is fully rendered with all coupon options and any search functionality loaded and ready for interaction.

steps.step: Scroll through the list of coupons in the popup (if applicable).
steps.result: The scrolling is smooth and responsive, with no lag or jerkiness in the movement.

steps.step: Select a coupon from the list.
steps.result: The selection is registered instantly, with immediate visual feedback (e.g., highlighting the selected coupon).

steps.step: Click the 'Apply' or 'OK' button to apply the selected coupon.
steps.result: The popup closes smoothly and instantly, returning to the cart view without any delay.

steps.step: Observe the cart update after applying the coupon.
steps.result: The cart immediately reflects the applied coupon, updating totals and showing any discount information without lag.

steps.step: Reopen the coupon popup by clicking 'Apply Coupon' again.
steps.result: The popup reappears instantly, maintaining the smooth animation if used.

steps.step: Click outside the popup area to dismiss it.
steps.result: The popup disappears immediately and smoothly, returning focus to the cart page.

steps.step: Rapidly open and close the coupon popup multiple times.
steps.result: Each interaction results in immediate and smooth appearance or disappearance of the popup, with no degradation in responsiveness.

TC-784.description: Verify that all payment options, including UPI, cards, and wallets, are properly announced and navigable using screen readers (TalkBack on Android, VoiceOver on iOS).
TC-784.preconditions:
1. The payment gateway application is installed and running on both Android and iOS devices.
2. Screen readers (TalkBack for Android, VoiceOver for iOS) are enabled on the respective devices.
3. The user has navigated to the payment options page.
4. Various payment options including UPI, cards, and wallets are available.
TC-784.title: Screen reader compatibility for payment options

attachments.name: Shiv - Card Payments & MOP Offers Test scenarios (1).pdf

steps.step: Navigate through all available payment options using the screen reader.
steps.result: Each payment option (UPI, cards, wallets) is properly announced by the screen reader, providing clear and accurate information about each option.

steps.step: Select each payment option using the screen reader.
steps.result: The screen reader announces the selection of each payment option correctly, and any additional information or fields related to the selected option are also announced.

steps.step: Navigate through any sub-options or fields within each payment method (e.g., different UPI apps, card types, wallet providers).
steps.result: All sub-options and fields are properly announced and can be navigated using the screen reader.

steps.step: Attempt to complete a payment using each method, guided solely by screen reader announcements.
steps.result: The user can successfully complete the payment process for each method using only the screen reader guidance, without need for visual cues.

steps.step: Verify the accessibility of any payment confirmation or receipt screens.
steps.result: Payment confirmation and receipt information is properly announced by the screen reader, providing all necessary transaction details.

TC-785.description: Ensure that error messages for payment failures are clearly announced by screen readers, providing users with actionable information without relying on visual cues.
TC-785.preconditions:
1. The payment gateway application is installed and running on both Android and iOS devices.
2. Screen readers (TalkBack for Android, VoiceOver for iOS) are enabled on the respective devices.
3. The user has initiated a payment process.
4. Test scenarios are set up to trigger various payment failure situations.
TC-785.title: Accessible error messages for payment failures

attachments.name: Shiv - Card Payments & MOP Offers Test scenarios (1).pdf

steps.step: Attempt a payment with insufficient funds.
steps.result: The screen reader clearly announces an error message indicating insufficient funds, providing actionable information on how to resolve the issue.

steps.step: Try to make a payment with an expired card.
steps.result: The screen reader announces an error message about the expired card, suggesting to update the card information or use a different payment method.

steps.step: Simulate a network error during payment processing.
steps.result: The screen reader announces a clear error message about the network issue, advising the user to check their connection and try again.

steps.step: Attempt a payment with an invalid UPI ID.
steps.result: The screen reader announces an error message indicating the UPI ID is invalid, providing guidance on how to enter a correct UPI ID.

steps.step: Navigate through all elements of the error message using the screen reader.
steps.result: All parts of the error message, including any additional details or action buttons, are properly announced and navigable.

TC-786.description: Verify that screen readers provide appropriate feedback during payment processing, including progress updates and confirmation of successful transactions.
TC-786.preconditions:
1. The payment gateway application is installed and running on both Android and iOS devices.
2. Screen readers (TalkBack for Android, VoiceOver for iOS) are enabled on the respective devices.
3. The user has selected a payment method and is ready to process a payment.
4. A test environment is set up to simulate various payment processing scenarios.
TC-786.title: Navigation feedback during payment processing

attachments.name: Shiv - Card Payments & MOP Offers Test scenarios (1).pdf

steps.step: Initiate a payment transaction.
steps.result: The screen reader announces that the payment process has started and provides initial feedback.

steps.step: Wait for the payment to process.
steps.result: The screen reader provides periodic updates on the payment progress, such as 'Verifying payment details', 'Processing transaction', etc.

steps.step: Simulate a successful transaction completion.
steps.result: The screen reader clearly announces the successful completion of the transaction, including relevant details like transaction ID or amount paid.

steps.step: Navigate through the payment confirmation screen using the screen reader.
steps.result: All elements of the confirmation screen, including transaction details and any follow-up actions, are properly announced and navigable.

steps.step: Simulate a payment that takes longer than usual to process.
steps.result: The screen reader provides appropriate updates about the extended processing time, reassuring the user that the transaction is still in progress.

TC-787.description: Ensure that the process of selecting and applying coupons is fully accessible via screen readers, including clear announcements of coupon details and effects on the payment.
TC-787.preconditions:
1. The payment gateway application is installed and running on both Android and iOS devices.
2. Screen readers (TalkBack for Android, VoiceOver for iOS) are enabled on the respective devices.
3. The user has navigated to the payment page where coupons can be applied.
4. Multiple coupons with different conditions are available for selection.
TC-787.title: Accessibility of coupon selection and application

attachments.name: Shiv - Card Payments & MOP Offers Test scenarios (1).pdf

steps.step: Navigate to the coupon selection area using the screen reader.
steps.result: The screen reader announces the presence of available coupons and how to access them.

steps.step: Browse through the list of available coupons using the screen reader.
steps.result: Each coupon's details, including its code, discount amount, and any conditions, are clearly announced by the screen reader.

steps.step: Select a coupon using the screen reader.
steps.result: The screen reader announces the selection of the coupon and any immediate effects on the payment (e.g., updated total amount).

steps.step: Apply the selected coupon to the payment.
steps.result: The screen reader announces the successful application of the coupon and the updated payment details, including the new total amount.

steps.step: Attempt to apply an incompatible coupon (e.g., for a different payment method).
steps.result: The screen reader clearly announces why the coupon cannot be applied and provides guidance on selecting a compatible coupon.

TC-788.description: Verify that the entire payment flow can be completed using keyboard navigation alone, ensuring all interactive elements are focusable and operable.
TC-788.preconditions:
1. The payment gateway application is accessible via a web browser or mobile app.
2. The user has items in their cart and is ready to proceed to payment.
3. Various payment options including UPI, cards, and wallets are available.
4. The testing environment supports keyboard navigation.
TC-788.title: Keyboard navigation for payment flow

attachments.name: Shiv - Card Payments & MOP Offers Test scenarios (1).pdf

steps.step: Navigate to the payment page using only keyboard controls (Tab, Shift+Tab, Enter).
steps.result: The user can successfully reach the payment page, with all interactive elements being focusable in a logical order.

steps.step: Tab through all payment options and select a payment method using only the keyboard.
steps.result: All payment options are focusable, and the user can select a payment method using the Enter key or Space bar.

steps.step: Enter payment details (e.g., card number, expiry date, CVV) using only the keyboard.
steps.result: All input fields are focusable and can be filled out using the keyboard. The user can navigate between fields using Tab and Shift+Tab.

steps.step: Navigate to and apply a coupon code using only keyboard controls.
steps.result: The coupon code field is focusable, can be filled out, and the 'Apply' button can be activated using the keyboard.

steps.step: Complete the payment process by navigating to and activating the 'Pay' button using only the keyboard.
steps.result: The 'Pay' button is focusable and can be activated using the Enter key or Space bar, successfully completing the payment process.

TC-779.description: Verify that the UPI payment flow, including app detection and deep linking, is consistent between Android and iOS platforms.
TC-779.preconditions:
1. The payment system is set up and functional on both Android and iOS platforms.
2. Test devices with the latest versions of Android and iOS are available.
3. UPI-supported apps (e.g., GPay, PhonePe) are installed on both Android and iOS devices.
4. A test user account is created and logged in on both platforms.
5. A cart with items meeting the minimum order value is prepared on both platforms.
TC-779.title: UPI payment flow consistency across platforms

attachments.name: Shiv - Card Payments & MOP Offers Test scenarios (1).pdf

steps.step: Launch the payment application on both Android and iOS devices.
steps.result: The application launches successfully on both platforms.

steps.step: Navigate to the payment page on both devices.
steps.result: The payment page is displayed consistently on both Android and iOS.

steps.step: Verify UPI payment option visibility.
steps.result: UPI payment option is visible on both platforms if supported apps are installed.

steps.step: Select the UPI payment option on both devices.
steps.result: UPI payment flow is initiated consistently on both platforms.

steps.step: Verify UPI app detection.
steps.result: The system correctly detects installed UPI apps (e.g., GPay, PhonePe) on both Android and iOS.

steps.step: Select a detected UPI app for payment.
steps.result: The selected UPI app is launched via deep linking on both platforms.

steps.step: Complete the UPI payment process in the launched app.
steps.result: The payment process completes successfully in the UPI app on both platforms.

steps.step: Return to the merchant app after payment completion.
steps.result: The user is redirected back to the merchant app consistently on both platforms.

steps.step: Verify payment status display.
steps.result: Payment success/failure status is displayed consistently on both Android and iOS.

steps.step: Check for any platform-specific inconsistencies or errors during the entire flow.
steps.result: No platform-specific issues or inconsistencies are observed in the UPI payment flow.

TC-780.description: Ensure that the card payment interface, including BIN detection and offer display, functions identically on both Android and iOS devices.
TC-780.preconditions:
1. The payment system is set up and functional on both Android and iOS platforms.
2. Test devices with the latest versions of Android and iOS are available.
3. A test user account is created and logged in on both platforms.
4. A cart with items meeting the minimum order value is prepared on both platforms.
5. Test credit/debit cards of various types (Visa, MasterCard, etc.) are available for testing.
TC-780.title: Card payment interface compatibility

attachments.name: Shiv - Card Payments & MOP Offers Test scenarios (1).pdf

steps.step: Launch the payment application on both Android and iOS devices.
steps.result: The application launches successfully on both platforms.

steps.step: Navigate to the payment page on both devices.
steps.result: The payment page is displayed consistently on both Android and iOS.

steps.step: Verify card payment option visibility.
steps.result: Card payment option is visible and accessible on both platforms.

steps.step: Select the card payment option on both devices.
steps.result: Card payment interface is displayed consistently on both platforms.

steps.step: Enter card details (number, expiry, CVV) for a Visa card on both devices.
steps.result: Card details are accepted, and BIN is detected correctly on both platforms.

steps.step: Verify offer display based on the entered card BIN.
steps.result: Relevant offers are displayed consistently based on the BIN on both Android and iOS.

steps.step: Repeat steps 5-6 with a MasterCard.
steps.result: BIN detection and offer display work correctly for MasterCard on both platforms.

steps.step: Test card scanning functionality if available.
steps.result: Card scanning works consistently on both platforms, auto-filling correct details.

steps.step: Attempt to submit payment with an invalid card number.
steps.result: Error handling for invalid card numbers is consistent across both platforms.

steps.step: Complete a successful card payment on both devices.
steps.result: Payment process completes successfully with consistent UI/UX on both platforms.

steps.step: Verify payment confirmation screen.
steps.result: Payment confirmation is displayed consistently on both Android and iOS.

TC-781.description: Validate that coupon application and related payment mode auto-selection behave consistently on Android and iOS platforms.
TC-781.preconditions:
1. The payment system is set up and functional on both Android and iOS platforms.
2. Test devices with the latest versions of Android and iOS are available.
3. A test user account is created and logged in on both platforms.
4. A cart with items meeting the minimum order value is prepared on both platforms.
5. Various test coupons (UPI-based, card-based) are available for testing.
6. Relevant payment apps (e.g., GPay for UPI) are installed on both devices.
TC-781.title: Coupon application behavior across platforms

attachments.name: Shiv - Card Payments & MOP Offers Test scenarios (1).pdf

steps.step: Launch the payment application on both Android and iOS devices.
steps.result: The application launches successfully on both platforms.

steps.step: Navigate to the coupon section on both devices.
steps.result: Coupon section is displayed consistently on both Android and iOS.

steps.step: Verify available coupons.
steps.result: The same set of eligible coupons is displayed on both platforms.

steps.step: Select a UPI-based coupon (e.g., GPay coupon) on both devices.
steps.result: Coupon is applied consistently, and payment mode is auto-selected to GPay on both platforms.

steps.step: Verify the auto-scroll to applied coupon popup.
steps.result: Page scrolls to Coupon & Offers section with popup consistently on both platforms.

steps.step: Remove the UPI-based coupon and select a card-based coupon (e.g., HDFC Visa coupon).
steps.result: Coupon is applied, and payment mode switches to HDFC Visa card (if saved) or prompts for card entry consistently on both platforms.

steps.step: Attempt to switch to an unsupported payment mode (e.g., COD) after applying the coupon.
steps.result: Error popup appears consistently on both platforms, preventing the switch.

steps.step: Apply a coupon with Minimum Order Value (MOV) requirement slightly above the current cart value.
steps.result: Coupon application is prevented with consistent error messaging on both platforms.

steps.step: Increase cart value to meet MOV and reapply the coupon.
steps.result: Coupon is successfully applied with consistent behavior on both Android and iOS.

steps.step: Proceed to payment with the applied coupon.
steps.result: Payment process initiates with the coupon applied consistently on both platforms.

TC-782.description: Verify that the payment method recommendation algorithm produces consistent results across Android and iOS platforms for the same user profile and cart contents.
TC-782.preconditions:
1. The payment system is set up and functional on both Android and iOS platforms.
2. Test devices with the latest versions of Android and iOS are available.
3. A test user account with a consistent profile is created and logged in on both platforms.
4. Identical cart contents meeting the minimum order value are prepared on both platforms.
5. The user has a history of using various payment methods on both platforms.
6. Multiple payment options (UPI, cards, wallets) are available and configured for the user.
TC-782.title: Payment method recommendation consistency

attachments.name: Shiv - Card Payments & MOP Offers Test scenarios (1).pdf

steps.step: Launch the payment application on both Android and iOS devices.
steps.result: The application launches successfully on both platforms.

steps.step: Navigate to the payment page on both devices.
steps.result: Payment page is displayed consistently on both Android and iOS.

steps.step: Observe the recommended payment methods.
steps.result: The order and selection of recommended payment methods are identical on both platforms.

steps.step: Verify that the top recommended method is consistent with the user's most frequent or recently used method.
steps.result: The top recommendation matches the user's usage pattern consistently on both platforms.

steps.step: Check if the recommendations factor in available offers and highest savings.
steps.result: Payment methods with the best offers are consistently highlighted or prioritized on both platforms.

steps.step: Modify the cart contents slightly (e.g., add/remove an item) on both devices.
steps.result: Recommendations update consistently on both platforms if the change affects available offers.

steps.step: Apply a coupon that is specific to a payment method (e.g., UPI-based coupon).
steps.result: The recommended payment methods update consistently to prioritize the coupon-specific method on both platforms.

steps.step: Remove all items from the cart and add a single low-value item.
steps.result: Recommendations adjust consistently on both platforms, possibly prioritizing methods with no minimum transaction value.

steps.step: Log out and log back in on both devices.
steps.result: Recommendations remain consistent with the user's profile after re-login on both platforms.

steps.step: Check the consistency of any additional information provided with recommendations (e.g., savings amount, cashback offers).
steps.result: Additional information displayed with recommendations is consistent and accurate on both Android and iOS.

TC-783.description: Ensure that payment status is correctly synchronized and displayed consistently when a user switches between Android and iOS devices during the payment process.
TC-783.preconditions:
1. The payment system is set up and functional on both Android and iOS platforms.
2. Test devices with the latest versions of Android and iOS are available.
3. A test user account is created and can be accessed on both platforms.
4. A cart with items meeting the minimum order value is prepared and synchronized across platforms.
5. The system supports cross-platform session management and data synchronization.
6. Various payment methods (UPI, cards, wallets) are configured and available for testing.
TC-783.title: Cross-platform payment status synchronization

attachments.name: Shiv - Card Payments & MOP Offers Test scenarios (1).pdf

steps.step: Launch the payment application on the Android device and log in.
steps.result: The application launches and the user is logged in successfully on Android.

steps.step: Navigate to the payment page and initiate a payment process on Android.
steps.result: Payment page is displayed and the process begins on the Android device.

steps.step: Select a payment method (e.g., UPI) and proceed to the next step without completing the payment.
steps.result: The selected payment method is recorded and the process is paused on Android.

steps.step: Launch the application on the iOS device and log in with the same account.
steps.result: The application launches and the user is logged in successfully on iOS.

steps.step: Navigate to the payment page on the iOS device.
steps.result: The payment page on iOS reflects the same cart contents and payment progress as on Android.

steps.step: Verify that the previously selected payment method (UPI) is still selected on iOS.
steps.result: The UPI payment method is shown as selected on the iOS device.

steps.step: Change the payment method to a card payment on iOS and proceed to the next step.
steps.result: The payment method is successfully changed to card payment on iOS.

steps.step: Switch back to the Android device and refresh the payment page.
steps.result: The Android device now shows the card payment method as selected, reflecting the change made on iOS.

steps.step: Complete the payment process on the Android device.
steps.result: The payment is successfully processed on Android.

steps.step: Immediately check the payment status on the iOS device.
steps.result: The iOS device displays the correct payment status (success or failure) without requiring a manual refresh.

steps.step: Verify order confirmation and payment details on both devices.
steps.result: Both Android and iOS devices show consistent and accurate order confirmation and payment details.

TC-774.description: Verify that sensitive payment data (card numbers, UPI IDs) are properly masked in system logs. Check various log levels to ensure no unmasked data is visible.
TC-774.preconditions:
1. The payment system is set up and operational.
2. System logging is enabled at various levels (e.g., debug, info, error).
3. Test data for different payment methods (cards, UPI) is available.
4. Access to system logs is available for verification.
TC-774.title: Sensitive data masking in logs

attachments.name: Shiv - Card Payments & MOP Offers Test scenarios (1).pdf

steps.step: Initiate a payment transaction using a credit card.
steps.result: The transaction is processed successfully.

steps.step: Check the system logs at all levels (debug, info, error).
steps.result: Credit card number is masked in all log entries, showing only the last 4 digits.

steps.step: Perform a UPI-based transaction.
steps.result: The UPI transaction is completed successfully.

steps.step: Examine the system logs for the UPI transaction.
steps.result: UPI ID is partially masked in all log entries, revealing only a safe portion of the ID.

steps.step: Attempt a failed transaction with an invalid card.
steps.result: The transaction fails, and an error is logged.

steps.step: Review the error logs for the failed transaction.
steps.result: The error log contains masked card information, not revealing the full card number.

steps.step: Check application crash logs (if any).
steps.result: No sensitive payment data is visible in crash logs.

steps.step: Review any third-party service logs integrated with the payment system.
steps.result: All logs from third-party services also mask sensitive payment data.

TC-775.description: Ensure that all payment data is encrypted during transmission between the app and the server. Use network monitoring tools to verify encryption.
TC-775.preconditions:
1. The payment system is set up and operational.
2. Network monitoring tools (e.g., Wireshark) are installed and configured.
3. Test environment allows for network traffic interception.
4. Test payment data for various methods (cards, UPI) is available.
TC-775.title: Encryption of payment data in transit

attachments.name: Shiv - Card Payments & MOP Offers Test scenarios (1).pdf

steps.step: Set up the network monitoring tool to capture traffic between the app and the server.
steps.result: The tool is ready to capture network traffic.

steps.step: Initiate a credit card payment transaction from the app.
steps.result: The transaction is processed successfully.

steps.step: Analyze the captured network traffic for the credit card transaction.
steps.result: All payment data in the network packets is encrypted and unreadable.

steps.step: Perform a UPI-based payment transaction.
steps.result: The UPI transaction is completed successfully.

steps.step: Examine the network traffic for the UPI transaction.
steps.result: UPI transaction data is encrypted in transit.

steps.step: Attempt to save a new payment method (e.g., a new credit card) in the app.
steps.result: The new payment method is saved successfully.

steps.step: Check the network traffic during the saving of the new payment method.
steps.result: The new payment method data is encrypted during transmission.

steps.step: Verify the encryption protocols used (e.g., TLS version).
steps.result: The system uses up-to-date, secure encryption protocols (e.g., TLS 1.2 or higher).

steps.step: Attempt to intercept and decrypt the encrypted traffic.
steps.result: Decryption attempts fail, confirming the strength of the encryption.

TC-776.description: Verify that sensitive payment information is masked on all UI screens, including saved cards and transaction history pages.
TC-776.preconditions:
1. The payment application is installed and operational.
2. Test user accounts with saved payment methods are available.
3. Access to all relevant UI screens (payment, saved cards, transaction history) is possible.
TC-776.title: UI masking of sensitive information

attachments.name: Shiv - Card Payments & MOP Offers Test scenarios (1).pdf

steps.step: Log into the application with a test user account.
steps.result: Successfully logged in to the application.

steps.step: Navigate to the saved cards section.
steps.result: Saved cards are displayed with masked numbers, showing only the last 4 digits.

steps.step: Attempt to view full card details for a saved card.
steps.result: Full card number remains masked, CVV is not displayed.

steps.step: Go to the transaction history page.
steps.result: Transaction history is displayed with masked payment method details.

steps.step: Select a specific transaction to view details.
steps.result: Detailed view shows masked card/UPI information.

steps.step: Initiate a new payment transaction.
steps.result: Payment screen opens with option to select payment method.

steps.step: Select a saved card for payment.
steps.result: Selected card is displayed with masked number.

steps.step: Enter a new card for payment.
steps.result: As card number is entered, it is dynamically masked except for the last 4 digits.

steps.step: Take a screenshot of the payment screen.
steps.result: Screenshot shows all sensitive data properly masked.

steps.step: Check UI elements for any tooltips or hover effects on payment fields.
steps.result: No tooltips or hover effects reveal unmasked sensitive data.

TC-777.description: Validate that saved payment methods (cards, UPI IDs) are stored securely and cannot be accessed through unauthorized means.
TC-777.preconditions:
1. The payment application is installed and operational.
2. Test user accounts with saved payment methods (cards, UPI IDs) are available.
3. Access to the application's data storage (if possible) is available.
4. Tools for attempting unauthorized access (e.g., rooted device, debugging tools) are prepared.
TC-777.title: Secure storage of payment methods

attachments.name: Shiv - Card Payments & MOP Offers Test scenarios (1).pdf

steps.step: Log into the application with a test user account.
steps.result: Successfully logged in to the application.

steps.step: Navigate to the saved payment methods section.
steps.result: Saved payment methods are displayed with masked information.

steps.step: Attempt to export or copy saved payment data from the UI.
steps.result: No option to export full payment data is available.

steps.step: Use a rooted device to access the application's local storage.
steps.result: Unable to find unencrypted payment data in local storage.

steps.step: Attempt to debug the application and access memory during a transaction.
steps.result: Sensitive payment data is not visible in memory dumps.

steps.step: Try to access the application's database directly (if applicable).
steps.result: Database is encrypted or does not contain plaintext payment data.

steps.step: Attempt to intercept API calls that fetch saved payment methods.
steps.result: API responses contain only masked or encrypted payment data.

steps.step: Use a man-in-the-middle attack to intercept communication between app and server.
steps.result: All intercepted data related to saved payment methods is encrypted.

steps.step: Attempt to access saved payment data through any export or backup features.
steps.result: Exported or backed-up data does not contain unencrypted payment information.

steps.step: Check if payment data is securely deleted when a user removes a saved payment method.
steps.result: Removed payment method data is not recoverable from the device.

TC-778.description: Ensure that error messages related to payment failures do not reveal sensitive information about the payment method or user account.
TC-778.preconditions:
1. The payment application is installed and operational.
2. Test user accounts with various payment methods are set up.
3. Test environment allows for simulating different payment failure scenarios.
TC-778.title: Privacy protection in error messages

attachments.name: Shiv - Card Payments & MOP Offers Test scenarios (1).pdf

steps.step: Log into the application with a test user account.
steps.result: Successfully logged in to the application.

steps.step: Initiate a payment with insufficient funds.
steps.result: Payment fails, and an error message is displayed.

steps.step: Verify the content of the error message for the failed payment.
steps.result: Error message does not reveal specific account balance or full card details.

steps.step: Attempt a payment with an expired card.
steps.result: Payment is declined, and an error is shown.

steps.step: Check the error message for the expired card scenario.
steps.result: Message indicates payment failure without specifying the card's expiration date.

steps.step: Try a payment with an invalid CVV.
steps.result: Transaction is rejected due to incorrect CVV.

steps.step: Examine the error message for the invalid CVV attempt.
steps.result: Error message does not mention 'CVV' specifically, uses a generic term like 'verification failed'.

steps.step: Perform a UPI transaction with an incorrect UPI PIN.
steps.result: UPI transaction fails due to incorrect PIN.

steps.step: Validate the error message for the failed UPI transaction.
steps.result: Message indicates authentication failure without mentioning 'PIN' or showing the UPI ID.

steps.step: Attempt a payment that triggers a fraud detection system.
steps.result: Payment is blocked by the fraud detection system.

steps.step: Review the error message for the blocked transaction.
steps.result: Message indicates the transaction cannot be processed without revealing specific fraud detection criteria.

TC-769.description: Verify that the system can handle 100+ users making payments simultaneously using a common card type (e.g., Visa). Ensure all transactions are processed correctly without deadlocks or double charges.
TC-769.preconditions:
1. The payment system is up and running.
2. A test environment is set up to simulate 100+ concurrent users.
3. A common card type (e.g., Visa) is available for testing.
4. The system has sufficient capacity to handle high-volume transactions.
5. Monitoring tools are in place to track system performance and transaction status.
6. A mechanism to verify individual transaction success and detect any double charges is available.
7. The test environment closely mimics the production environment in terms of infrastructure and configuration.
8. A large enough pool of unique order IDs is available for testing.
9. The system's database is in a known state before the test begins.
TC-769.title: Concurrent payment processing with common card

attachments.name: Shiv - Card Payments & MOP Offers Test scenarios (1).pdf

steps.step: Set up 100+ simulated users with unique identities but using the same card type (e.g., Visa).
steps.result: 100+ user profiles are created successfully with the same card type.

steps.step: Prepare a batch of 100+ unique orders with varying amounts for these users.
steps.result: 100+ unique orders are created in the system, each associated with a different user.

steps.step: Initiate simultaneous payment requests for all 100+ orders using the common card type.
steps.result: All payment requests are initiated concurrently without any system errors or crashes.

steps.step: Monitor the system's performance metrics during the concurrent transactions.
steps.result: The system maintains stability, with response times and resource utilization within acceptable limits.

steps.step: Allow all transactions to complete.
steps.result: All transactions are processed without the system hanging or crashing.

steps.step: Verify the status of each transaction in the system.
steps.result: Each transaction shows a clear status (success or failure) without any pending or unclear states.

steps.step: Check for any error logs or exception reports generated during the process.
steps.result: No critical errors or unexpected exceptions are logged. Any minor issues are documented for further investigation.

steps.step: Confirm the number of successful transactions.
steps.result: The number of successful transactions matches the number of initiated transactions, allowing for a small acceptable failure rate due to normal processing issues (e.g., insufficient funds).

steps.step: Verify each user's order status and payment confirmation.
steps.result: Each user receives the correct order status and payment confirmation for their transaction.

steps.step: Check the payment gateway logs for any signs of transaction duplication or deadlocks.
steps.result: No signs of transaction duplication or deadlocks are found in the payment gateway logs.

steps.step: Verify the financial records to ensure no double charges occurred.
steps.result: Financial records show exactly one charge per successful transaction, with no double charges.

steps.step: Compare the total amount charged across all transactions with the sum of individual order amounts.
steps.result: The total amount charged matches the sum of individual order amounts for successful transactions.

steps.step: Analyze the transaction processing times.
steps.result: Transaction processing times are within acceptable limits, showing no significant slowdown due to concurrent processing.

steps.step: Check if any transactions were left in a 'pending' state.
steps.result: No transactions are left in a 'pending' state after a reasonable time period (e.g., 5 minutes after initiation).

steps.step: Verify that the system's database maintains consistency after the bulk transaction processing.
steps.result: Database consistency is maintained, with all transaction records properly stored and no data corruption.

TC-764.description: Verify that the system prevents payment processing when a user attempts to pay with a card scheme (e.g., Visa) that doesn't match the selected offer or coupon (e.g., MasterCard-specific offer). This test ensures strict enforcement of card scheme restrictions.

steps.result: The system should allow the payment to be processed successfully.

steps.result: System prevents the completion of the transaction and displays a clear error message about the coupon and payment method mismatch

steps.step: Verify the order summary
steps.result: The discount from the MasterCard coupon is not applied to the order total

TC-756.description: Ensure that when multiple payment methods are eligible for a coupon, the system correctly applies the discount regardless of which eligible method is chosen. This test checks the system's flexibility in handling various valid payment scenarios.
TC-756.preconditions:
1. User has an active account in the payment system
2. Multiple payment methods are available, including various UPI apps (GPay, PhonePe) and cards
3. A coupon applicable to multiple payment methods (e.g., all UPI payments) is available in the system
4. User has added items to the cart with a total amount that meets the minimum order value for the coupon
TC-756.title: Handle multiple eligible payment methods

attachments.name: Shiv - Card Payments & MOP Offers Test scenarios (1).pdf

steps.step: Navigate to the cart page
steps.result: Cart page is displayed with correct items and total amount

steps.step: Click on 'Apply Coupon' in the Cart
steps.result: System scrolls to Coupon & Offers section with a popup

steps.step: Select and apply the multi-method eligible coupon (e.g., UPI coupon)
steps.result: Coupon is applied successfully, and the payment mode is set to one of the eligible UPI methods

steps.step: Proceed to checkout
steps.result: Checkout page is displayed with the applied coupon and a selected UPI payment method

steps.step: Change the payment method to another eligible UPI app (e.g., from GPay to PhonePe)
steps.result: System allows the change and maintains the applied coupon

steps.step: Complete the payment using the chosen UPI method
steps.result: Payment is processed successfully

steps.step: Verify the final order amount
steps.result: The correct discount as per the UPI coupon is applied to the order total

steps.step: Repeat the process with another eligible payment method
steps.result: The system consistently applies the discount for all eligible payment methods

TC-757.description: Verify that in cases of split or partial payments, the coupon is only applied if the qualifying amount is paid using the matching payment method. This test ensures correct handling of complex payment scenarios while maintaining coupon validity rules.
TC-757.preconditions:
1. User has an active account in the payment system
2. Multiple payment methods are available, including a specific card (e.g., HDFC card) and other methods
3. A coupon specific to HDFC card payments is available in the system
4. User has added items to the cart with a total amount that exceeds the minimum order value for the coupon and allows for split payment
TC-757.title: Coupon validation for partial payments

attachments.name: Shiv - Card Payments & MOP Offers Test scenarios (1).pdf

steps.step: Navigate to the cart page
steps.result: Cart page is displayed with correct items and total amount

steps.step: Click on 'Apply Coupon' in the Cart
steps.result: System scrolls to Coupon & Offers section with a popup

steps.step: Select and apply the HDFC card-specific coupon
steps.result: Coupon is applied successfully, and the payment mode is automatically set to HDFC card

steps.step: Proceed to checkout
steps.result: Checkout page is displayed with the applied coupon and selected payment method (HDFC card)

steps.step: Initiate a split payment, using HDFC card for an amount less than the coupon's qualifying amount
steps.result: System warns that the coupon may not be applicable if the qualifying amount is not met with the HDFC card

steps.step: Complete the split payment using HDFC card for less than the qualifying amount and another method for the remainder
steps.result: Payment is processed, but the coupon discount is not applied

steps.step: Verify the final order amount
steps.result: The order total does not include the HDFC card coupon discount

steps.step: Repeat the process, but use the HDFC card for the full qualifying amount in the split payment
steps.result: Payment is processed successfully, and the coupon discount is applied to the order total

TC-758.description: Confirm that the system performs real-time validation of the coupon against the chosen payment method during the payment processing stage, accounting for any last-minute changes. This test validates the system's ability to ensure coupon eligibility up to the point of transaction completion.
TC-758.preconditions:
1. User has an active account in the payment system
2. Multiple payment methods are available
3. Various coupons for different payment methods are available in the system
4. User has added items to the cart with a total amount that meets the minimum order value for available coupons
TC-758.title: Real-time coupon validation during payment processing

attachments.name: Shiv - Card Payments & MOP Offers Test scenarios (1).pdf

steps.step: Navigate to the cart page
steps.result: Cart page is displayed with correct items and total amount

steps.step: Click on 'Apply Coupon' in the Cart
steps.result: System scrolls to Coupon & Offers section with a popup

steps.step: Proceed to checkout

steps.step: Change the payment method to a different card type (e.g., MasterCard) just before initiating the payment

TC-749.description: Verify that the system prevents a user from applying a coupon that has reached its maximum redemption limit. This test ensures strict enforcement of coupon usage restrictions to maintain offer integrity.
TC-749.preconditions:
1. The user has a valid account in the payment system.
2. A coupon with a specific redemption limit is available in the system.
3. The coupon is near or at its maximum redemption limit.
4. The user has a cart with items that meet the coupon's eligibility criteria (e.g., minimum order value).
TC-749.title: Prevent exceeding coupon usage limit

attachments.name: Shiv - Card Payments & MOP Offers Test scenarios (1).pdf

steps.step: Navigate to the checkout page with eligible items in the cart.
steps.result: The checkout page is displayed with the cart summary and payment options.

steps.step: Attempt to apply the coupon that has reached its maximum redemption limit.
steps.result: The system should not apply the coupon and should prevent its usage.

steps.step: Verify the cart total and discounts.
steps.result: The cart total should remain unchanged, and no discount from the coupon should be applied.

steps.step: Attempt to proceed with the payment.
steps.result: The payment process should continue without the coupon applied.

steps.step: Check the coupon's status in the user's account or coupon section.
steps.result: The coupon should be marked as unavailable or expired due to reaching the redemption limit.

TC-750.description: Confirm that an appropriate error message is displayed when a user attempts to apply a coupon that has reached its redemption limit. This test validates clear communication of coupon ineligibility to users.
TC-750.preconditions:
1. The user has a valid account in the payment system.
2. A coupon with a specific redemption limit is available in the system.
3. The coupon has reached its maximum redemption limit.
4. The user has a cart with items that would otherwise be eligible for the coupon.
TC-750.title: Display error for limit-reached coupon

attachments.name: Shiv - Card Payments & MOP Offers Test scenarios (1).pdf

steps.step: Navigate to the checkout page with eligible items in the cart.
steps.result: The checkout page is displayed with the cart summary and payment options.

steps.step: Attempt to apply the coupon that has reached its redemption limit.
steps.result: The system should not apply the coupon and should display an error message.

steps.step: Verify the error message content.
steps.result: The error message should clearly state that the coupon has reached its usage limit and cannot be applied.

steps.step: Check if the error message provides any alternative suggestions or actions.
steps.result: The message may suggest other available coupons or promotions, if applicable.

steps.step: Attempt to proceed with the payment after the error message.
steps.result: The payment process should allow continuation without the coupon applied.

steps.step: Verify the visibility and readability of the error message on different devices and screen sizes.
steps.result: The error message should be clearly visible and readable on various devices and screen sizes.

TC-751.description: Ensure that the system correctly enforces per-user coupon redemption limits, preventing a single user from exceeding their allocated usage of a specific coupon. This test checks the system's ability to track and limit individual user coupon usage.
TC-751.preconditions:
1. The user has a valid account in the payment system.
2. A coupon with a specific per-user redemption limit is available in the system.
3. The user has not yet reached their personal limit for the coupon.
4. The user has a cart with items that meet the coupon's eligibility criteria.
TC-751.title: Handle per-user coupon limits

attachments.name: Shiv - Card Payments & MOP Offers Test scenarios (1).pdf

steps.step: Apply the coupon to an eligible order and complete the purchase.
steps.result: The coupon should be successfully applied and the order completed.

steps.step: Verify the user's coupon usage count in their account.
steps.result: The system should show that the user has used the coupon once.

steps.step: Create a new cart with eligible items and attempt to apply the same coupon again.
steps.result: The coupon should be applied successfully if the user hasn't reached their limit.

steps.step: Repeat steps 1-3 until reaching one use before the per-user limit.
steps.result: The coupon should be applied successfully in each attempt until reaching one below the limit.

steps.step: Attempt to apply the coupon one more time, exceeding the per-user limit.
steps.result: The system should prevent the coupon from being applied and display an error message about reaching the personal usage limit.

steps.step: Verify the error message content for exceeding the per-user limit.
steps.result: The message should clearly state that the user has reached their personal limit for this coupon.

steps.step: Check the coupon's status in the user's account.
steps.result: The coupon should be marked as fully redeemed or unavailable for this user.

TC-752.description: Verify that the coupon redemption count updates in real-time after a successful order, ensuring that subsequent attempts to use the coupon reflect the updated limit. This test validates the system's ability to maintain accurate, up-to-date coupon usage data.
TC-752.preconditions:
1. The user has a valid account in the payment system.
2. A coupon with a specific redemption limit is available in the system.
3. The coupon is close to reaching its maximum redemption limit.
4. Multiple users have the coupon available to them.
TC-752.title: Real-time limit update after successful order

attachments.name: Shiv - Card Payments & MOP Offers Test scenarios (1).pdf

steps.step: User A applies the coupon to an eligible order and completes the purchase.
steps.result: The coupon is successfully applied and the order is completed.

steps.step: Immediately after User A's purchase, User B attempts to apply the same coupon to their order.
steps.result: The system should reflect the updated coupon count and either allow or deny the coupon based on the remaining limit.

steps.step: If User B's attempt is successful, repeat with User C until the coupon limit is reached.
steps.result: Each successful use should decrease the available coupon count. The final attempt should be denied due to reaching the limit.

steps.step: Verify the coupon status in the system immediately after the limit is reached.
steps.result: The coupon should be marked as fully redeemed or unavailable system-wide.

steps.step: Attempt to apply the coupon to a new order within 1 minute of the limit being reached.
steps.result: The system should deny the coupon application and display an error message about the coupon being fully redeemed.

steps.step: Check the coupon's redemption count in the admin or reporting interface.
steps.result: The redemption count should accurately reflect the number of successful applications, matching the coupon's limit.

TC-753.description: Confirm that coupon redemption limits are enforced consistently across multiple devices or sessions for the same user account. This test ensures that users cannot circumvent usage limits by using different devices or browsers.
TC-753.preconditions:
1. The user has a valid account in the payment system.
2. The user has access to multiple devices or browsers (e.g., smartphone app, desktop browser, tablet).
3. A coupon with a specific per-user redemption limit is available in the system.
4. The user has not yet used the coupon on any device.
TC-753.title: Limit enforcement across multiple devices

attachments.name: Shiv - Card Payments & MOP Offers Test scenarios (1).pdf

steps.step: User logs into their account on Device A (e.g., smartphone app) and applies the coupon to an eligible order.
steps.result: The coupon is successfully applied and the order is completed on Device A.

steps.step: User logs into their account on Device B (e.g., desktop browser) and attempts to apply the same coupon to a new eligible order.
steps.result: The system should recognize the previous usage and either allow or deny the coupon based on the remaining per-user limit.

steps.step: If the coupon is still valid, complete the order on Device B.
steps.result: The order should be completed with the coupon applied, and the usage count should be updated across all devices.

steps.step: User logs into their account on Device C (e.g., tablet) and checks the coupon status.
steps.result: The coupon status and remaining uses should be consistent with the actions performed on Devices A and B.

steps.step: Attempt to apply the coupon on Device C to a new eligible order.
steps.result: The system should enforce the per-user limit consistently, regardless of the device being used.

steps.step: If the per-user limit has been reached, verify that the coupon cannot be applied on any device.
steps.result: All devices should show the coupon as fully redeemed or unavailable for this user.

steps.step: Log out and log back in on each device, then check the coupon status.
steps.result: The coupon status and usage count should remain consistent across all devices, even after re-authentication.

TC-739.description: Verify that when a valid card BIN is entered, the system dynamically updates the available offers based on the card's issuer and type. This test ensures real-time offer personalization based on card information.

1. The payment system is up and running.
2. The user is on the payment page.
3. A variety of card-based offers are available in the system for different issuers and card types.
4. The system has a database of valid BIN numbers and their corresponding issuer and card type information.

attachments.name: Shiv - Card Payments & MOP Offers Test scenarios (1).pdf

steps.step: Enter the first 6 digits (BIN) of a valid card number
steps.result: The system should recognize the BIN and identify the card issuer and type

steps.step: Observe the offers displayed on the page
steps.result: The offers should update dynamically to show relevant offers based on the entered BIN

steps.step: Verify that the displayed offers match the card issuer
steps.result: Offers specific to the identified card issuer should be prominently displayed

steps.step: Verify that the displayed offers match the card type
steps.result: Offers specific to the identified card type (e.g., Visa, MasterCard) should be shown

steps.step: Check for any general offers not specific to the card
steps.result: General offers applicable to all cards should still be visible

steps.step: Verify the order of displayed offers
steps.result: Issuer and card type specific offers should be prioritized over general offers

2. The user is on the payment page.
3. Multiple issuer-specific offers are available in the system (e.g., for HDFC, ICICI, SBI, etc.).

attachments.name: Shiv - Card Payments & MOP Offers Test scenarios (1).pdf

steps.result: The system should recognize the BIN as belonging to HDFC

TC-741.description: Ensure that offers specific to the card type (e.g., Visa, MasterCard, RuPay) are correctly displayed when a card BIN of that type is entered. This test checks the system's capability to filter offers based on card network.

2. The user is on the payment page.
3. Various card type-specific offers (Visa, MasterCard, RuPay, etc.) are available in the system.

steps.result: The system should identify the entered BIN as a Visa card

TC-742.description: Verify that the system handles unknown or newly introduced BINs gracefully, either by displaying a default set of offers or by showing a message indicating no specific offers are available. This test ensures robustness in handling various BIN scenarios.
TC-742.preconditions:
1. The payment system is operational.
2. The user is on the payment page.
3. A set of default offers is available in the system.
4. The system has a mechanism to identify unknown or new BINs.
TC-742.title: Handle unknown or new BINs gracefully

attachments.name: Shiv - Card Payments & MOP Offers Test scenarios (1).pdf

steps.step: Enter a BIN (first 6 digits) that is not in the system's database
steps.result: The system should recognize that the BIN is unknown or new

steps.step: Observe the offers displayed on the page

steps.result: The system should update and show relevant offers for the valid BIN

2. The user is on the payment page.
3. Multiple offers for different card issuers and types are available in the system.
4. The system has an up-to-date database of BIN numbers and their corresponding details.

TC-734.description: Verify that an error popup appears when switching to an unsupported payment mode (e.g., COD) after applying a specific payment method coupon (e.g., card-based). This test ensures clear communication of payment method incompatibility to users.
