4. The checkout page is open with the coupon applied and the corresponding card payment mode selected
TC-734.title: Error on switching to incompatible payment mode

attachments.name: Shiv - Card Payments & MOP Offers Test scenarios (1).pdf

steps.step: Attempt to switch the payment mode to Cash on Delivery (COD)
steps.result: An error popup should appear, indicating that the selected payment mode is incompatible with the applied coupon

steps.step: Verify the content of the error message
steps.result: The error message should clearly state that COD is not supported with the current coupon and suggest keeping the card payment or removing the coupon

steps.step: Attempt to proceed with the payment
steps.result: The system should prevent proceeding with the payment until the conflict is resolved

steps.step: Close the error popup
steps.result: The payment mode should remain on the previously selected card payment option

TC-735.description: Confirm that incompatible payment modes are either disabled or not displayed in the payment options after applying a specific coupon. This test validates the system's ability to proactively prevent users from selecting incompatible payment methods.
TC-735.preconditions:
1. User is logged into the application
2. User has added items to the cart
3. The checkout page is open
TC-735.title: Prevent selection of incompatible payment modes

attachments.name: Shiv - Card Payments & MOP Offers Test scenarios (1).pdf

steps.step: Apply a UPI-based coupon (e.g., GPay coupon)
steps.result: The coupon should be successfully applied and the payment mode should automatically switch to UPI

steps.step: Check the available payment options
steps.result: Non-UPI payment options should either be disabled or not displayed in the list of payment methods

steps.step: Attempt to select a card payment option
steps.result: The option should be unselectable if visible, or not present in the list

steps.step: Attempt to select Cash on Delivery
steps.result: The COD option should be unselectable if visible, or not present in the list

steps.step: Verify that UPI payment options are selectable
steps.result: All UPI payment options should be enabled and selectable

TC-736.description: Ensure that when a user forcibly selects an incompatible payment mode, the system either removes the applied coupon or prompts the user to confirm the action. This test checks the system's handling of conflicts between coupons and payment methods.
TC-736.preconditions:
1. User is logged into the application
2. User has added items to the cart
3. A payment method-specific coupon (e.g., HDFC Visa coupon) has been applied
4. The checkout page is open with the coupon applied
TC-736.title: Coupon removal on incompatible payment mode selection

attachments.name: Shiv - Card Payments & MOP Offers Test scenarios (1).pdf

steps.step: Attempt to change the payment mode to an incompatible option (e.g., COD or UPI)
steps.result: A warning popup should appear, informing the user that changing the payment mode will remove the applied coupon

steps.step: Verify the content of the warning message
steps.result: The message should clearly state that proceeding will remove the coupon and ask for confirmation

steps.step: Confirm the action to change the payment mode
steps.result:
1. The payment mode should change to the selected incompatible option
2. The previously applied coupon should be removed from the order

steps.step: Check the order summary
steps.result: The order total should be updated to reflect the removal of the coupon discount

steps.step: Verify the availability of coupons
steps.result: The removed coupon should now be available for reapplication if the user switches back to a compatible payment mode

TC-737.description: Verify that when multiple coupons are applied, the system correctly identifies and allows only compatible payment modes for all active coupons. This test ensures proper handling of complex scenarios with multiple discount offers.
TC-737.preconditions:
1. User is logged into the application
2. User has added items to the cart meeting the minimum order value for multiple coupons
3. The checkout page is open
TC-737.title: Multiple coupon compatibility check

attachments.name: Shiv - Card Payments & MOP Offers Test scenarios (1).pdf

steps.step: Apply a card-based coupon (e.g., HDFC Visa 10% off)
steps.result: The coupon should be successfully applied and the payment mode should switch to card payment

steps.step: Apply an additional coupon compatible with card payments (e.g., Festive Season 5% off)
steps.result: Both coupons should be successfully applied

steps.step: Check the available payment options
steps.result: Only card payment options should be enabled and selectable

steps.step: Attempt to apply a UPI-based coupon
steps.result: The system should prevent the application of the UPI coupon or display an error message about incompatibility

steps.step: Remove one of the card-based coupons
steps.result: The removed coupon should be cleared, but the payment options should still be restricted to card payments due to the remaining coupon

steps.step: Remove all card-based coupons
steps.result: All payment options should become available again

TC-738.description: Confirm that payment mode restrictions are lifted when a coupon expires or becomes invalid during the checkout process. This test validates the system's ability to handle dynamic changes in coupon validity and payment method compatibility.
TC-738.preconditions:
1. User is logged into the application
2. User has added items to the cart
3. A time-sensitive, payment method-specific coupon (e.g., Flash Sale HDFC 20% off, valid for 5 minutes) has been applied
4. The checkout page is open with the coupon applied and the corresponding payment method selected
TC-738.title: Payment mode compatibility after coupon expiry

attachments.name: Shiv - Card Payments & MOP Offers Test scenarios (1).pdf

steps.step: Verify that only the compatible payment mode (HDFC card) is selectable
steps.result: Other payment options should be disabled or hidden

steps.step: Wait for the coupon to expire (simulate if necessary)
steps.result: The system should detect the coupon expiry

steps.step: Check the order summary after coupon expiry
steps.result: The expired coupon should be automatically removed and the order total updated

steps.step: Verify the available payment options after coupon expiry
steps.result: All payment options should now be enabled and selectable

steps.step: Attempt to reapply the expired coupon
steps.result: The system should prevent reapplication of the expired coupon and display an appropriate error message

steps.step: Select a previously incompatible payment mode (e.g., UPI)
steps.result: The user should be able to select and proceed with the UPI payment option

TC-729.description: Verify that a coupon with a minimum order value (MOV) requirement is disabled when the cart value is below the specified MOV. For example, a ₹400 MOV coupon should be inactive for a ₹300 cart. This test ensures proper enforcement of MOV restrictions.
TC-729.preconditions:
1. The user is logged into the application.
2. A coupon with a minimum order value (MOV) of ₹400 is available in the system.
3. The user's cart contains items with a total value of ₹300.
TC-729.title: Disable coupon below minimum order value

attachments.name: Shiv - Card Payments & MOP Offers Test scenarios (1).pdf

steps.step: Navigate to the cart page.
steps.result: The cart page is displayed showing items with a total value of ₹300.

steps.step: Attempt to apply the ₹400 MOV coupon to the cart.
steps.result: The coupon is displayed as inactive or disabled.

steps.step: Check if there's any visual indication that the coupon is not applicable.
steps.result: There should be a clear visual cue (e.g., grayed out, disabled button) indicating that the coupon cannot be applied.

steps.step: Attempt to force-apply the coupon (if possible in the UI).
steps.result: The system should prevent the coupon from being applied and display an error message explaining the MOV requirement.

steps.step: Verify the cart total and any discount applied.
steps.result: The cart total should remain ₹300 with no discount applied.

TC-730.description: Confirm that a coupon becomes eligible and can be applied when the cart value exactly matches the minimum order value requirement. This test checks the system's precision in MOV calculations and coupon activation.
TC-730.preconditions:
1. The user is logged into the application.
2. A coupon with a minimum order value (MOV) of ₹400 is available in the system.
3. The user's cart initially contains items with a total value slightly below ₹400.
TC-730.title: Enable coupon at exact minimum order value

attachments.name: Shiv - Card Payments & MOP Offers Test scenarios (1).pdf

steps.step: Navigate to the cart page.
steps.result: The cart page is displayed showing items with a total value slightly below ₹400.

steps.step: Add an item to the cart that brings the total value to exactly ₹400.
steps.result: The cart updates and shows a total of exactly ₹400.

steps.step: Check the status of the ₹400 MOV coupon.
steps.result: The coupon should become active and eligible for application.

steps.step: Apply the ₹400 MOV coupon to the cart.
steps.result: The coupon is successfully applied to the cart.

steps.step: Verify the cart total and applied discount.
steps.result: The cart should show the original ₹400 total, the applied coupon, and the new discounted total.

TC-731.description: Ensure that coupon eligibility updates dynamically as items are added to or removed from the cart, affecting the total order value. This test validates real-time coupon status updates based on cart modifications.
TC-731.preconditions:
1. The user is logged into the application.
2. Multiple coupons with different MOVs (e.g., ₹300, ₹500, ₹700) are available in the system.
3. The user's cart initially contains items with a total value of ₹200.
TC-731.title: Dynamic coupon status update with cart changes

attachments.name: Shiv - Card Payments & MOP Offers Test scenarios (1).pdf

steps.step: Navigate to the cart page and observe the coupon section.
steps.result: All coupons should be displayed but those with MOVs above ₹200 should be inactive.

steps.step: Add items to increase the cart value to ₹350.
steps.result: The ₹300 MOV coupon becomes active, while ₹500 and ₹700 coupons remain inactive.

steps.step: Remove an item to decrease the cart value to ₹280.
steps.result: All coupons should now be inactive as the cart value is below all MOVs.

steps.step: Add items to increase the cart value to ₹600.
steps.result: The ₹300 and ₹500 MOV coupons become active, while the ₹700 coupon remains inactive.

steps.step: Increase the cart value to ₹800.
steps.result: All coupons (₹300, ₹500, ₹700 MOV) should now be active and eligible for application.

TC-732.description: Verify that when multiple coupons with different minimum order values are available, only those meeting the current cart value are enabled. This test checks the system's ability to handle and display multiple coupons with varying eligibility criteria.
TC-732.preconditions:
1. The user is logged into the application.
2. Multiple coupons with different MOVs are available: Coupon A (₹300 MOV), Coupon B (₹500 MOV), Coupon C (₹700 MOV).
3. The user's cart initially contains items with a total value of ₹400.
TC-732.title: Multiple coupon eligibility with different MOVs

attachments.name: Shiv - Card Payments & MOP Offers Test scenarios (1).pdf

steps.step: Navigate to the cart or coupon page.
steps.result: All coupons should be visible, but only Coupon A (₹300 MOV) should be enabled.

steps.step: Attempt to apply Coupon B (₹500 MOV).
steps.result: The system should prevent application and display an error message about insufficient cart value.

steps.step: Add items to increase the cart value to ₹600.
steps.result: Coupons A and B should now be enabled, while Coupon C remains disabled.

steps.step: Apply Coupon B (₹500 MOV) to the cart.
steps.result: Coupon B is successfully applied, and the discount is reflected in the cart total.

steps.step: Increase the cart value to ₹800.
steps.result: All coupons (A, B, C) should now be enabled, but Coupon B remains applied.

TC-733.description: Confirm that an applied coupon is automatically removed if the cart value drops below the minimum order value, and reapplied if the value increases above the MOV again. This test ensures consistent coupon application based on dynamic cart values.
TC-733.preconditions:
1. The user is logged into the application.
2. A coupon with a minimum order value (MOV) of ₹500 is available in the system.
3. The user's cart initially contains items with a total value of ₹600.
TC-733.title: Coupon application and removal based on MOV

attachments.name: Shiv - Card Payments & MOP Offers Test scenarios (1).pdf

steps.step: Navigate to the cart page and apply the ₹500 MOV coupon.
steps.result: The coupon is successfully applied and the discount is reflected in the cart total.

steps.step: Remove items from the cart to reduce the total value to ₹450.
steps.result: The coupon should be automatically removed, and the cart total should update to reflect no discount.

steps.step: Check for any system notification about coupon removal.
steps.result: A notification or message should inform the user that the coupon was removed due to insufficient cart value.

steps.step: Add items back to the cart to increase the total value to ₹550.
steps.result: The ₹500 MOV coupon should be automatically reapplied, and the discount should be reflected in the cart total.

steps.step: Verify the final cart total and applied discount.
steps.result: The cart should show the ₹550 total, the reapplied coupon, and the new discounted total.

TC-724.description: Verify that when a card-specific coupon (e.g., HDFC Visa) is applied, the system automatically selects the matching saved card as the payment mode. This test ensures seamless integration between coupon application and payment method selection.
TC-724.preconditions:
1. User is logged in to the payment system.
2. User has at least one saved HDFC Visa card in their account.
3. User has added items to the cart and proceeded to the checkout page.
4. A card-specific coupon (e.g., HDFC Visa) is available for application.
TC-724.title: Auto-select saved card for matching coupon

attachments.name: Shiv - Card Payments & MOP Offers Test scenarios (1).pdf

steps.step: Navigate to the coupon section on the checkout page.
steps.result: Coupon section is displayed with available coupons.

steps.step: Apply the HDFC Visa specific coupon.
steps.result: Coupon is successfully applied and reflected in the order summary.

steps.step: Observe the payment mode section.
steps.result: The system automatically selects the saved HDFC Visa card as the payment mode.

steps.step: Verify the selected payment mode details.
steps.result: The selected card matches the coupon criteria (HDFC Visa).

steps.step: Attempt to proceed with the payment.
steps.result: The system allows proceeding with the payment using the auto-selected HDFC Visa card.

TC-725.description: Confirm that when a card-specific coupon is applied and no matching card is saved, the system prompts the user to enter the card details. This test validates the system's ability to guide users towards completing the payment with the correct card type.
TC-725.preconditions:
1. User is logged in to the payment system.
2. User does not have any saved cards matching the coupon criteria.
3. User has added items to the cart and proceeded to the checkout page.
4. A card-specific coupon (e.g., HDFC Visa) is available for application.
TC-725.title: Prompt card entry for unsaved matching card

attachments.name: Shiv - Card Payments & MOP Offers Test scenarios (1).pdf

steps.step: Navigate to the coupon section on the checkout page.
steps.result: Coupon section is displayed with available coupons.

steps.step: Apply the HDFC Visa specific coupon.
steps.result: Coupon is successfully applied and reflected in the order summary.

steps.step: Observe the payment mode section.
steps.result: The system prompts the user to enter new card details.

steps.step: Click on the prompt to enter new card details.
steps.result: A form to enter new card details is displayed.

steps.step: Enter valid HDFC Visa card details.
steps.result: The system accepts the card details and sets it as the selected payment mode.

steps.step: Attempt to proceed with the payment.
steps.result: The system allows proceeding with the payment using the newly entered HDFC Visa card.

TC-726.description: Ensure that when multiple saved cards match the applied coupon (e.g., multiple HDFC Visa cards), the system either selects the most recently used card or prompts the user to choose. This test checks the system's behavior in scenarios with multiple eligible payment methods.
TC-726.preconditions:
1. User is logged in to the payment system.
2. User has multiple saved HDFC Visa cards in their account.
3. User has added items to the cart and proceeded to the checkout page.
4. A card-specific coupon (e.g., HDFC Visa) is available for application.
TC-726.title: Handle multiple saved cards for coupon

attachments.name: Shiv - Card Payments & MOP Offers Test scenarios (1).pdf

steps.step: Navigate to the coupon section on the checkout page.
steps.result: Coupon section is displayed with available coupons.

steps.step: Apply the HDFC Visa specific coupon.
steps.result: Coupon is successfully applied and reflected in the order summary.

steps.step: Observe the payment mode section.
steps.result: The system either selects the most recently used HDFC Visa card or prompts the user to choose from eligible cards.

steps.step: If prompted, select one of the eligible HDFC Visa cards.
steps.result: The selected card is set as the payment mode.

steps.step: Verify the selected payment mode details.
steps.result: The selected card matches the coupon criteria (HDFC Visa).

steps.step: Attempt to proceed with the payment.
steps.result: The system allows proceeding with the payment using the selected HDFC Visa card.

TC-727.description: Verify that when a card-specific coupon is removed, the auto-selected payment mode reverts to the previous selection or default state. This test ensures that the payment mode selection remains consistent with user actions and coupon status.
TC-727.preconditions:
1. User is logged in to the payment system.
2. User has at least one saved card in their account.
3. User has added items to the cart and proceeded to the checkout page.
4. A card-specific coupon (e.g., HDFC Visa) has been applied and auto-selected the payment mode.
TC-727.title: Revert auto-selection on coupon removal

attachments.name: Shiv - Card Payments & MOP Offers Test scenarios (1).pdf

steps.step: Verify that a card-specific coupon is applied and the corresponding payment mode is auto-selected.
steps.result: The coupon is applied and the matching card is selected as the payment mode.

steps.step: Navigate to the coupon section on the checkout page.
steps.result: Coupon section is displayed with the applied coupon.

steps.step: Remove the applied card-specific coupon.
steps.result: The coupon is successfully removed and no longer reflected in the order summary.

steps.step: Observe the payment mode section.
steps.result: The payment mode reverts to the previous selection or the default state.

steps.step: Verify the current payment mode.
steps.result: The payment mode is either the previously selected mode before coupon application or the default payment mode.

steps.step: Attempt to proceed with the payment.
steps.result: The system allows proceeding with the payment using the reverted payment mode.

TC-728.description: Confirm that when a user enters a new card matching an applied coupon, the system automatically selects this new card as the payment mode. This test validates the dynamic update of payment mode based on user input during the checkout process.
TC-728.preconditions:
1. User is logged in to the payment system.
2. User has added items to the cart and proceeded to the checkout page.
3. A card-specific coupon (e.g., HDFC Visa) is available and has been applied.
4. The system is prompting for new card entry as no matching saved card is available.
TC-728.title: Auto-selection with new card entry

attachments.name: Shiv - Card Payments & MOP Offers Test scenarios (1).pdf

steps.step: Verify that a card-specific coupon is applied.
steps.result: The coupon is applied and reflected in the order summary.

steps.step: Observe the payment mode section prompting for new card entry.
steps.result: A form or option to enter new card details is displayed.

steps.step: Enter new card details matching the coupon criteria (e.g., HDFC Visa card details).
steps.result: The system accepts the new card details.

steps.step: Complete the card entry process.
steps.result: The new card is successfully added.

steps.step: Observe the payment mode section after adding the new card.
steps.result: The system automatically selects the newly entered card as the payment mode.

steps.step: Verify the selected payment mode details.
steps.result: The selected card matches the coupon criteria (HDFC Visa).

steps.step: Attempt to proceed with the payment.
steps.result: The system allows proceeding with the payment using the newly added and auto-selected card.

TC-719.description: Verify that when only Google Pay (GPay) is installed on the device, only GPay-related coupons are displayed in the coupon section. This test ensures the system correctly detects installed UPI apps and filters coupons accordingly.
TC-719.preconditions:
1. The test device has only Google Pay (GPay) installed as a UPI app.
2. The payment system is properly configured to detect installed UPI apps.
3. There are active GPay-related coupons available in the system.
4. The user is logged into the application and has navigated to the coupon section.
TC-719.title: Display GPay coupons when only GPay is installed

attachments.name: Shiv - Card Payments & MOP Offers Test scenarios (1).pdf

steps.step: Open the application and navigate to the coupon section.
steps.result: The coupon section is displayed.

steps.step: Observe the list of available coupons.
steps.result: Only GPay-related coupons are visible in the coupon section.

steps.step: Verify that no coupons for other UPI apps (e.g., PhonePe) are displayed.
steps.result: No coupons for other UPI apps are present in the list.

steps.step: Select a GPay coupon and proceed to the payment screen.
steps.result: The selected GPay coupon is applied, and the payment mode is automatically set to GPay.

steps.step: Attempt to search for coupons related to other UPI apps.
steps.result: No results are found for coupons related to other UPI apps.

TC-720.description: Confirm that no UPI-based coupons are displayed in the coupon section when no supported UPI apps (like GPay or PhonePe) are installed on the device. This test validates the system's ability to handle scenarios where UPI payments are not possible.
TC-720.preconditions:
1. The test device has no UPI apps installed (GPay, PhonePe, or any other supported UPI app).
2. The payment system is properly configured to detect installed UPI apps.
3. There are active UPI-based coupons available in the system for various UPI apps.
4. The user is logged into the application and has navigated to the coupon section.
TC-720.title: Hide UPI coupons when no UPI apps are installed

attachments.name: Shiv - Card Payments & MOP Offers Test scenarios (1).pdf

steps.step: Open the application and navigate to the coupon section.
steps.result: The coupon section is displayed.

steps.step: Observe the list of available coupons.
steps.result: No UPI-based coupons are visible in the coupon section.

steps.step: Attempt to search for UPI-based coupons (e.g., GPay, PhonePe).
steps.result: No results are found for UPI-based coupons.

steps.step: Navigate to the payment screen and check available payment methods.
steps.result: UPI payment options are either not present or clearly marked as unavailable.

steps.step: Attempt to manually apply a UPI-based coupon code (if such functionality exists).
steps.result: The system rejects the UPI-based coupon code and displays an appropriate error message.

TC-721.description: Ensure that when multiple supported UPI apps (e.g., GPay and PhonePe) are installed, coupons for all installed apps are displayed. This test checks the system's capability to detect and display coupons for multiple UPI apps simultaneously.
TC-721.preconditions:
1. The test device has multiple supported UPI apps installed (e.g., GPay and PhonePe).
2. The payment system is properly configured to detect installed UPI apps.
3. There are active coupons available in the system for all installed UPI apps.
4. The user is logged into the application and has navigated to the coupon section.
TC-721.title: Show all eligible UPI coupons with multiple UPI apps

attachments.name: Shiv - Card Payments & MOP Offers Test scenarios (1).pdf

steps.step: Open the application and navigate to the coupon section.
steps.result: The coupon section is displayed.

steps.step: Observe the list of available coupons.
steps.result: Coupons for all installed UPI apps (GPay and PhonePe) are visible in the coupon section.

steps.step: Verify that coupons for each installed UPI app are present.
steps.result: Both GPay and PhonePe coupons are listed.

steps.step: Select a coupon for one UPI app (e.g., GPay) and proceed to the payment screen.
steps.result: The selected coupon is applied, and the payment mode is automatically set to the corresponding UPI app (GPay).

steps.step: Go back to the coupon section and select a coupon for the other UPI app (e.g., PhonePe).
steps.result: The system allows changing the coupon, applies the new PhonePe coupon, and updates the payment mode to PhonePe.

TC-722.description: Verify that the coupon display updates dynamically when a new supported UPI app is installed on the device. This test ensures the system can detect changes in installed apps and update the coupon offerings accordingly without requiring an app restart.
TC-722.preconditions:
1. The test device initially has no UPI apps installed.
2. The payment system is properly configured to detect installed UPI apps.
3. There are active coupons available in the system for various UPI apps.
4. The user is logged into the application and has the coupon section open.
5. The user has the ability to install a supported UPI app (e.g., GPay) during the test.
TC-722.title: Update coupon display after UPI app installation

attachments.name: Shiv - Card Payments & MOP Offers Test scenarios (1).pdf

steps.step: Open the application and navigate to the coupon section.
steps.result: The coupon section is displayed with no UPI-based coupons visible.

steps.step: Without closing the application, install a supported UPI app (e.g., GPay) on the device.
steps.result: The UPI app is successfully installed on the device.

steps.step: Return to the application's coupon section and refresh the page (if not auto-refreshed).
steps.result: The coupon section updates to display GPay-related coupons.

steps.step: Select a newly appeared GPay coupon and proceed to the payment screen.
steps.result: The selected GPay coupon is applied, and the payment mode is automatically set to GPay.

steps.step: Verify that the system detected the new UPI app without requiring an application restart.
steps.result: The application successfully recognized the newly installed UPI app and updated the coupon offerings accordingly.

TC-723.description: Confirm that the correct logos are displayed alongside the coupons for each supported UPI app. This test ensures visual accuracy and helps users quickly identify coupons relevant to their installed UPI apps.
TC-723.preconditions:
1. The test device has multiple supported UPI apps installed (e.g., GPay and PhonePe).
2. The payment system is properly configured to detect installed UPI apps.
3. There are active coupons available in the system for all installed UPI apps.
4. The user is logged into the application and has navigated to the coupon section.
5. The application has the correct and up-to-date logos for all supported UPI apps stored.
TC-723.title: Correct logo display for UPI app coupons

attachments.name: Shiv - Card Payments & MOP Offers Test scenarios (1).pdf

steps.step: Open the application and navigate to the coupon section.
steps.result: The coupon section is displayed with coupons for all installed UPI apps.

steps.step: Observe the logos displayed alongside the UPI app coupons.
steps.result: Each UPI app coupon is accompanied by its correct and current logo.

steps.step: Verify the GPay logo is correctly displayed next to GPay coupons.
steps.result: The GPay logo is accurate, clear, and properly sized.

steps.step: Verify the PhonePe logo is correctly displayed next to PhonePe coupons.
steps.result: The PhonePe logo is accurate, clear, and properly sized.

steps.step: Check the consistency of logo placement and size across different coupons.
steps.result: All logos are consistently placed and sized across different coupons.

steps.step: Verify logo visibility in different display modes (e.g., dark mode if applicable).
steps.result: Logos remain clear and visible in all applicable display modes.

APNA-WH-701.description: Verify accurate WH Bill Value
APNA-WH-701.title: Billing - Verify WH Bill, coupon, and other offers discount Value

steps.result: The final bill value must be correct: (WH Price + Commission + Discounts).

APNA-WH-702.description: Verify breakdown of WH discount vs. Coupon
APNA-WH-702.title: Billing - WholeSale offer billing

steps.result: The UI must provide a transparent breakdown of savings to the user.
The invoice sent to the user must clearly reflect the actual savings and final price paid.

APNA-WH-801.description: Ensure applying a WH discount doesn’t conflict with or overwrite the milestone offer flow.
APNA-WH-801.title: Existing Offer Construct - Milestone offer interaction with WH unlock

APNA-WH-802.description: Validate that compound discounts from all three sources are calculated and applied correctly.
APNA-WH-802.title: Existing Offer Construct -  Best Deals + Special Price Deal + Coupon + WH interaction

APNA-WH-501.description: The Normal Cart & WH cart should gracefully handle this transition.
APNA-WH-501.title: Address Change - Change to a non-serviceable location

APNA-WH-601.description: The cart state, including item count and WH status, must be preserved.
APNA-WH-601.title: Verify Cart State - User adds to cart, backgrounds the app, then returns

APNA-WH-202.description: The offer zone must dynamically update to show the WH price if the cart is unlocked.
APNA-WH-202.title: Existing Offer Zones - Offer Zone shows SPO/OSP prices

APNA-WH-205.description: MOV should be correctly recalculated based on the items in the normal cart.
APNA-WH-205.title: Existing Offer Zones - Minimum Order Value (MOV) visibility

APNA-WH-206.description: Offer application for normal cart should be correct in terms of price and quantity.
APNA-WH-206.title: Existing Offer Zones - Correct Offer Price and Qty added to CART upon unlock offer items

APNA-WH-301.description: The final bill should correctly show savings from both the WH discount and the coupon.
APNA-WH-301.title: Coupon Offers -  Apply coupon when WH is unlocked

APNA-WH-302.description: The unlocked state should persist if the threshold was met before the coupon was applied.
APNA-WH-302.title: Coupon Offers - Coupon reduces cart value below WH unlock threshold

TC-424.description: Perform multiple rapid store status changes (e.g., pickers marking in/out, going on/off breaks in quick succession). Verify that the system accurately captures all changes, maintains correct final store status, and updates all relevant tables without data loss or inconsistencies.
TC-424.preconditions:
1. The system is up and running.
2. Multiple stores are set up in the system.
3. Multiple pickers are registered and associated with different stores.
4. All relevant tables (StoreUnserviceabilityLog, StoreUnserviceabilityReason, StoreRunningMetrics, Smpermission_UserRoleMap, OrderAssignment, PickingStatus, PickerStatus) are accessible and in a known state.
5. The system is configured to handle concurrent operations.
6. Monitoring tools are in place to track system performance and data consistency.
TC-424.title: Rapid successive store status changes

steps.step: Select a target store for the test.
steps.result: A store is selected and its initial status is recorded.

steps.step: Rapidly mark in multiple pickers for the selected store within a short time frame (e.g., 5 seconds).
steps.result: All picker mark-ins are recorded accurately. The store becomes serviceable if it wasn't already. StoreRunningMetrics is updated correctly with the new active picker count.

steps.step: Immediately after step 2, mark out half of the pickers within a short time frame.
steps.result: All picker mark-outs are recorded accurately. The store remains serviceable as long as at least one picker is still active. StoreRunningMetrics is updated correctly.

steps.step: Put all remaining active pickers on break simultaneously.
steps.result: All break statuses are recorded accurately. The store becomes unserviceable with 'NO_PICKERS' reason. A new entry is created in StoreUnserviceabilityLog with the correct timestamp and reason.

steps.step: Cancel the break for one picker immediately.
steps.result: The break cancellation is processed correctly. The store becomes serviceable again. The 'NO_PICKERS' reason is removed from StoreUnserviceabilityReason.

steps.step: Revoke the STORE_PICKER role from the active picker.
steps.result: The role revocation is processed correctly. The store becomes unserviceable again with 'NO_PICKERS' reason. A new entry is created in StoreUnserviceabilityLog.

steps.step: Mark in a new picker with TEMP_PICKER role.
steps.result: The new picker is marked in successfully. The store becomes serviceable. The 'NO_PICKERS' reason is removed from StoreUnserviceabilityReason.

steps.step: Check the final status of the store and verify all relevant table entries.
steps.result: The store's final status is serviceable. All tables (StoreUnserviceabilityLog, StoreUnserviceabilityReason, StoreRunningMetrics, Smpermission_UserRoleMap, PickerStatus) reflect the correct final state with accurate timestamps and reasons for all status changes.

steps.step: Verify the CX App display throughout the test.
steps.result: The CX App correctly displayed 'Temporarily Unavailable' message during periods when the store was unserviceable, and showed the store as available when it was serviceable.

steps.step: Check system logs for any errors or warnings during the rapid status changes.
steps.result: No critical errors are logged. Any warnings are related to expected issues from rapid status changes, not data inconsistencies or lost updates.

TC-425.description: Simulate high-volume concurrent status updates across multiple stores (e.g., shift change time with many pickers marking in/out simultaneously). Verify system performance, ensuring all updates are processed correctly without delays or data inconsistencies.
TC-425.preconditions:
1. The system is up and running with multiple stores configured.
2. A large number of pickers (e.g., 100+) are registered across different stores.
3. All relevant tables (StoreUnserviceabilityLog, StoreUnserviceabilityReason, StoreRunningMetrics, Smpermission_UserRoleMap, OrderAssignment, PickingStatus, PickerStatus) are accessible and in a known state.
4. The system is configured to handle high concurrency.
5. Performance monitoring tools are set up to track system metrics (response time, throughput, error rates).
6. A mechanism to simulate concurrent user actions is in place.
TC-425.title: High-volume concurrent status updates across multiple stores

steps.step: Select multiple stores (e.g., 20) for the concurrent update test.
steps.result: Stores are selected and their initial statuses are recorded.

steps.step: Simulate a shift change scenario by marking out all active pickers across the selected stores simultaneously.
steps.result: All mark-out operations are processed correctly. Stores with no remaining active pickers become unserviceable with 'NO_PICKERS' reason. Corresponding entries are created in StoreUnserviceabilityLog and StoreUnserviceabilityReason tables for each affected store.

steps.step: Immediately after step 2, simulate new shift pickers marking in across all selected stores concurrently.
steps.result: All mark-in operations are processed correctly. Stores become serviceable as pickers mark in. 'NO_PICKERS' reason is removed from StoreUnserviceabilityReason for each store as it becomes serviceable.

steps.step: During the mark-in process, simulate some pickers going on break and others canceling breaks across different stores.
steps.result: Break starts and cancellations are processed accurately. Store serviceability status changes accordingly based on the number of active pickers.

steps.step: Concurrently with steps 3 and 4, simulate role changes (e.g., revoking STORE_PICKER role and assigning TEMP_PICKER role) for some pickers across different stores.
steps.result: Role changes are processed correctly. Store serviceability is affected appropriately when the last active picker's role is revoked.

steps.step: Monitor system performance metrics throughout the test.
steps.result: System maintains acceptable performance levels: response times remain within defined SLAs, no significant increase in error rates, and throughput handles the high volume of concurrent updates.

steps.step: Verify the final status of all stores involved in the test.
steps.result: Each store's final status (serviceable or unserviceable) is correct based on the final state of its pickers.

steps.step: Check data consistency across all relevant tables.
steps.result: All tables (StoreUnserviceabilityLog, StoreUnserviceabilityReason, StoreRunningMetrics, Smpermission_UserRoleMap, PickerStatus) show consistent data reflecting the final state of each store and its pickers. No data loss or inconsistencies are observed.

steps.step: Verify CX App displays for a sample of stores throughout the test.
steps.result: CX App correctly reflected the changing store statuses, showing 'Temporarily Unavailable' when stores were unserviceable and available status when serviceable.

steps.step: Analyze system logs for any errors, warnings, or performance issues during the high-volume concurrent updates.
steps.result: No critical errors are logged. Any warnings are expected and do not indicate data inconsistencies or system failures. Performance logs show the system handled the load within acceptable parameters.

TC-426.description: Perform store status changes during simulated peak load periods (e.g., high order volumes, multiple system operations ongoing). Verify that status updates are processed accurately and timely, without impacting other system operations or causing delays in status representation.
TC-426.preconditions:
1. The system is up and running with multiple stores configured.
2. A mechanism to simulate peak load conditions (high order volumes, multiple system operations) is in place.
3. Multiple pickers are registered and associated with different stores.
4. All relevant tables (StoreUnserviceabilityLog, StoreUnserviceabilityReason, StoreRunningMetrics, Smpermission_UserRoleMap, OrderAssignment, PickingStatus, PickerStatus) are accessible and in a known state.
5. Performance monitoring tools are set up to track system metrics (response time, throughput, error rates).
6. The CX App is integrated and accessible for status verification.
TC-426.title: Store status updates during peak load periods

steps.step: Initiate simulated peak load conditions (e.g., high order volume, multiple system operations).
steps.result: System enters a high-load state with increased database transactions and API calls.

steps.step: Select a set of target stores for the test.
steps.result: Stores are selected and their initial statuses are recorded.

steps.step:
For each target store, perform a series of rapid status changes:
- Mark out the last active picker
- Mark in a new picker
- Put the active picker on break
- Cancel the break
- Revoke and reassign picker roles
steps.result: All status changes are processed accurately and in the correct order. Store serviceability status changes are reflected correctly in the system.

steps.step: Simultaneously with step 3, simulate order assignments and picking operations for stores that remain serviceable.
steps.result: Order assignments and picking operations are processed without errors. The system maintains data consistency between store status and order processing.

steps.step: Monitor system performance metrics throughout the test.
steps.result: System maintains acceptable performance levels under peak load: response times for status updates remain within defined SLAs, no significant increase in error rates, and the system continues to process other operations (like order assignments) efficiently.

steps.step: Verify the status of all target stores after the series of changes.
steps.result: Each store's status (serviceable or unserviceable) is correct based on the final state of its pickers. StoreUnserviceabilityLog and StoreUnserviceabilityReason tables accurately reflect the status history.

steps.step: Check data consistency across all relevant tables.
steps.result: All tables (StoreUnserviceabilityLog, StoreUnserviceabilityReason, StoreRunningMetrics, Smpermission_UserRoleMap, OrderAssignment, PickingStatus, PickerStatus) show consistent data reflecting the final state of each store and its pickers. No data loss or inconsistencies are observed.

steps.step: Verify CX App displays for the target stores throughout the test.
steps.result: CX App correctly reflected the changing store statuses in real-time, showing 'Temporarily Unavailable' when stores were unserviceable and available status when serviceable, without significant delays.

steps.step: Attempt to perform invalid operations during peak load (e.g., marking in an already active picker, ending a non-existent unserviceability reason).
steps.result: System correctly rejects invalid operations and logs appropriate errors, maintaining data integrity even under peak load.

steps.step: Analyze system logs for any errors, warnings, or performance issues during the peak load status updates.
steps.result: No critical errors are logged. Any warnings are expected and do not indicate data inconsistencies or system failures. Performance logs show the system handled the load within acceptable parameters, with status updates not significantly impacting other system operations.

TC-427.description: Gradually accumulate multiple unserviceability reasons for a store over time. Measure system performance in processing status changes and queries as the number of reasons increases, ensuring the system maintains efficiency and accuracy under complex status conditions.
TC-427.preconditions:
1. The system is up and running with multiple stores configured.
2. All relevant tables (StoreUnserviceabilityLog, StoreUnserviceabilityReason, StoreRunningMetrics) are accessible and in a known state.
3. Performance monitoring tools are set up to track system metrics (response time, throughput, error rates).
4. A mechanism to add and remove unserviceability reasons is in place.
5. The CX App is integrated and accessible for status verification.
TC-427.title: System performance with accumulated unserviceability reasons

steps.step: Select a target store for the test.
steps.result: A store is selected and its initial status is recorded.

steps.step: Add an initial unserviceability reason (e.g., 'NO_PICKERS') to the target store.
steps.result: The reason is added successfully. StoreUnserviceabilityLog and StoreUnserviceabilityReason tables are updated correctly. The store becomes unserviceable.

steps.step: Measure and record the system's performance in processing status changes and queries for the store.
steps.result: Baseline performance metrics (response time, throughput) are recorded.

steps.step: Gradually add more unserviceability reasons to the store (e.g., 'STORE_CLOSED', 'SYSTEM_MAINTENANCE', custom reasons) one by one, up to a high number (e.g., 50 reasons).
steps.result: Each reason is added successfully. StoreUnserviceabilityLog and StoreUnserviceabilityReason tables are updated correctly for each addition.

steps.step:
After each addition, perform a set of operations:
- Query the store's status
- Attempt to mark in a picker
- Check the CX App display
steps.result: The system correctly reports the store as unserviceable, rejects picker mark-in attempts, and the CX App shows 'Temporarily Unavailable' consistently.

steps.step: Measure and record the system's performance after each new reason is added.
steps.result: Performance metrics are recorded, showing any changes in response time or throughput as the number of reasons increases.

steps.step: Attempt to add duplicate reasons during the accumulation process.
steps.result: System correctly rejects duplicate reasons, maintaining data integrity.

steps.step: Once the maximum number of reasons is reached, attempt to remove reasons one by one, starting with 'NO_PICKERS'.
steps.result: Each reason is removed successfully. StoreUnserviceabilityLog and StoreUnserviceabilityReason tables are updated correctly for each removal. The store remains unserviceable until all reasons are removed.

steps.step: After each removal, repeat the set of operations from step 5.
steps.result: The system continues to correctly report the store as unserviceable and reject picker mark-in attempts until all reasons are removed. The CX App display remains consistent.

steps.step: Measure and record the system's performance after each reason is removed.
steps.result: Performance metrics are recorded, showing any changes in response time or throughput as the number of reasons decreases.

steps.step: After all reasons are removed, verify the final status of the store.
steps.result: The store becomes serviceable. StoreUnserviceabilityLog shows the complete history of changes. StoreUnserviceabilityReason table is empty for this store.

steps.step: Analyze the performance metrics collected throughout the test.
steps.result: Performance remains within acceptable levels throughout the test. Any degradation in performance as reasons accumulated is within expected parameters. Performance returns to baseline levels after reasons are removed.

steps.step: Check data consistency across all relevant tables.
steps.result: All tables (StoreUnserviceabilityLog, StoreUnserviceabilityReason, StoreRunningMetrics) show consistent data reflecting the history of unserviceability reasons. No data loss or inconsistencies are observed.

steps.step: Verify system logs for any errors or warnings during the test.
steps.result: No critical errors are logged. Any warnings are expected and do not indicate data inconsistencies or system failures.

TC-420.description: Change store status through TezPos App (e.g., last picker marks out) and verify that the CX App immediately reflects the change by showing 'Temporarily Unavailable'. Ensure consistency in status representation across both platforms.
TC-420.preconditions:
1. TezPos App and CX App are both operational and connected to the system.
2. At least one store is active in the system with multiple pickers.
3. The store is currently serviceable.
4. Test environment allows for simulating picker actions and monitoring store status across both apps.
TC-420.title: Consistent store status across TezPos and CX App

steps.step: Open TezPos App and navigate to the store management section.
steps.result: TezPos App displays the current store status as 'Serviceable'.

steps.step: Open CX App and navigate to the store selection or order placement section.
steps.result: CX App shows the store as available for order placement.

steps.step: In TezPos App, mark out all but one picker from their shifts.
steps.result: TezPos App updates to show reduced number of active pickers. Store remains serviceable.

steps.step: Mark out the last active picker from their shift in TezPos App.
steps.result: TezPos App immediately updates the store status to 'Unserviceable' with reason 'NO_PICKERS'.

steps.step: Immediately check the store status in the CX App.
steps.result: CX App should quickly update to show the store as 'Temporarily Unavailable'.

steps.step: Attempt to place an order for this store in the CX App.
steps.result: CX App should not allow order placement, displaying a message indicating the store is temporarily unavailable.

steps.step: In TezPos App, mark in a picker to make the store serviceable again.
steps.result: TezPos App immediately updates the store status to 'Serviceable'.

steps.step: Quickly switch to the CX App and check the store status.
steps.result: CX App should promptly update to show the store as available for order placement.

steps.step: Attempt to place an order for this store in the CX App.
steps.result: CX App should now allow order placement, confirming the store is serviceable.

TC-421.description: Simulate high network latency and change store status. Verify that the CX App eventually shows the correct status ('Temporarily Unavailable' for unserviceable store) without showing intermediate incorrect states, ensuring eventual consistency across platforms.
TC-421.preconditions:
1. TezPos App and CX App are both operational and connected to the system.
2. A test environment is set up that allows simulation of network latency.
3. At least one store is active and serviceable in the system.
4. Tools are available to monitor and log status changes and their timestamps across both apps.
TC-421.title: Store status update in CX App during network latency

steps.step: Set up network latency simulation to introduce a significant delay (e.g., 5-10 seconds) between the backend and the CX App.
steps.result: Network latency is successfully simulated, causing delays in communication between the backend and CX App.

steps.step: Open TezPos App and verify the current store status.
steps.result: TezPos App shows the store as 'Serviceable'.

steps.step: Open CX App and check the store status.
steps.result: CX App shows the store as available for order placement.

steps.step: In TezPos App, perform an action to make the store unserviceable (e.g., mark out all pickers).
steps.result: TezPos App immediately updates to show the store as 'Unserviceable' with reason 'NO_PICKERS'.

steps.step: Continuously monitor the CX App for status changes.
steps.result: CX App should not immediately reflect the change due to simulated network latency.

steps.step: Wait for the duration of the simulated latency.
steps.result: After the latency period, CX App should update to show the store as 'Temporarily Unavailable' without displaying any intermediate incorrect states.

steps.step: Attempt to place an order for this store in the CX App.
steps.result: CX App should not allow order placement, confirming the unserviceable status.

steps.step: In TezPos App, make the store serviceable again (e.g., mark in a picker).
steps.result: TezPos App immediately updates to show the store as 'Serviceable'.

steps.step: Continue monitoring the CX App for status changes.
steps.result: After the latency period, CX App should update to show the store as available for order placement, without any incorrect intermediate states.

steps.step: Verify the timestamps of status changes in both apps and compare with the backend logs.
steps.result: The timestamps should reflect the simulated latency, showing a delay between TezPos App updates and CX App updates, but maintaining eventual consistency.

TC-422.description: Add and remove multiple unserviceability reasons in quick succession. Verify that both TezPos and CX App consistently represent the store's final status, handling the rapid changes correctly across platforms.
TC-422.preconditions:
1. TezPos App and CX App are both operational and connected to the system.
2. A test environment is set up that allows rapid addition and removal of multiple unserviceability reasons.
3. At least one store is active and initially serviceable in the system.
4. Tools are available to monitor and log status changes across both apps in real-time.
TC-422.title: Cross-platform status consistency during multiple reason changes

steps.step: Open TezPos App and verify the initial store status.
steps.result: TezPos App shows the store as 'Serviceable'.

steps.step: Open CX App and check the initial store status.
steps.result: CX App shows the store as available for order placement.

steps.step: In TezPos App, quickly add unserviceability reason 'NO_PICKERS' (e.g., mark out all pickers).
steps.result: TezPos App updates to show the store as 'Unserviceable' with reason 'NO_PICKERS'.

steps.step: Immediately add another unserviceability reason 'STORE_CLOSED' in TezPos App.
steps.result: TezPos App shows the store as 'Unserviceable' with both reasons 'NO_PICKERS' and 'STORE_CLOSED'.

steps.step: Quickly remove the 'NO_PICKERS' reason (e.g., mark in a picker) in TezPos App.
steps.result: TezPos App continues to show the store as 'Unserviceable' due to the remaining 'STORE_CLOSED' reason.

steps.step: Rapidly check the status in CX App multiple times during these changes.
steps.result: CX App should consistently show the store as 'Temporarily Unavailable' throughout the changes, without flickering between states.

steps.step: Remove the 'STORE_CLOSED' reason in TezPos App.
steps.result: TezPos App updates to show the store as 'Serviceable'.

steps.step: Immediately check the status in CX App.
steps.result: CX App should update to show the store as available for order placement.

steps.step: Attempt to place an order in CX App.
steps.result: CX App should allow order placement, confirming the final serviceable status.

steps.step: Review the logs of status changes in both apps and compare with backend logs.
steps.result: The logs should show that both apps maintained consistent representation of the store's final status, handling the rapid changes correctly without showing intermediate states to the user.

TC-423.description: Simulate an outage in one part of the system (e.g., TezPos API) and change store status. Verify that both TezPos App (using cache) and CX App show consistent and correct store status, demonstrating resilience in cross-platform integration.
TC-423.preconditions:
1. TezPos App and CX App are both operational and connected to the system.
2. A test environment is set up that allows simulation of partial system outages.
3. At least one store is active and initially serviceable in the system.
4. TezPos App has a functioning cache system for offline operations.
5. Tools are available to monitor app behaviors and system status during the simulated outage.
TC-423.title: Status representation during partial system outage

steps.step: Verify initial store status in both TezPos and CX Apps.
steps.result: Both apps show the store as 'Serviceable' and available for order placement.

steps.step: Simulate an outage in the TezPos API while keeping other parts of the system operational.
steps.result: TezPos API becomes unavailable, but other system components remain functional.

steps.step: Attempt to change store status in TezPos App (e.g., mark out all pickers).
steps.result: TezPos App uses cache data to reflect the status change locally, showing store as 'Unserviceable'.

steps.step: Check if TezPos App displays any indicators of operating in offline mode.
steps.result: TezPos App should indicate it's using cached data or operating in offline mode.

steps.step: Attempt to sync TezPos App with the backend.
steps.result: Sync attempt should fail due to the simulated API outage.

steps.step: Check the store status in CX App.
steps.result: CX App should still show the correct store status (Temporarily Unavailable) despite TezPos API outage, as it can communicate with other operational parts of the system.

steps.step: Attempt to place an order through CX App.
steps.result: CX App should not allow order placement, consistent with the unserviceable status.

steps.step: Restore the TezPos API connection.
steps.result: TezPos API becomes available again.

steps.step: Trigger a sync in TezPos App.
steps.result: TezPos App should successfully sync with the backend, confirming the unserviceable status.

steps.step: Change the store status back to serviceable in TezPos App.
steps.result: TezPos App updates to show the store as 'Serviceable' and successfully syncs with the backend.

steps.step: Check the store status in CX App.
steps.result: CX App should update to show the store as available for order placement.

steps.step: Review system logs and compare status changes across both apps during the outage period.
steps.result: Logs should demonstrate that both apps maintained consistent and correct store status representation throughout the partial system outage, with TezPos App using cache and CX App communicating with available system components.

TC-416.description: Simulate a database transaction failure during a picker's mark-in operation. Verify that the system handles the failure gracefully, maintains data consistency, and either retries the operation or fails without corrupting the store's status or picker count.
TC-416.preconditions:
1. The system is up and running.
2. A test store is set up in the system.
3. At least one picker is registered and associated with the test store.
4. The store is currently unserviceable due to NO_PICKERS reason.
5. A mechanism to simulate database transaction failure is in place.
6. All relevant tables (StoreUnserviceabilityLog, StoreUnserviceabilityReason, StoreRunningMetrics) are accessible and in a known state.
7. Error logging system is configured and operational.
TC-416.title: Mark-in operation during database transaction failure

steps.step: Initiate a mark-in operation for a picker in the test store.
steps.result: The system begins processing the mark-in operation.

steps.step: Simulate a database transaction failure during the mark-in operation.
steps.result: The system detects the database transaction failure.

steps.step: Observe the system's response to the database transaction failure.
steps.result:
The system should handle the failure gracefully:
- An error is logged detailing the nature of the failure.
- The mark-in operation is either retried automatically or fails without corrupting data.
- The picker's status remains unchanged (not marked in).
- The store's unserviceable status due to NO_PICKERS reason remains intact.

steps.step: Attempt to query the store's status and picker count.
steps.result:
The system returns accurate information:
- Store status shows as unserviceable.
- Picker count remains unchanged.
- NO_PICKERS reason is still present in StoreUnserviceabilityReason.

steps.step: Check the system logs.
steps.result: Logs show the attempted mark-in operation, the database transaction failure, and the system's response to the failure.

steps.step: Resolve the simulated database issue and retry the mark-in operation.
steps.result:
The mark-in operation succeeds:
- Picker is successfully marked in.
- Store becomes serviceable.
- NO_PICKERS reason is removed from StoreUnserviceabilityReason.
- StoreUnserviceabilityLog is updated with the correct timestamp and reason for becoming serviceable.

steps.step: Verify the final state of all relevant tables.
steps.result: All tables (StoreUnserviceabilityLog, StoreUnserviceabilityReason, StoreRunningMetrics) reflect the correct final state after the successful mark-in operation.

TC-417.description: Trigger a store unserviceability event (e.g., last picker marking out) during a simulated database outage. Verify that the system uses cache data to maintain operations, logs the event for later processing, and updates the database once it's available without creating inconsistencies.
TC-417.preconditions:
1. The system is up and running.
2. A test store is set up in the system.
3. The store has only one active picker.
4. The store is currently serviceable.
5. A mechanism to simulate a database outage is in place.
6. TezPos App is configured to use cache data during API failures.
7. All relevant tables (StoreUnserviceabilityLog, StoreUnserviceabilityReason, StoreRunningMetrics) are accessible and in a known state.
8. Error logging system is configured and operational.
TC-417.title: Store unserviceability update during database outage

steps.step: Simulate a database outage.
steps.result: The system detects the database is unavailable and switches to using cache data.

steps.step: Initiate a mark-out operation for the last active picker in the test store using the TezPos App.
steps.result:
The TezPos App processes the mark-out operation using cached data:
- The picker is marked out in the app's local state.
- The app shows the store as unserviceable with NO_PICKERS reason.

steps.step: Attempt to query the store's status and picker count through the system API.
steps.result:
The system returns cached data:
- Store status shows as unserviceable.
- Picker count is zero.
- NO_PICKERS reason is present.

steps.step: Check the CX App integration.
steps.result: The CX App shows the store as 'Temporarily Unavailable' based on the cached data.

steps.step: Resolve the simulated database outage.
steps.result: The system detects that the database is available again.

steps.step: Observe the system's synchronization process.
steps.result:
The system synchronizes the cached data with the database:
- StoreUnserviceabilityLog is updated with the correct timestamp and NO_PICKERS reason.
- StoreUnserviceabilityReason table is updated to include NO_PICKERS reason.
- StoreRunningMetrics is updated to reflect zero active pickers.

steps.step: Verify the final state of all relevant tables and system status.
steps.result:
All tables and system status reflect the correct final state:
- Store is unserviceable with NO_PICKERS reason.
- StoreUnserviceabilityLog shows the correct entry for the store becoming unserviceable.
- StoreRunningMetrics shows zero active pickers.
- CX App continues to show the store as 'Temporarily Unavailable'.

steps.step: Check the system logs.
steps.result: Logs show the database outage, the use of cached data, and the synchronization process after database recovery.

TC-418.description: Perform multiple store status changes during a database outage. After the database recovers, verify that all changes are correctly applied, maintaining the correct final state of the store, accurate picker counts, and proper unserviceability logs.
TC-418.preconditions:
1. The system is up and running.
2. A test store is set up in the system with multiple pickers.
3. The store is initially serviceable.
4. A mechanism to simulate a database outage is in place.
5. TezPos App is configured to use cache data during API failures.
6. All relevant tables (StoreUnserviceabilityLog, StoreUnserviceabilityReason, StoreRunningMetrics) are accessible and in a known state.
7. Error logging system is configured and operational.
TC-418.title: Data consistency after recovery from database failure

steps.step: Simulate a database outage.
steps.result: The system detects the database is unavailable and switches to using cache data.

steps.step:
Perform multiple store status changes using the TezPos App:
1. Mark out all but one picker.
2. Put the last picker on break.
3. Cancel the break for the last picker.
4. Mark in a previously marked out picker.
steps.result:
The TezPos App processes all operations using cached data:
- Picker statuses are updated in the app's local state.
- The app shows appropriate store status changes based on picker availability.

steps.step: Attempt to query the store's status and picker count through the system API during the outage.
steps.result: The system returns cached data reflecting the latest known state.

steps.step: Resolve the simulated database outage.
steps.result: The system detects that the database is available again.

steps.step: Observe the system's synchronization process.
steps.result:
The system synchronizes the cached data with the database:
- StoreUnserviceabilityLog is updated with correct timestamps and reasons for each status change.
- StoreUnserviceabilityReason table is updated to reflect the final state.
- StoreRunningMetrics is updated to show the correct number of active pickers.

steps.step: Verify the final state of all relevant tables and system status.
steps.result:
All tables and system status reflect the correct final state:
- Store is serviceable.
- StoreUnserviceabilityLog shows correct entries for all status changes during the outage.
- StoreRunningMetrics shows the correct number of active pickers.
- No inconsistencies in picker counts or store status are present.

steps.step: Check the system logs.
steps.result: Logs show the database outage, all status change operations during the outage, and the synchronization process after database recovery.

steps.step: Perform a series of new operations (mark-in, mark-out, break) to verify normal system functionality.
steps.result: All new operations are processed correctly and reflected immediately in the database and system status.

TC-419.description: Simulate API response failures while performing operations in the TezPos App. Verify that the app uses cached data for critical operations like mark-in/out, maintaining functionality and later synchronizing with the server when available.
TC-419.preconditions:
1. The system is up and running.
2. A test store is set up in the system with multiple pickers.
3. The store is initially serviceable.
4. TezPos App is installed and configured on a test device.
5. A mechanism to simulate API response failures is in place.
6. TezPos App is configured to use cache data during API failures.
7. All relevant tables (StoreUnserviceabilityLog, StoreUnserviceabilityReason, StoreRunningMetrics) are accessible and in a known state.
8. Error logging system is configured and operational.
TC-419.title: TezPos App functionality during API response failure

steps.step: Log in to the TezPos App with a test picker account.
steps.result: The picker is successfully logged in to the TezPos App.

steps.step: Simulate API response failure for the TezPos App.
steps.result: The TezPos App detects API failure and switches to using cached data.

steps.step: Perform a mark-out operation for the logged-in picker using the TezPos App.
steps.result:
The TezPos App processes the mark-out operation using cached data:
- The picker is shown as marked out in the app.
- The app updates its local store status if this was the last active picker.

steps.step: Attempt to view store status and picker information in the TezPos App.
steps.result: The TezPos App displays information based on its cached data, showing the updated picker status and store status.

steps.step: Perform a break operation for another picker (if available) using the TezPos App.
steps.result:
The TezPos App processes the break operation using cached data:
- The picker is shown as on break in the app.
- The app updates its local store status if this affects store serviceability.

steps.step: Resolve the simulated API response failure.
steps.result: The TezPos App detects that the API is available again.

steps.step: Observe the TezPos App's synchronization process.
steps.result:
The TezPos App synchronizes its cached data with the server:
- All operations performed during the API failure are sent to the server.
- The app receives and applies any updates from the server.
- The app's displayed information matches the server's state.

steps.step: Verify the final state of all relevant tables and system status on the server.
steps.result:
All tables and system status reflect the correct final state:
- StoreUnserviceabilityLog shows correct entries for any status changes.
- StoreRunningMetrics shows the correct number of active pickers.
- Store status is consistent with the number of available pickers.

steps.step: Perform a new operation (e.g., mark-in) in the TezPos App.
steps.result:
The operation is processed normally:
- The change is immediately reflected in the app.
- The server receives and processes the operation correctly.
- The app and server remain in sync.

steps.step: Check the system logs on both the TezPos App and the server.
steps.result: Logs show the API failure period, operations performed during the failure, and the synchronization process after API recovery.

TC-412.description: Set up a store with multiple unserviceability reasons (e.g., NO_PICKERS, STORE_CLOSED). Resolve each reason sequentially and verify that the store becomes serviceable only when all reasons are resolved. Ensure each resolution is accurately logged and the store status is updated correctly after each step.
TC-412.preconditions:
1. A store is set up in the system.
2. The store has multiple unserviceability reasons active (e.g., NO_PICKERS, STORE_CLOSED).
3. The StoreUnserviceabilityLog and StoreUnserviceabilityReason tables are properly configured and accessible.
4. The system is set up to handle multiple unserviceability reasons independently.
5. There are no active pickers in the store.
6. The store is currently closed.
TC-412.title: Sequential resolution of multiple unserviceability reasons

steps.step: Verify the initial state of the store
steps.result: The store is unserviceable with multiple reasons (NO_PICKERS, STORE_CLOSED) logged in StoreUnserviceabilityLog and StoreUnserviceabilityReason tables.

steps.step: Resolve the NO_PICKERS reason by having a picker mark in
steps.result:
1. The NO_PICKERS reason is removed from StoreUnserviceabilityReason table.
2. A new entry is added to StoreUnserviceabilityLog with the correct timestamp and reason for resolution.
3. The store remains unserviceable due to the STORE_CLOSED reason.

steps.step: Verify the store status after resolving NO_PICKERS reason
steps.result: The store is still unserviceable. The StoreUnserviceabilityLog shows the resolution of NO_PICKERS reason, but STORE_CLOSED reason persists.

steps.step: Resolve the STORE_CLOSED reason by opening the store
steps.result:
1. The STORE_CLOSED reason is removed from StoreUnserviceabilityReason table.
2. A new entry is added to StoreUnserviceabilityLog with the correct timestamp and reason for resolution.
3. The store becomes serviceable as all unserviceability reasons are resolved.

steps.step: Verify the final state of the store
steps.result:
1. The store is now serviceable.
2. StoreUnserviceabilityLog contains entries for both resolutions with correct timestamps.
3. StoreUnserviceabilityReason table is empty for this store.
4. The CX App no longer shows 'Temporarily Unavailable' for this store.

TC-413.description: Configure a store with multiple unserviceability reasons. Trigger the resolution of all reasons simultaneously (e.g., pickers marking in while store reopens). Verify that the system handles the concurrent resolutions correctly, updating the store status to serviceable and logging all changes accurately.
TC-413.preconditions:
1. A store is set up in the system.
2. The store has multiple unserviceability reasons active (e.g., NO_PICKERS, STORE_CLOSED).
3. The StoreUnserviceabilityLog and StoreUnserviceabilityReason tables are properly configured and accessible.
4. The system is capable of handling concurrent operations.
5. There are no active pickers in the store.
6. The store is currently closed.
TC-413.title: Simultaneous resolution of multiple unserviceability reasons

steps.step: Verify the initial state of the store
steps.result: The store is unserviceable with multiple reasons (NO_PICKERS, STORE_CLOSED) logged in StoreUnserviceabilityLog and StoreUnserviceabilityReason tables.

steps.step:
Simultaneously trigger the resolution of all unserviceability reasons:
- Have a picker mark in (resolves NO_PICKERS)
- Open the store (resolves STORE_CLOSED)
steps.result:
1. The system handles the concurrent resolutions correctly.
2. Both NO_PICKERS and STORE_CLOSED reasons are removed from StoreUnserviceabilityReason table.
3. A single new entry is added to StoreUnserviceabilityLog with the correct timestamp, consolidating both reason resolutions.
4. The store becomes serviceable.

steps.step: Verify the store status immediately after the simultaneous resolutions
steps.result:
1. The store is serviceable.
2. StoreUnserviceabilityLog contains a single entry for the simultaneous resolution with the correct timestamp.
3. StoreUnserviceabilityReason table is empty for this store.

steps.step: Check the CX App integration
steps.result: The CX App no longer shows 'Temporarily Unavailable' for this store.

steps.step: Verify the StoreRunningMetrics
steps.result: StoreRunningMetrics table accurately reflects the current state with one active picker and the store being open.

TC-414.description: Set up a store with multiple unserviceability reasons. Resolve some, but not all, reasons and verify that the store remains unserviceable. Ensure the system accurately logs the resolved reasons while maintaining the unserviceable status due to remaining reasons.
TC-414.preconditions:
1. A store is set up in the system.
2. The store has multiple unserviceability reasons active (e.g., NO_PICKERS, STORE_CLOSED, TECHNICAL_ISSUE).
3. The StoreUnserviceabilityLog and StoreUnserviceabilityReason tables are properly configured and accessible.
4. The system is set up to handle multiple unserviceability reasons independently.
5. There are no active pickers in the store.
6. The store is currently closed.
7. There is an ongoing technical issue affecting the store.
TC-414.title: Partial resolution of unserviceability reasons

steps.step: Verify the initial state of the store
steps.result: The store is unserviceable with multiple reasons (NO_PICKERS, STORE_CLOSED, TECHNICAL_ISSUE) logged in StoreUnserviceabilityLog and StoreUnserviceabilityReason tables.

steps.step: Resolve the NO_PICKERS reason by having a picker mark in
steps.result:
1. The NO_PICKERS reason is removed from StoreUnserviceabilityReason table.
2. A new entry is added to StoreUnserviceabilityLog with the correct timestamp and reason for resolution.
3. The store remains unserviceable due to the STORE_CLOSED and TECHNICAL_ISSUE reasons.

steps.step: Resolve the STORE_CLOSED reason by opening the store
steps.result:
1. The STORE_CLOSED reason is removed from StoreUnserviceabilityReason table.
2. A new entry is added to StoreUnserviceabilityLog with the correct timestamp and reason for resolution.
3. The store remains unserviceable due to the TECHNICAL_ISSUE reason.

steps.step: Verify the store status after resolving two out of three reasons
steps.result:
1. The store is still unserviceable.
2. StoreUnserviceabilityLog shows the resolution of NO_PICKERS and STORE_CLOSED reasons.
3. StoreUnserviceabilityReason table still contains the TECHNICAL_ISSUE reason for this store.

steps.step: Check the CX App integration
steps.result: The CX App still shows 'Temporarily Unavailable' for this store.

steps.step: Verify the StoreRunningMetrics
steps.result: StoreRunningMetrics table accurately reflects the current state with one active picker and the store being open, but still unserviceable due to the technical issue.

TC-415.description: Make a store unserviceable due to NO_PICKERS. Simulate multiple pickers performing actions simultaneously (e.g., one marking in, another returning from break). Verify that the system correctly resolves the NO_PICKERS reason and updates the store status, handling the concurrent picker actions accurately.
TC-415.preconditions:
1. A store is set up in the system.
2. The store is currently unserviceable due to NO_PICKERS reason.
3. The StoreUnserviceabilityLog and StoreUnserviceabilityReason tables are properly configured and accessible.
4. The system is capable of handling concurrent operations.
5. There are multiple pickers associated with the store, but all are currently inactive.
6. At least one picker is on a break.
TC-415.title: Recovery from NO_PICKERS reason with multiple picker actions

steps.step: Verify the initial state of the store
steps.result: The store is unserviceable with NO_PICKERS reason logged in StoreUnserviceabilityLog and StoreUnserviceabilityReason tables.

steps.step:
Simultaneously trigger multiple picker actions:
- Have one picker mark in
- Have another picker return from break
steps.result:
1. The system handles the concurrent picker actions correctly.
2. The NO_PICKERS reason is removed from StoreUnserviceabilityReason table.
3. A new entry is added to StoreUnserviceabilityLog with the correct timestamp, indicating the resolution of NO_PICKERS reason.
4. The store becomes serviceable.
5. StoreRunningMetrics is updated to reflect two active pickers.

steps.step: Verify the store status immediately after the simultaneous picker actions
steps.result:
1. The store is serviceable.
2. StoreUnserviceabilityLog contains an entry for the resolution of NO_PICKERS reason with the correct timestamp.
3. StoreUnserviceabilityReason table is empty for this store.

steps.step: Check the CX App integration
steps.result: The CX App no longer shows 'Temporarily Unavailable' for this store.

steps.step: Verify the StoreRunningMetrics
steps.result: StoreRunningMetrics table accurately reflects the current state with two active pickers.

steps.step: Attempt to trigger a duplicate mark-in event for one of the pickers
steps.result: The system rejects the duplicate mark-in event and maintains the correct picker count.

TC-407.description: Attempt to create a new unserviceability log when the store already has an open log. Verify that the system rejects the operation and logs an error, maintaining data integrity by preventing duplicate logs for the same store.
TC-407.preconditions:
1. A store exists in the system
2. The store currently has an open unserviceability log
3. The system is configured to log errors
4. Access to StoreUnserviceabilityLog table is available
TC-407.title: Prevention of duplicate unserviceability logs

steps.step: Attempt to create a new unserviceability log for the store
steps.result: The system should reject the operation

steps.step: Check the system logs
steps.result: An error should be logged indicating the attempt to create a duplicate unserviceability log

steps.step: Verify the StoreUnserviceabilityLog table
steps.result: Only the original unserviceability log should exist for the store, with no new entries added

steps.step: Attempt to create a new unserviceability log with a different reason
steps.result: The system should still reject the operation and log an error

steps.step: Close the existing unserviceability log for the store
steps.result: The system should successfully close the log

steps.step: Attempt to create a new unserviceability log for the store
steps.result: The system should now allow the creation of a new log

TC-408.description: Try to add the same unserviceability reason (e.g., NO_PICKERS) multiple times for a store. Confirm that the system rejects the duplicate reason and logs an error, ensuring each reason is recorded only once per unserviceability period.
TC-408.preconditions:
1. A store exists in the system
2. The store has an open unserviceability log
3. The unserviceability log has the NO_PICKERS reason already added
4. The system is configured to log errors
5. Access to StoreUnserviceabilityReason table is available
