steps.result: No significant memory leaks are detected. The app demonstrates efficient memory cleanup and state restoration across interruptions.

steps.step: Perform a final force close and relaunch of the app.
steps.result: The app relaunches with normal memory usage and correctly recognizes the completed permission flow state.

TC-285.description: Measure and validate the app launch time for a fresh installation. The app should launch within acceptable time limits and smoothly transition to the language selection screen without delays.
TC-285.preconditions:
1. A test device with the app not installed
2. The latest version of the app available for installation
3. A stopwatch or performance measurement tool ready
4. Network connectivity available
TC-285.title: Fresh install launch time measurement

steps.step: Install the app on the test device
steps.result: The app is successfully installed

steps.step: Start the performance measurement tool
steps.result: The tool is ready to measure launch time

steps.step: Launch the app for the first time
steps.result: The app begins to launch

steps.step: Observe and record the launch time until the language selection screen appears
steps.result: The launch time is recorded and is within acceptable limits (e.g., under 3 seconds)

steps.step: Verify the smooth transition to the language selection screen
steps.result: The language selection screen appears without any visible delays or glitches

steps.step: Repeat steps 3-5 at least 3 times to get an average launch time
steps.result: Multiple launch times are recorded and averaged

steps.step: Compare the average launch time with the acceptable time limit defined in the app's performance requirements
steps.result: The average launch time is within the acceptable range

TC-286.description: Evaluate app launch performance immediately after an update. Verify that the app launches efficiently, maintains user data, and resumes at the correct state in the permission flow if previously interrupted.
TC-286.preconditions:
1. A test device with the current version of the app installed and set up (user logged in, permissions granted)
2. An update for the app available
3. The app was previously interrupted during the permission flow
4. A stopwatch or performance measurement tool ready
TC-286.title: Launch performance after app update

steps.step: Update the app to the latest version
steps.result: The app is successfully updated

steps.step: Start the performance measurement tool
steps.result: The tool is ready to measure launch time

steps.step: Launch the updated app
steps.result: The app begins to launch

steps.step: Observe and record the launch time
steps.result: The launch time is recorded and is within acceptable limits

steps.step: Verify that the app maintains user data (login state, saved addresses)
steps.result: User data is preserved after the update

steps.step: Check if the app resumes at the correct state in the permission flow
steps.result: The app correctly resumes the permission flow where it was previously interrupted

steps.step: Repeat steps 3-6 at least 3 times to ensure consistent performance
steps.result: Multiple launch times are recorded and performance is consistent

steps.step: Compare the average launch time with pre-update performance benchmarks
steps.result: The post-update launch performance is comparable to or better than pre-update performance

TC-287.description: Test app launch performance and behavior after a reinstall. The app should launch efficiently and handle the reinstall scenario appropriately, either by resuming the previous state or starting a fresh onboarding process.
TC-287.preconditions:
1. A test device that previously had the app installed and set up
2. The app uninstalled from the device
3. The latest version of the app available for reinstallation
4. A stopwatch or performance measurement tool ready
TC-287.title: Reinstall launch behavior and performance

steps.step: Reinstall the app on the test device
steps.result: The app is successfully reinstalled

steps.step: Start the performance measurement tool
steps.result: The tool is ready to measure launch time

steps.step: Launch the reinstalled app
steps.result: The app begins to launch

steps.step: Observe and record the launch time
steps.result: The launch time is recorded and is within acceptable limits

steps.step: Verify if the app resumes the previous state or starts a fresh onboarding process
steps.result: The app either resumes the previous state or starts fresh onboarding appropriately

steps.step: If resuming previous state, check if user data and permissions are retained
steps.result: User data and permissions are handled correctly based on the app's design for reinstalls

steps.step: If starting fresh, verify that the language selection screen appears
steps.result: The language selection screen is displayed without issues

steps.step: Repeat steps 3-7 at least 3 times to ensure consistent behavior
steps.result: The app demonstrates consistent launch behavior and performance across multiple launches

steps.step: Compare the launch performance with fresh install and normal launch benchmarks
steps.result: The reinstall launch performance is comparable to fresh install or normal launch scenarios

TC-288.description: Measure launch performance for users with previously granted permissions and saved addresses. The app should efficiently load user data and bypass unnecessary permission prompts.
TC-288.preconditions:
1. A test device with the app installed and fully set up
2. User logged in with an existing account
3. All permissions (location, GPS, notifications) previously granted
4. At least one address saved in the app
5. A stopwatch or performance measurement tool ready
TC-288.title: Launch time with saved permissions and addresses

steps.step: Ensure the app is not running in the background
steps.result: The app is fully closed and not in recent apps

steps.step: Start the performance measurement tool
steps.result: The tool is ready to measure launch time

steps.step: Launch the app
steps.result: The app begins to launch

steps.step: Observe and record the launch time until the main screen or home feed is fully loaded
steps.result: The launch time is recorded and is within acceptable limits

steps.step: Verify that the app bypasses unnecessary permission prompts
steps.result: No permission prompts are shown for already granted permissions

steps.step: Check if saved addresses are immediately available
steps.result: Saved addresses are loaded and accessible without delay

steps.step: Verify that the user's logged-in state is maintained
steps.result: The user remains logged in without need for re-authentication

steps.step: Repeat steps 3-7 at least 3 times to get an average launch time
steps.result: Multiple launch times are recorded and averaged

steps.step: Compare the average launch time with the app's performance benchmarks for returning users
steps.result: The launch time for users with saved data is faster or equal to the benchmark for returning users

TC-289.description: Compare and validate app launch times for cold start (app not in memory) versus warm start (app in background) scenarios. Ensure consistent performance and correct state restoration in both cases.
TC-289.preconditions:
1. A test device with the app installed and set up
2. User logged in with permissions granted
3. A stopwatch or performance measurement tool ready
4. The ability to force close the app and clear it from recent apps
5. The ability to put the app in the background
TC-289.title: Cold start vs warm start launch performance

steps.step: Perform a cold start test:
steps.result: Cold start test initiated

steps.step: Force close the app and clear it from recent apps
steps.result: The app is fully closed and not in memory

steps.step: Start the performance measurement tool
steps.result: The tool is ready to measure launch time

steps.step: Launch the app
steps.result: The app begins to launch from a cold start

steps.step: Record the time until the app is fully loaded and interactive
steps.result: Cold start launch time is recorded

steps.step: Verify correct state restoration and functionality
steps.result: The app restores to the correct state and is fully functional

steps.step: Perform a warm start test:
steps.result: Warm start test initiated

steps.step: Put the app in the background for at least 30 seconds
steps.result: The app is in the background state

steps.step: Start the performance measurement tool
steps.result: The tool is ready to measure launch time

steps.step: Bring the app back to the foreground
steps.result: The app begins to launch from a warm start

steps.step: Record the time until the app is fully responsive
steps.result: Warm start launch time is recorded

steps.step: Verify correct state preservation and functionality
steps.result: The app preserves its previous state and is fully functional

steps.step: Repeat steps 1-12 at least 3 times each to get average times
steps.result: Multiple cold and warm start times are recorded and averaged

steps.step: Compare cold start and warm start average launch times
steps.result: Warm start times are consistently faster than cold start times

steps.step: Verify both cold and warm start times meet performance requirements
steps.result: Both launch scenarios meet or exceed the app's performance benchmarks

TC-280.description: Verify that the app correctly detects the absence of GPS hardware, skips the GPS permission prompt, and proceeds directly to notification permissions while maintaining full functionality using alternative location methods.
TC-280.preconditions:
1. The app is installed on a device without GPS hardware.
2. The app is in its initial state after a fresh install.
3. The device has an active internet connection.
4. Location services are enabled on the device (if applicable).
5. The user has not granted any permissions to the app yet.
TC-280.title: App functionality on device without GPS hardware

steps.step: Launch the app for the first time.
steps.result: The app opens and displays the language selection screen.

steps.step: Complete the language selection process.
steps.result: The app proceeds to the mobile number entry screen.

steps.step: Enter a valid mobile number and complete the OTP verification process.
steps.result: The app successfully verifies the OTP and proceeds to the signup completion screen.

steps.step: Complete the signup process.
steps.result: The app detects the absence of GPS hardware and skips the GPS permission prompt.

steps.step: Observe the next permission request.
steps.result: The app directly requests notification permissions, bypassing any GPS-related prompts.

steps.step: Grant notification permissions.
steps.result: The app acknowledges the permission and proceeds to the next step in the flow (e.g., store assignment or home screen).

steps.step: Attempt to use location-based features within the app.
steps.result: The app uses alternative location methods (e.g., network-based location) or prompts for manual address entry.

steps.step: Navigate through various sections of the app that typically require GPS.
steps.result: All sections of the app remain functional, using alternative location methods or manual input where necessary.

steps.step: Check the app settings or info page.
steps.result: The app correctly reports the device's lack of GPS hardware or indicates that it's using alternative location methods.

steps.step: Force close the app and relaunch it.
steps.result: The app maintains its state and continues to function without attempting to access non-existent GPS hardware.

TC-281.description: Test app behavior on devices with limited location accuracy capabilities. The app should detect this limitation, treat it as location not given, and provide appropriate alternative flows or manual address entry options.
TC-281.preconditions:
1. The app is installed on a device with limited location accuracy capabilities.
2. The app is in its initial state after a fresh install.
3. The device has an active internet connection.
4. Location services are enabled on the device.
5. The user has not granted any permissions to the app yet.
TC-281.title: Performance on devices with limited location accuracy

steps.step: Launch the app for the first time.
steps.result: The app opens and displays the language selection screen.

steps.step: Complete the language selection process and proceed through the signup flow (mobile number entry, OTP verification).
steps.result: The app successfully completes the signup process and moves to the location permission request.

steps.step: When prompted, grant location permission to the app.
steps.result: The app detects the limited location accuracy and treats it as if location was not given.

steps.step: Observe the app's response to the limited location accuracy.
steps.result: The app displays a dialog or screen indicating that precise location is unavailable and offers alternative options (e.g., manual address entry).

steps.step: Attempt to use the 'Use Current Location' feature.
steps.result: The app informs the user that precise location is unavailable and suggests manual address entry.

steps.step: Enter an address manually using the provided option.
steps.result: The app accepts the manual address entry and proceeds with the flow.

steps.step: Navigate to different sections of the app that typically use location data.
steps.result: The app consistently uses the manually entered address or provides options for updating location information.

steps.step: Check the app settings or location preferences.
steps.result: The app correctly indicates that precise location is unavailable or using approximate location.

steps.step: Force close the app and relaunch it.
steps.result: The app remembers the manually entered address and maintains appropriate functionality.

steps.step: If applicable, try to update the device's location settings to allow for more precise location (if possible on the device).
steps.result: The app detects the change and prompts the user to retry location permission or automatically updates to use more accurate location data.

TC-282.description: Evaluate app performance when device location services are disabled at the OS level. The app should detect this, show appropriate dialogs, and provide guidance on enabling required services.
TC-282.preconditions:
1. The app is installed on a device with location services disabled at the OS level.
2. The app is in its initial state after a fresh install.
3. The device has an active internet connection.
4. The user has not granted any permissions to the app yet.
TC-282.title: Functionality with location services disabled at OS level

steps.step: Launch the app for the first time.
steps.result: The app opens and displays the language selection screen.

steps.step: Complete the language selection process and proceed through the signup flow (mobile number entry, OTP verification).
steps.result: The app successfully completes the signup process and attempts to request location permission.

steps.step: Observe the app's behavior when it detects that location services are disabled.
steps.result: The app displays a dialog informing the user that location services are disabled at the OS level.

steps.step: Check if the dialog provides guidance on enabling location services.
steps.result: The dialog includes clear instructions or a button to open the device's location settings.

steps.step: Attempt to proceed without enabling location services.
steps.result: The app provides an alternative flow, such as manual address entry or limited functionality mode.

steps.step: Enable location services at the OS level without closing the app.
steps.result: The app detects the change and prompts the user to grant location permission.

steps.step: Grant location permission to the app.
steps.result: The app acknowledges the permission and proceeds with location-based features.

steps.step: Disable location services again at the OS level.
steps.result: The app detects the change and reverts to the alternative flow or displays a notification about limited functionality.

steps.step: Navigate through various sections of the app that typically require location services.
steps.result: The app consistently provides appropriate messaging and alternative options when location-based features are accessed.

steps.step: Force close the app and relaunch it with location services still disabled.
steps.result: The app remembers the location services state and maintains appropriate functionality or limitations.

TC-283.description: Verify app functionality on devices that do not support push notifications. The app should skip notification permission requests and adapt its communication strategy accordingly.
TC-283.preconditions:
1. The app is installed on a device that does not support push notifications.
2. The app is in its initial state after a fresh install.
3. The device has an active internet connection.
4. The user has completed the initial signup process.
TC-283.title: Adaptation to devices with no notification capability

steps.step: Launch the app and complete the initial setup (language selection, mobile number entry, OTP verification).
steps.result: The app successfully completes the signup process and moves to the permission request phase.

steps.step: Observe the app's behavior during the permission request flow.
steps.result: The app skips the notification permission request and proceeds to the next step in the flow (e.g., location permissions or home screen).

steps.step: Navigate to the app settings or notification preferences within the app.
steps.result: The app either doesn't show notification settings or clearly indicates that notifications are not supported on this device.

steps.step: Perform actions that would typically trigger a notification (e.g., receiving a message, completing an order).
steps.result: The app uses alternative methods to communicate important information, such as in-app messages or email notifications.

steps.step: Check the app's communication settings or preferences.
steps.result: The app offers alternative communication channels (e.g., email, SMS) for important updates.

steps.step: Attempt to manually enable notifications for the app through device settings (if applicable).
steps.result: The option is either not available or has no effect on the app's behavior.

steps.step: Use the app for an extended period, performing various actions that would typically generate notifications.
steps.result: The app consistently uses alternative communication methods and does not attempt to send push notifications.

steps.step: Force close the app and relaunch it.
steps.result: The app maintains its adapted communication strategy without attempting to request or use push notifications.

steps.step: If possible, transfer the app to a device that does support notifications.
steps.result: The app detects the new capabilities and offers to enable push notifications (this step may not be feasible in all testing environments).

steps.step: Review any documentation or help sections within the app related to notifications or updates.
steps.result: The app provides clear information about its communication methods, acknowledging the lack of push notification support where applicable.

TC-284.description: Test app performance and stability on low-end devices with limited processing power and memory. Ensure smooth functionality during permission flows and animations without crashes or excessive lag.
TC-284.preconditions:
1. The app is installed on a low-end device with limited processing power and memory.
2. The app is in its initial state after a fresh install.
3. The device has an active internet connection.
4. The device has minimal background processes running to simulate typical user conditions.
TC-284.title: Performance on low-end devices with limited resources

steps.step: Launch the app for the first time.
steps.result: The app opens within an acceptable time frame without freezing or crashing.

steps.step: Complete the language selection process.
steps.result: The language selection screen responds to user input without significant lag.

steps.step: Proceed through the signup flow (mobile number entry, OTP verification).
steps.result: Each step of the signup process completes without excessive delay or app instability.

steps.step: When prompted, quickly grant or deny permissions (location, GPS, notifications) in succession.
steps.result: The app handles rapid permission changes without crashing or missing any permission states.

steps.step: Observe any animations during the permission flow (e.g., location marker animation).
steps.result: Animations play smoothly without causing the app to freeze or consume excessive resources.

steps.step: Rapidly navigate between different sections of the app immediately after setup.
steps.result: The app responds to quick navigation without significant lag or missed screen transitions.

steps.step: Perform resource-intensive actions such as loading maps or location-based features.
steps.result: The app manages resource allocation effectively, possibly showing loading indicators but not crashing or becoming unresponsive.

steps.step: Minimize and reopen the app multiple times in quick succession.
steps.result: The app resumes quickly each time without loss of state or crashes.

steps.step: Enable any background processes or features the app offers.
steps.result: Background features function without noticeably impacting the device's overall performance or the app's responsiveness.

steps.step: Leave the app running in the background for an extended period, then return to it.
steps.result: The app resumes quickly and maintains its state without crashes or data loss.

steps.step: Perform a series of rapid interactions across various app features (e.g., searching, scrolling, entering data).
steps.result: The app remains responsive and stable throughout the rapid interaction sequence.

steps.step: Check the app's resource consumption in the device's settings or developer options.
steps.result: The app's memory and CPU usage remain within reasonable limits for a low-end device.

TC-275.description: Verify app behavior when location permission is revoked after initial grant. The app should detect the change, update UI accordingly, and provide appropriate options to re-request permission or use alternative flows.
TC-275.preconditions:
1. The app is installed and set up on the device.
2. The user has completed the initial onboarding process.
3. Location permission has been initially granted to the app.
4. The app is in a state where it's actively using or displaying location-based features.
TC-275.title: Location permission revocation after initial grant

steps.step: Open the device's Settings app.
steps.result: The Settings app opens successfully.

steps.step: Navigate to the app's permission settings.
steps.result: The app's permission settings are displayed.

steps.step: Revoke the location permission for the app.
steps.result: The location permission is successfully revoked.

steps.step: Return to the app without closing it (if it's in the background).
steps.result: The app is brought to the foreground.

steps.step: Observe the app's behavior and UI updates.
steps.result: The app should detect the permission change and update its UI accordingly. This may include showing a message about the revoked permission and providing options to re-request permission or use alternative flows.

steps.step: Attempt to use a location-based feature in the app.
steps.result: The app should prompt the user to grant location permission again or offer an alternative method (e.g., manual address entry).

steps.step: If prompted, grant the location permission again.
steps.result: The app should resume normal location-based functionality.

steps.step: Verify that the app maintains correct state and functionality after permission is re-granted.
steps.result: The app should return to its previous state with location features working correctly.

TC-276.description: Test app's response when user changes location permission from precise to approximate. The app should treat this as location not given and trigger the appropriate fallback flow.
TC-276.preconditions:
1. The app is installed and set up on the device.
2. The user has completed the initial onboarding process.
3. Precise location permission has been initially granted to the app.
4. The app is in a state where it's actively using or displaying location-based features.
TC-276.title: Transition from precise to approximate location

steps.step: Open the device's Settings app.
steps.result: The Settings app opens successfully.

steps.step: Navigate to the app's location permission settings.
steps.result: The app's location permission settings are displayed.

steps.step: Change the location permission from precise to approximate.
steps.result: The location permission is successfully changed to approximate.

steps.step: Return to the app without closing it (if it's in the background).
steps.result: The app is brought to the foreground.

steps.step: Observe the app's behavior and UI updates.
steps.result: The app should detect the permission change and treat it as if location is not given. It should update its UI accordingly, possibly showing a message about the need for precise location.

steps.step: Attempt to use a location-based feature in the app.
steps.result: The app should trigger the appropriate fallback flow, such as prompting for precise location permission or offering manual address entry.

steps.step: If prompted, grant precise location permission.
steps.result: The app should resume normal location-based functionality with precise location.

steps.step: Verify that the app maintains correct state and functionality after permission is updated.
steps.result: The app should return to its previous state with precise location features working correctly.

TC-277.description: Evaluate app behavior when GPS permission is revoked after initial grant. The app should detect the change and adapt its functionality, possibly reverting to non-GPS location methods or prompting for re-enablement.
TC-277.preconditions:
1. The app is installed and set up on the device.
2. The user has completed the initial onboarding process.
3. GPS permission has been initially granted to the app.
4. The app is in a state where it's actively using GPS-based features.
TC-277.title: GPS permission revocation impact

steps.step: Open the device's Settings app.
steps.result: The Settings app opens successfully.

steps.step: Navigate to the device's location settings.
steps.result: The device's location settings are displayed.

steps.step: Disable GPS or change location mode to a non-GPS option (e.g., Wi-Fi only).
steps.result: GPS is successfully disabled or the location mode is changed.

steps.step: Return to the app without closing it (if it's in the background).
steps.result: The app is brought to the foreground.

steps.step: Observe the app's behavior and UI updates.
steps.result: The app should detect the GPS permission change and adapt its functionality. It may show a message about GPS being unavailable.

steps.step: Attempt to use a GPS-dependent feature in the app.
steps.result: The app should either revert to non-GPS location methods (if available) or prompt the user to re-enable GPS.

steps.step: If prompted, re-enable GPS through the app's UI (if possible).
steps.result: The app should guide the user to enable GPS in system settings.

steps.step: Verify that the app resumes normal GPS-based functionality after GPS is re-enabled.
steps.result: The app should return to its previous state with GPS features working correctly.

TC-278.description: Assess app's handling of notification permission changes post-onboarding. The app should detect the change and adjust its behavior accordingly, possibly re-prompting or adapting its communication strategy.
TC-278.preconditions:
1. The app is installed and set up on the device.
2. The user has completed the initial onboarding process, including granting notification permissions.
3. The app has been used for some time after onboarding.
4. The app relies on notifications for some of its features or communications.
TC-278.title: Notification permission state change after onboarding

steps.step: Open the device's Settings app.
steps.result: The Settings app opens successfully.

steps.step: Navigate to the app's notification settings.
steps.result: The app's notification settings are displayed.

steps.step: Disable notifications for the app.
steps.result: Notifications are successfully disabled for the app.

steps.step: Return to the app and use it for a while, engaging with features that typically trigger notifications.
steps.result: The app should function normally, but no notifications should be received.

steps.step: Check if the app detects the notification permission change and displays any in-app messages or prompts.
steps.result: The app may display a message about notifications being disabled and offer an option to re-enable them.

steps.step: If prompted, attempt to re-enable notifications through the app's UI.
steps.result: The app should guide the user to enable notifications in system settings.

steps.step: Re-enable notifications for the app in the system settings.
steps.result: Notifications are successfully re-enabled for the app.

steps.step: Return to the app and verify that notifications are working correctly.
steps.result: The app should resume sending notifications for relevant events or features.

TC-279.description: Test app's response when OS-level location services are disabled after permissions were initially granted. The app should detect this change and provide appropriate UI and functionality adjustments.
TC-279.preconditions:
1. The app is installed and set up on the device.
2. The user has completed the initial onboarding process.
3. All necessary permissions (location, GPS) have been initially granted to the app.
4. The app is in a state where it's actively using location-based features.
TC-279.title: OS-level location service disabling impact

steps.step: Open the device's Settings app.
steps.result: The Settings app opens successfully.

steps.step: Navigate to the device's location services settings.
steps.result: The device's location services settings are displayed.

steps.step: Disable the OS-level location services.
steps.result: Location services are successfully disabled at the OS level.

steps.step: Return to the app without closing it (if it's in the background).
steps.result: The app is brought to the foreground.

steps.step: Observe the app's behavior and UI updates.
steps.result: The app should detect that location services are disabled and update its UI accordingly. This may include showing a message about location services being unavailable.

steps.step: Attempt to use a location-based feature in the app.
steps.result: The app should prompt the user to enable location services or offer alternative functionality that doesn't require location.

steps.step: If prompted, attempt to enable location services through the app's UI.
steps.result: The app should guide the user to enable location services in system settings.

steps.step: Enable location services in the system settings and return to the app.
steps.result: The app should detect that location services are re-enabled and resume normal location-based functionality.

steps.step: Verify that the app maintains correct state and functionality after location services are re-enabled.
steps.result: The app should return to its previous state with location features working correctly.

TC-270.description: Verify that the app maintains correct state when backgrounded and foregrounded while the location permission prompt is active. Upon return, the app should resume at the correct step in the permission flow.
TC-270.preconditions:
1. The app is freshly installed on a device.
2. The user has completed the initial setup steps (language selection, mobile number entry, OTP verification).
3. The app is at the point where it is about to show the location permission prompt.
TC-270.title: Background transition during location permission prompt

steps.step: Launch the app and complete the initial setup process (language selection, mobile number entry, OTP verification).
steps.result: The app should progress through the initial setup steps successfully.

steps.step: When the location permission prompt appears, do not interact with it. Instead, immediately background the app (e.g., press the home button or switch to another app).
steps.result: The app should go into the background without crashing. The location permission prompt should disappear from view.

steps.step: Wait for approximately 30 seconds.
steps.result: The app should remain in the background without any visible activity.

steps.step: Bring the app back to the foreground (e.g., tap on the app icon or use the recent apps menu).
steps.result: The app should resume and the location permission prompt should reappear, maintaining the correct state in the permission flow.

steps.step: Interact with the location permission prompt (either allow or deny).
steps.result:
The app should respond appropriately to the user's choice:
- If allowed: proceed to the next step in the permission flow (GPS or notification, depending on the variant).
- If denied: show the context page with 'Allow Access' and 'Select Address Manually' options (for variants that include this step).

steps.step: Complete the remaining steps in the permission flow (GPS, notification, etc.).
steps.result: The app should guide the user through the remaining permission steps without any issues, maintaining the correct sequence based on the app variant (Current State, Variant A, B, or C).

steps.step: Verify that the final state of the app is correct (e.g., store assigned, main screen displayed).
steps.result: The app should reach its final state correctly, with all permissions handled and the user able to use the app's main functionality.

TC-271.description: Test app's ability to restore state correctly when killed and relaunched during the GPS permission flow. The app should resume at the appropriate step without losing user progress.
TC-271.preconditions:
1. The app is installed on a device with GPS capability.
2. The user has completed the initial setup and granted location permission.
3. The app is at the point where it is about to show the GPS permission prompt.
TC-271.title: App kill and relaunch during GPS permission flow

steps.step: Launch the app and progress through the initial setup and location permission grant.
steps.result: The app should successfully complete the initial setup and location permission should be granted.

steps.step: When the GPS permission prompt appears, do not interact with it. Instead, immediately force close the app (kill the app process).
steps.result: The app should close completely without any error messages.

steps.step: Relaunch the app from the device's home screen or app drawer.
steps.result: The app should start up normally without crashing.

steps.step: Observe the first screen that appears after the app relaunch.
steps.result: The app should resume at the Select Delivery Location after all initial login process, maintaining the user's progress in the permission flow.

steps.step: Interact with the GPS permission prompt (either allow or deny).
steps.result: The app should respond appropriately to the user's choice: - If allowed: proceed to the next step in the permission flow (typically notification permission). - If denied: show appropriate UI for GPS-disabled state (e.g., option to enable GPS or use app with limited functionality).

steps.step: Complete the remaining steps in the permission flow (notification, etc.).
steps.result: The app should guide the user through the remaining permission steps without any issues, maintaining the correct sequence based on the app variant.

steps.step: Verify that the app reaches its final state correctly (e.g., store assigned, main screen displayed).
steps.result: The app should reach its final state correctly, with all permissions handled and the user able to use the app's main functionality.

TC-272.description: Validate that the app preserves the state of the context page (with 'Allow Access' and 'Select Address Manually' options) when backgrounded and foregrounded. The correct options and animations should be maintained.
TC-272.preconditions:
1. The app is installed on the device.
2. The user has completed the initial setup steps.
3. The user has denied the location permission, triggering the display of the context page with 'Allow Access' and 'Select Address Manually' options.
TC-272.title: Background transition during context page display

steps.step: Launch the app and progress through the initial setup until the location permission is denied, showing the context page.
steps.result: The context page should be displayed with 'Allow Access' and 'Select Address Manually' options. The location marker animation should be visible, moving up and down.

steps.step: Observe the context page for a few seconds, ensuring the animation is playing.
steps.result: The location marker should animate, moving up and down two times.

steps.step: Without interacting with the options, background the app (e.g., press the home button or switch to another app).
steps.result: The app should go into the background without crashing. The context page should disappear from view.

steps.step: Wait for approximately 1 minute.
steps.result: The app should remain in the background without any visible activity.

steps.step: Bring the app back to the foreground (e.g., tap on the app icon or use the recent apps menu).
steps.result: The app should resume, displaying the context page with 'Allow Access' and 'Select Address Manually' options. The animation should restart or continue from where it left off.

steps.step: Interact with one of the options ('Allow Access' or 'Select Address Manually').
steps.result:
The app should respond appropriately to the chosen option:
- If 'Allow Access': The location permission prompt should reappear.
- If 'Select Address Manually': The manual address entry screen should appear.

steps.step: Complete the remaining steps in the permission or address entry flow.
steps.result: The app should guide the user through the remaining steps without any issues, maintaining the correct sequence based on the user's choice and app variant.

steps.step: Verify that the app reaches its final state correctly (e.g., store assigned, main screen displayed).
steps.result: The app should reach its final state correctly, with all permissions handled or address entered, and the user able to use the app's main functionality.

TC-273.description: Ensure the app maintains the correct state when backgrounded and foregrounded while the notification permission prompt is active. The app should resume the permission flow accurately.
TC-273.preconditions:
1. The app is installed on the device.
2. The user has completed the initial setup steps.
3. The user has granted location and GPS permissions (if applicable to the app variant).
4. The app is at the point where it is about to show the notification permission prompt.
TC-273.title: State persistence during notification permission prompt

steps.step: Launch the app and progress through the initial setup, granting location and GPS permissions as required.
steps.result: The app should successfully complete the initial setup and previous permission grants.

steps.step: When the notification permission prompt appears, do not interact with it. Instead, immediately background the app (e.g., press the home button or switch to another app).
steps.result: The app should go into the background without crashing. The notification permission prompt should disappear from view.

steps.step: Wait for approximately 45 seconds.
steps.result: The app should remain in the background without any visible activity.

steps.step: Bring the app back to the foreground (e.g., tap on the app icon or use the recent apps menu).
steps.result: The app should resume and the notification permission prompt should reappear, maintaining the correct state in the permission flow.

steps.step: Interact with the notification permission prompt (either allow or deny).
steps.result:
The app should respond appropriately to the user's choice:
- If allowed: proceed to the next step in the app flow (e.g., store assignment or main screen).
- If denied: show appropriate UI for notification-disabled state or proceed to the next step based on the app's design.

steps.step: Complete any remaining steps in the app's onboarding or setup process.
steps.result: The app should guide the user through any remaining steps without issues, maintaining the correct sequence based on the app variant.

steps.step: Verify that the app reaches its final state correctly (e.g., store assigned, main screen displayed).
steps.result: The app should reach its final state correctly, with all permissions handled and the user able to use the app's main functionality.

steps.step: If possible, check the app's notification settings in the device's system settings.
steps.result: The notification settings for the app should reflect the user's choice made during the permission prompt.

TC-274.description: Verify that the app preserves the state of the address bottom sheet, including keyboard visibility and saved addresses, when backgrounded and foregrounded during user interaction.
TC-274.preconditions:
1. The app is installed on the device.
2. The user has completed the initial setup and granted necessary permissions.
3. The user has reached the point where the address bottom sheet is displayed.
4. The app has a mix of users with and without saved addresses to test both scenarios.
TC-274.title: Background transition during address bottom sheet interaction

steps.step: Launch the app and progress through the initial setup and permission grants to reach the address bottom sheet.
steps.result: The address bottom sheet should be displayed. For users with saved addresses, it should show the list of addresses without the keyboard. For users without saved addresses, it should show the keyboard for address entry.

steps.step: For users with saved addresses, scroll through the list of addresses. For users without saved addresses, start typing an address.
steps.result: The bottom sheet should respond correctly to user interactions, showing smooth scrolling for saved addresses or appropriate keyboard behavior for address entry.

steps.step: Without completing the address selection or entry, background the app (e.g., press the home button or switch to another app).
steps.result: The app should go into the background without crashing. The address bottom sheet should disappear from view.

steps.step: Wait for approximately 30 seconds.
steps.result: The app should remain in the background without any visible activity.

steps.step: Bring the app back to the foreground (e.g., tap on the app icon or use the recent apps menu).
steps.result: The app should resume, displaying the address bottom sheet in the same state as before backgrounding. For users with saved addresses, the list should be visible. For users without saved addresses, the keyboard and any partially entered address should be preserved.

steps.step: Continue interacting with the address bottom sheet (select a saved address or complete address entry).
steps.result:
The app should respond appropriately to the user's actions:
- For saved address selection: The chosen address should be highlighted or set as the current address.
- For manual address entry: The app should accept the entered address and proceed to the next step.

steps.step: Complete any remaining steps in the app's setup or onboarding process.
steps.result: The app should guide the user through any remaining steps without issues, using the selected or entered address as appropriate.

steps.step: Verify that the app reaches its final state correctly (e.g., store assigned, main screen displayed) with the correct address information.
steps.result: The app should reach its final state correctly, with the chosen or entered address properly associated with the user's account or current session.

steps.step: If possible, navigate to a section of the app where the user's address is displayed or used.
steps.result: The address information should be correctly displayed or utilized, matching the selection or entry made during the bottom sheet interaction.

TC-265.description: Verify app stability when user rapidly toggles location permission on/off during the onboarding process. The app should handle quick allow/deny actions without crashes or missed screens, maintaining the correct flow state.
TC-265.preconditions:
1. The app is freshly installed on a device with GPS capability.
2. The device has a stable internet connection.
3. The app is configured to request location permissions during onboarding.
4. The user has not yet completed the onboarding process.
5. The device's location services are initially turned off.
6. The user is familiar with how to quickly toggle device location permissions.
TC-265.title: Rapid location permission changes during onboarding

steps.step: Launch the app for the first time.
steps.result: The app should open and begin the onboarding process, starting with the language selection screen.

steps.step: Complete the language selection screen.
steps.result: The app should move to the next step in the onboarding process (mobile number entry or location permission request, depending on the variant).

steps.step: When prompted for location permission, quickly toggle the device location permission on and off multiple times in rapid succession (at least 5 times within 10 seconds).
steps.result: The app should handle the rapid changes without crashing. It should consistently show the appropriate UI for the current permission state (allowed or denied) after each toggle.

steps.step: Observe the app's behavior during and after the rapid toggles.
steps.result: The app should maintain its state and not skip any screens in the onboarding flow. It should react to each permission change appropriately, showing relevant dialogs or prompts as needed.

steps.step: After the rapid toggles, leave the location permission in the 'allowed' state and continue with the onboarding process.
steps.result: The app should proceed with the next step in the onboarding flow (e.g., GPS permission, notification permission, or signup completion, depending on the variant).

steps.step: Complete the remaining steps of the onboarding process.
steps.result: The app should successfully finish the onboarding process, assigning a store (if applicable) and reaching the main screen or appropriate post-onboarding state.

steps.step: Verify the final location permission state in the app settings.
steps.result: The app settings should correctly reflect that location permission is granted.

steps.step: Attempt to use a feature that requires location services (e.g., store locator or current location feature).
steps.result: The app should be able to access and use the device's location without requiring additional permission prompts.

TC-266.description: Test app performance when user rapidly enables and disables GPS while granting location permission. The app should adapt to GPS state changes without UI glitches or flow disruptions.
TC-266.preconditions:
1. The app is freshly installed on a device with GPS capability.
2. The user has completed the language selection and signup process.
3. The app is at the location permission prompt screen.
4. The device's location services are initially enabled.
5. The app has not been granted any permissions yet.
TC-266.title: Quick GPS toggle during location permission flow

steps.step: Tap 'Allow' on the location permission prompt.
steps.result: The app should proceed to the GPS permission prompt.

steps.step: Quickly disable GPS from the device settings.
steps.result: The app should detect the GPS state change and update the UI accordingly, showing the GPS permission prompt or a dialog indicating GPS is required.

steps.step: Quickly re-enable GPS from the device settings.
steps.result: The app should detect the GPS state change and update the UI, returning to the GPS permission prompt if it was previously shown.

steps.step: Repeat steps 2 and 3 rapidly for 5-10 cycles.
steps.result: The app should consistently respond to each GPS state change without crashing, freezing, or showing UI glitches. It should maintain the correct position in the permission flow.

steps.step: Complete the GPS permission grant process.
steps.result: The app should proceed to the next step in the permission flow (likely the notification permission prompt) without any issues.

steps.step: Navigate through the rest of the app's onboarding process.
steps.result: The app should complete the onboarding process successfully, assigning a store and reaching the main screen without any adverse effects from the rapid GPS toggling.

TC-267.description: Evaluate app stability when user quickly cycles through granting and revoking location, GPS, and notification permissions in succession. The app should handle multiple permission state changes without crashing or losing track of the current flow step.
TC-267.preconditions:
1. The app is freshly installed on a device with GPS capability.
2. The user has completed the language selection and signup process.
3. The app is at the beginning of the permission flow (location permission prompt).
4. The device's location services are initially enabled.
5. The app has not been granted any permissions yet.
TC-267.title: Rapid permission changes across location, GPS, and notifications

steps.step: Tap 'Allow' on the location permission prompt.
steps.result: The app should proceed to the GPS permission prompt.

steps.step: Quickly revoke the location permission from device settings.
steps.result: The app should detect the permission change and revert to the location permission prompt or show a context page for denied location.

steps.step: Quickly re-grant the location permission from device settings.
steps.result: The app should detect the permission change and proceed to the GPS permission prompt.

steps.step: Tap 'Allow' on the GPS permission prompt.
steps.result: The app should proceed to the notification permission prompt.

steps.step: Quickly disable GPS from device settings.
steps.result: The app should detect the GPS state change and show appropriate UI (e.g., GPS required dialog).

steps.step: Quickly re-enable GPS from device settings.
steps.result: The app should detect the GPS state change and return to the notification permission prompt.

steps.step: Tap 'Allow' on the notification permission prompt.
steps.result: The app should grant notification permission and proceed to the next step in the flow.

steps.step: Quickly revoke notification permission from device settings.
steps.result: The app should detect the permission change and revert to the notification permission prompt or handle it gracefully.

steps.step: Repeat steps 2-8 rapidly for 3-5 cycles.
steps.result: The app should handle all permission changes without crashing, freezing, or losing track of the current step in the flow. It should consistently update its UI and state according to the latest permission status.

steps.step: Complete the permission granting process for all permissions.
steps.result: The app should successfully finish the permission flow and proceed to the main functionality (e.g., store assignment) without any issues caused by the rapid permission changes.

TC-268.description: Assess app performance when user rapidly switches between 'Allow Access' and 'Select Address Manually' options on context pages. The app should respond correctly to quick changes without UI lag or incorrect state transitions.
TC-268.preconditions:
1. The app is freshly installed on a device with GPS capability.
2. The user has completed the language selection and signup process.
3. The app is configured to show context pages for permission choices (Variant B or C).
4. The device's location services are enabled.
5. The app has not been granted any permissions yet.
TC-268.title: Fast user actions on permission context pages

steps.step: Navigate to the context page showing 'Allow Access' and 'Select Address Manually' options.
steps.result: The context page should be displayed with both options clearly visible.

steps.step: Tap 'Allow Access' option.
steps.result: The app should initiate the location permission flow.

steps.step: Quickly tap the back button or gesture to return to the context page.
steps.result: The app should return to the context page without delay.

steps.step: Tap 'Select Address Manually' option.
steps.result: The app should navigate to the manual address entry screen.

steps.step: Quickly tap the back button or gesture to return to the context page.
steps.result: The app should return to the context page without delay.

steps.step: Repeat steps 2-5 rapidly for 10-15 cycles, alternating between 'Allow Access' and 'Select Address Manually'.
steps.result: The app should respond correctly to each tap without lag, crashes, or UI glitches. It should consistently navigate between the context page, permission flow, and manual address entry as appropriate.

steps.step: On the final cycle, choose 'Allow Access' and complete the location permission grant.
steps.result: The app should successfully grant location permission and proceed to the next step in the flow (e.g., GPS permission or notification permission).

steps.step: Complete the remaining permission flow.
steps.result: The app should finish the entire permission and onboarding process without any issues caused by the rapid context page interactions.

TC-269.description: Verify app stability when user quickly interacts with both app UI and system permission dialogs, rapidly granting and revoking permissions. The app should synchronize its state with system changes without crashes or inconsistencies.
TC-269.preconditions:
1. The app is freshly installed on a device with GPS capability.
2. The user has completed the language selection and signup process.
3. The app is at the beginning of the permission flow (location permission prompt).
4. The device's location services are initially enabled.
5. The app has not been granted any permissions yet.
TC-269.title: Stress test permission flow with system dialog interactions

steps.step: Tap 'Allow' on the app's location permission UI prompt.
steps.result: The system location permission dialog should appear.

steps.step: Quickly tap 'Deny' on the system dialog before it closes automatically.
steps.result: The app should detect the permission denial and update its UI accordingly (e.g., show a context page or reprompt).

steps.step: Immediately re-trigger the location permission request from the app UI.
steps.result: The system location permission dialog should reappear.

steps.step: Quickly tap 'Allow' on the system dialog.
steps.result: The app should detect the permission grant and proceed to the next permission (GPS or notifications).

steps.step: Rapidly open the device settings and revoke the location permission.
steps.result: The app should detect the permission revocation and update its UI, possibly returning to the location permission prompt or context page.

steps.step: Quickly re-grant the location permission from device settings.
steps.result: The app should detect the permission grant and proceed to the next permission step.

steps.step: Repeat steps 1-6 for GPS and notification permissions, rapidly interacting with both app UI and system dialogs.
steps.result: The app should consistently synchronize its state with system permission changes, updating UI and flow state accordingly without crashes or inconsistencies.

steps.step: During the above steps, occasionally background the app and quickly return to it.
steps.result: The app should resume in the correct state of the permission flow, consistent with the last permission action taken.

steps.step: Attempt to interact with app UI elements (e.g., back buttons, context page options) immediately after system dialog interactions.
steps.result: The app should respond correctly to these interactions without lag or erroneous state transitions.

steps.step: After multiple rapid cycles, ensure all permissions are finally granted.
steps.result: The app should successfully complete the permission flow and proceed to the main functionality (e.g., store assignment) without any issues caused by the stress test.

TC-258.description: Test the app's ability to handle rapid changes in permission status (granting and revoking quickly) during the permission flow. Ensure the app doesn't crash, lose state, or skip steps in the flow when permissions are changed rapidly.
TC-258.preconditions:
1. The app is freshly installed on a test device.
2. The device has all necessary permissions (location, GPS, notifications) initially disabled.
3. The app is configured to run through the permission flow (any variant - Current State, A, B, or C).
4. The test device allows for quick toggling of permissions from the system settings.
TC-258.title: Handle rapid permission changes during flow

steps.step: Launch the app and proceed to the first permission request (location).
steps.result: The app should display the location permission request.

steps.step: Quickly grant the location permission, then immediately revoke it from system settings.
steps.result: The app should detect the permission change and either revert to the permission request or move to the next step in the flow without crashing.

steps.step: If the app reverted to the location permission request, grant it again.
steps.result: The app should proceed to the next permission request (GPS or notifications, depending on the variant).

steps.step: For the GPS permission request, quickly toggle the GPS on and off in system settings.
steps.result: The app should handle the rapid GPS state changes without crashing, maintaining its place in the flow.

steps.step: Proceed to the notification permission request.
steps.result: The notification permission request should be displayed.

steps.step: Quickly grant and revoke the notification permission multiple times.
steps.result: The app should handle these rapid changes without losing state or crashing.

steps.step: Complete the permission flow by granting all necessary permissions.
steps.result: The app should successfully complete the permission flow and proceed to the next step (e.g., store assignment or language selection, depending on the variant).

steps.step: Verify the final state of the app after the permission flow.
steps.result: The app should be in the correct state based on the granted permissions, with all features accessible as expected.

TC-259.description: Verify the app's behavior on devices with limited location accuracy capabilities. Ensure the app detects this limitation, treats it appropriately (as location not given), and provides suitable alternatives or guidance to the user.
TC-259.preconditions:
1. The app is installed on a device with limited location accuracy capabilities.
2. The app is configured to run through the permission flow (any variant - Current State, A, B, or C).
3. The device's location services are initially turned off.
TC-259.title: Manage permission flow on devices with limited location accuracy

steps.step: Launch the app and proceed through the initial screens (language selection, signup, etc.) until reaching the location permission request.
steps.result: The app should display the location permission request.

steps.step: Grant the location permission from the system dialog.
steps.result: The app should detect that location permission is granted but accuracy is limited.

steps.step: Observe the app's behavior after granting limited accuracy location permission.
steps.result: The app should treat this as if location is not given, similar to when location is denied.

steps.step: Check if the app provides any indication or message about limited location accuracy.
steps.result: The app should display a message or UI element informing the user about limited location accuracy and its implications.

steps.step: Verify if the app offers alternatives for location selection.
steps.result: The app should provide options like manual address entry or selecting from a list of areas.

steps.step: Attempt to use a feature that requires precise location (e.g., 'Use Current Location' in the address bottom sheet).
steps.result: The app should inform the user that this feature is not available due to limited location accuracy and suggest alternatives.

steps.step: Complete the permission flow by granting any remaining permissions (e.g., notifications).
steps.result: The app should successfully complete the permission flow, adapting its behavior for limited location accuracy.

steps.step: Navigate through the app and verify location-dependent features.
steps.result: Location-dependent features should either be disabled or offer alternative methods of operation suitable for limited accuracy.

TC-261.description: Validate the app's behavior during the permission flow when network connectivity is intermittent. Ensure that permission states are correctly saved and restored, and that the flow can continue or resume appropriately when connectivity is re-established.
TC-261.preconditions:
1. The app is installed on a device with controllable network connectivity (e.g., ability to toggle airplane mode or use a network throttling tool).
2. The app is configured to run through the permission flow (any variant - Current State, A, B, or C).
3. All permissions (location, GPS, notifications) are initially disabled on the device.
TC-261.title: Verify permission flow with intermittent network connectivity

steps.step: Launch the app with a stable network connection and proceed to the first permission request (location).
steps.result: The app should display the location permission request.

steps.step: Just before granting the location permission, disable the network connection.
steps.result: The system permission dialog should still appear and allow the user to grant the permission.

steps.step: Grant the location permission while offline.
steps.result: The app should recognize that the permission was granted, even without an active network connection.

steps.step: Keep the network disabled and proceed to the next step in the permission flow (e.g., GPS permission or notification permission).
steps.result: The app should be able to proceed to the next permission request without requiring network connectivity.

steps.step: Enable the network connection and immediately disable it again.
steps.result: The app should handle the brief connectivity without crashing or losing its state in the permission flow.

steps.step: Grant or deny the current permission request while offline.
steps.result: The app should properly record the permission state and proceed accordingly.

steps.step: Force close the app while still in the permission flow and with no network connection.
steps.result: The app should close without errors.

steps.step: Re-enable network connectivity and relaunch the app.
steps.result: The app should resume the permission flow from where it left off, recognizing previously granted permissions.

steps.step: Complete the remaining steps of the permission flow with intermittent network connectivity (toggling on and off).
steps.result: The app should successfully complete the permission flow, handling network changes gracefully and maintaining correct permission states.

steps.step: After completing the flow, verify the final permission states and app functionality.
steps.result: All granted permissions should be correctly recognized by the app, and features should be accessible according to the permission states, regardless of network interruptions during the flow.

TC-262.description: Check the app's ability to handle permission requests and maintain flow state when the device is in a low storage condition. Verify that critical permission data is saved and the flow can be resumed even if the app is terminated due to low storage.
TC-262.preconditions:
1. The app is installed on a device with very limited available storage (nearly full).
2. The app is configured to run through the permission flow (any variant - Current State, A, B, or C).
3. All permissions (location, GPS, notifications) are initially disabled on the device.
4. The device is set up to allow forcing app termination due to low storage (this may require developer options or special test device settings).
TC-262.title: Test permission handling during low device storage scenarios

steps.step: Launch the app and proceed to the first permission request (location).
steps.result: The app should display the location permission request without any storage-related issues.

steps.step: Grant the location permission.
steps.result: The app should successfully record the granted permission state.

steps.step: Before proceeding to the next permission, fill the device storage to its limit (e.g., by copying large files or using a storage filling tool).
steps.result: The device should indicate very low storage availability.

steps.step: Attempt to proceed to the next permission request in the flow.
steps.result: The app should try to display the next permission request, but it may be terminated by the system due to low storage.

steps.step: If the app is terminated, clear some device storage and relaunch the app.
steps.result: The app should resume the permission flow from where it left off, recognizing the previously granted location permission.

steps.step: Proceed with granting or denying the next permission (GPS or notifications) while the device is still in a low storage state.
steps.result: The app should handle the permission response and save the state successfully, despite low storage conditions.

steps.step: Force the app to be terminated due to low storage conditions (this may require triggering a low memory condition or using developer options).
steps.result: The app should close, but critical permission data should be saved.

steps.step: Clear some more device storage and relaunch the app.
steps.result: The app should resume the permission flow from the last saved state, with all previously granted or denied permissions correctly recognized.

steps.step: Complete the remaining steps of the permission flow, alternating between very low and slightly better storage conditions.
steps.result: The app should successfully navigate through all permission requests, saving states correctly between potential terminations due to low storage.

steps.step: After completing the flow, verify the final permission states and app functionality.
steps.result: All permission states should be correctly saved and recognized by the app, with appropriate features accessible or limited based on the granted permissions, despite the low storage interruptions during the flow.

TC-263.description: For devices supporting multiple users, test the permission flow behavior when switching between different device users. Ensure that each user's permission state is maintained separately and the flow resumes correctly for each user.
TC-263.preconditions:
1. The app is installed on a device that supports multiple user profiles.
2. At least two user profiles are set up on the device.
3. The app is in its initial state (fresh install) for both user profiles.
4. The device has working GPS and location services.
TC-263.title: Validate permission flow with multiple users on device

steps.step: Launch the app with User A and proceed through the initial flow until reaching the location permission prompt.
steps.result: The app should launch and progress through the language selection, mobile number entry, and OTP verification screens before showing the location permission prompt.

steps.step: Allow location permission for User A.
steps.result: The app should proceed to the next step in the permission flow (GPS permission or notification permission, depending on the variant).

steps.step: Switch to User B on the device.
steps.result: The device should switch to User B's profile.

steps.step: Launch the app with User B.
steps.result: The app should start from the beginning of the flow for a fresh install (language selection screen).

steps.step: Progress through the initial flow with User B until reaching the location permission prompt.
steps.result: The app should show the location permission prompt for User B, regardless of User A's choices.

steps.step: Deny location permission for User B.
steps.result: The app should show the appropriate flow for denied location permission (e.g., context page with options to allow access or select address manually).

steps.step: Switch back to User A.
steps.result: The device should switch to User A's profile.

steps.step: Launch the app with User A.
steps.result: The app should resume from where User A left off in the permission flow, maintaining User A's previous permission grants.

steps.step: Complete the permission flow for User A.
steps.result: User A should be able to complete the flow and reach the main app screen or store assignment.

steps.step: Switch to User B and launch the app.
steps.result: The app should resume the flow for User B from the point after location permission was denied, maintaining the separate state for User B.

steps.step: Complete the permission flow for User B, choosing to allow permissions this time.
steps.result: User B should be able to complete the flow independently of User A's choices and reach the main app screen or store assignment.

TC-264.description: Verify the app's response when system settings affecting permissions (like turning off device location services) are changed during the permission flow. Ensure the app detects these changes and adjusts its flow accordingly.
TC-264.preconditions:
1. The app is installed on a device with functioning GPS and location services.
2. The app is in its initial state (fresh install).
3. The device allows quick access to system settings for location services.
TC-264.title: Handle permission flow during system setting changes

steps.step: Launch the app and proceed through the initial flow until reaching the location permission prompt.
steps.result: The app should progress through language selection, mobile number entry, and OTP verification before showing the location permission prompt.

steps.step: When the location permission prompt appears, quickly switch to device settings and turn off location services without responding to the app's prompt.
steps.result: The system should disable location services. The app's UI should update to reflect that location services are now disabled (e.g., showing both upper and lower dialogs in the location sheet).

steps.step: Return to the app without granting or denying permission.
steps.result: The app should detect that location services are off and adjust its flow, possibly showing a prompt to enable location services or offering alternative options.

steps.step: Enable location services from the device settings without interacting with the app.
steps.result: The app should detect that location services are now enabled and update its UI accordingly, possibly re-prompting for location permission.

steps.step: Grant location permission to the app.
steps.result: The app should proceed to the next step in the permission flow (GPS permission or notification permission, depending on the variant).

steps.step: When prompted for GPS permission, quickly turn off GPS from device settings before responding.
steps.result: The app should detect the change and update its UI, possibly showing a prompt to enable GPS or offering alternative options.

steps.step: Turn GPS back on from device settings.
steps.result: The app should detect that GPS is now available and update its UI accordingly, possibly re-prompting for GPS permission.

steps.step: Grant GPS permission to the app.
steps.result: The app should proceed to the next step in the flow (e.g., notification permission).

steps.step: When prompted for notification permission, quickly navigate to app settings and revoke location permission before responding.
steps.result: The app should detect the revoked location permission and adjust its flow, possibly returning to the location permission prompt or showing appropriate error messaging.

steps.step: Grant location permission again from app settings.
steps.result: The app should detect the re-granted permission and resume the flow appropriately, possibly re-prompting for GPS and then notification permissions.

steps.step: Complete the permission flow by granting all remaining permissions.
steps.result: The app should successfully complete the permission flow and proceed to the main app screen or store assignment.

steps.step: Force close the app and relaunch it.
steps.result: The app should recognize the previously granted permissions and not re-prompt for them, proceeding directly to the main app screen or store assignment.

TC-251.description: Test the app's ability to correctly resume the permission flow after an app update is installed. Ensure that previously granted permissions are retained and the flow continues from the appropriate step based on the user's progress before the update.
TC-251.preconditions:
1. The app is installed on a test device.
2. The user has partially completed the permission flow (e.g., granted location permission but not GPS or notifications).
3. An app update is available and ready to be installed.
TC-251.title: Verify permission flow recovery after app update

steps.step: Launch the app and navigate through the permission flow until at least one permission (e.g., location) is granted.
steps.result: The app should successfully grant the chosen permission and move to the next step in the flow.

steps.step: Without completing the entire permission flow, close the app and install the available update.
steps.result: The app update should install successfully.

steps.step: Relaunch the updated app.
steps.result: The app should launch without errors and recognize the user's previous session.

steps.step: Observe the app's behavior regarding the permission flow.
steps.result: The app should resume the permission flow from the appropriate step based on previously granted permissions. It should not re-prompt for already granted permissions.

steps.step: Continue through the remaining permission prompts (e.g., GPS, notifications).
steps.result: The app should present the remaining permission requests in the correct order as per the current variant (A, B, or C).

steps.step: Complete the permission flow.
steps.result: The app should successfully finish the permission flow and proceed to the appropriate next screen (e.g., store assignment or language selection, depending on the variant).

steps.step: Verify the permissions granted in the device settings.
steps.result: The device settings should reflect all the permissions granted during the flow, including those granted before and after the update.

TC-252.description: Confirm that when the app is uninstalled and then reinstalled, it correctly identifies the user's previous permission choices (if stored server-side) and resumes the flow appropriately. Verify that users aren't unnecessarily re-prompted for already granted permissions.
TC-252.preconditions:
1. The app is installed on a test device and the user has completed the initial setup, granting some or all permissions.
2. The app's server-side storage for user permission states is accessible and functioning (if applicable).
3. The device has a stable internet connection.
TC-252.title: Validate permission state preservation after app reinstall

steps.step: Launch the app and note the current permission states (location, GPS, notifications).
steps.result: The app should display the correct permission states as previously set by the user.

steps.step: Uninstall the app from the device.
steps.result: The app should be successfully removed from the device.

steps.step: Reinstall the app on the same device.
steps.result: The app should install successfully.

steps.step: Launch the newly installed app.
steps.result: The app should start and either recognize the returning user or prompt for login.

steps.step: If prompted, log in with the previous user credentials.
steps.result: The app should successfully authenticate the user.

steps.step: Observe the app's behavior regarding permissions.
steps.result: The app should identify previously granted permissions (if stored server-side) and not re-prompt for these. For permissions not yet granted, it should resume the flow at the appropriate step.

steps.step: Navigate through the permission flow if any permissions are still required.
steps.result: The app should only request permissions that weren't previously granted, following the correct sequence for the current variant (A, B, or C).

steps.step: Complete the permission flow if necessary.
steps.result: The app should successfully finish any remaining permission requests and proceed to the main functionality.

steps.step: Verify the permissions in the device settings.
steps.result: The device settings should reflect all the permissions granted, matching the state before the app was uninstalled (for previously granted permissions) and any new permissions granted during the reinstall process.

TC-253.description: Check the app's behavior in maintaining and recovering the permission flow state when a user logs out and then logs back in. Ensure that the app resumes at the correct step in the permission flow based on the user's previous actions.
TC-253.preconditions:
1. The app is installed on a test device.
2. A user account exists with a partially completed permission flow (e.g., location granted, but not GPS or notifications).
3. The app's server-side storage for user permission states is functioning (if applicable).
TC-253.title: Test permission flow recovery after user logout and login

steps.step: Launch the app and log in with the test user account.
steps.result: The app should successfully authenticate the user and display the correct permission state.

steps.step: Note the current progress in the permission flow.
steps.result: The app should show the correct permissions already granted and the next permission to be requested.

steps.step: Log out of the app.
steps.result: The app should successfully log out the user and return to the login or initial screen.

steps.step: Log back in with the same user account.
steps.result: The app should authenticate the user successfully.

steps.step: Observe the app's behavior regarding the permission flow.
steps.result: The app should resume the permission flow from where the user left off, not re-prompting for already granted permissions.

steps.step: Continue through any remaining permission prompts.
steps.result: The app should present the remaining permission requests in the correct order as per the current variant (A, B, or C).

steps.step: Complete the permission flow if any permissions are still required.
steps.result: The app should successfully finish the permission flow and proceed to the main functionality or appropriate next screen.

steps.step: Verify the permissions in the device settings.
steps.result: The device settings should reflect all the permissions granted, including those from before logout and after login.

steps.step: Repeat the logout and login process once more.
